name: Aladdin
description:
I am a charmer and will want fuss from most people even strangers.

I need a prospective adopter to visit me a couple of time or more to get to know me before I can go on a home trial

I cannot be placed in a property with no direct access to a private garden.

I need a home that is at ease with my kind of dog, who have a good understanding and experience of dog behaviour.

name: Willow
description:
I'm looking for my forever home. Could you be my perfect match?

In our form you can tell us all about your home, your lifestyle and the kind of dogs you’re interested in. You won’t be applying for a specific dog, but you can add favourites to give us an idea of the dogs you like. We’ll use this information to find a great match for you.

We’ll also ask you to select a rehoming centre. The team at this centre will look after your application and assess you against all suitable dogs in their care. This doesn’t have to be your nearest centre, but you will need to travel there within a few days once we’ve found...

We’ll also ask you to select a rehoming centre. The team at this centre will look after your application and assess you against all suitable dogs in their care. This doesn’t have to be your nearest centre, but you will need to travel there within a few days once we’ve found you a match.

We’ll review your application against the dogs in our care at your chosen centre. If we think we have the right dog for you, we’ll be in touch as soon as possible. If we don’t hear back from you, we will close your application for now, and you can reapply when you’re ready.

If we don’t have a dog for you right now, we’ll keep your application open for three months and keep you in mind for any new dogs that arrive. If within this time you see another dog you are interested in, you won't need to apply again but you can amend your application by...

If we don’t have a dog for you right now, we’ll keep your application open for three months and keep you in mind for any new dogs that arrive. If within this time you see another dog you are interested in, you won't need to apply again but you can amend your application by calling us on0303 003 0000or visiting your local rehoming centre.

If we’ve found a dog who seems right for you, we’ll invite you to come and meet them at the rehoming centre.

Some of the dogs in our care will need to meet potential owners several times to get to know one another. This lets us see you’re compatible and gives you...

Some of the dogs in our care will need to meet potential owners several times to get to know one another. This lets us see you’re compatible and gives you the chance to build a bond before making a commitment to give them a new home. By taking some time at the beginning to make sure everyone’s comfortable, an adoption is more likely to be successful.

We may also arrange a home visit with you, to see how your house will be set up and check that the garden (if you have one) is secure. This may not be necessary, though, especially if you’ve adopted from us before. Where you already have dogs, we may accept a reference from your vet as an alternative.

If we haven’t found the right dog for you within three months, we’ll let you know your application is closed. We’ll invite you to apply again so we have up-to-date information about you, and we’ll keep looking.

When we’ve matched you with a dog, we’ll help you welcome them to your home. After adoption we’ll keep in touch to see how you and the dog are doing. If you need any advice or support, we’re just a phone call away.

With our free behaviour support, our Canine Care Card and more, we’re here to support you and your dog for as long as you need us.

Discover more about this dogs needs and how you might be able to support them should your rehoming application be successful.

Registered Charity Numbers:1167663 and SC053144 & Company No. 09365971. Donations are tax exempt and Gift Aid can be claimed.

Get top dog training tips and behaviour advice straight to your inbox, as well as fun activities and plenty of pupdates.

We’ll also ask you to select a rehoming centre. The team at this centre will look after your application and assess you against all suitable dogs in their care. This doesn’t have to be your nearest centre, but you will need to travel there within a few days once we’ve found...

We’ll also ask you to select a rehoming centre. The team at this centre will look after your application and assess you against all suitable dogs in their care. This doesn’t have to be your nearest centre, but you will need to travel there within a few days once we’ve found you a match.

We’ll review your application against the dogs in our care at your chosen centre. If we think we have the right dog for you, we’ll be in touch as soon as possible. If we don’t hear back from you, we will close your application for now, and you can reapply when you’re ready.

If we don’t have a dog for you right now, we’ll keep your application open for three months and keep you in mind for any new dogs that arrive. If within this time you see another dog you are interested in, you won't need to apply again but you can amend your application by...

If we don’t have a dog for you right now, we’ll keep your application open for three months and keep you in mind for any new dogs that arrive. If within this time you see another dog you are interested in, you won't need to apply again but you can amend your application by calling us on0303 003 0000or visiting your local rehoming centre.

If we’ve found a dog who seems right for you, we’ll invite you to come and meet them at the rehoming centre.

Some of the dogs in our care will need to meet potential owners several times to get to know one another. This lets us see you’re compatible and gives you...

Some of the dogs in our care will need to meet potential owners several times to get to know one another. This lets us see you’re compatible and gives you the chance to build a bond before making a commitment to give them a new home. By taking some time at the beginning to make sure everyone’s comfortable, an adoption is more likely to be successful.

We may also arrange a home visit with you, to see how your house will be set up and check that the garden (if you have one) is secure. This may not be necessary, though, especially if you’ve adopted from us before. Where you already have dogs, we may accept a reference from your vet as an alternative.

If we haven’t found the right dog for you within three months, we’ll let you know your application is closed. We’ll invite you to apply again so we have up-to-date information about you, and we’ll keep looking.

When we’ve matched you with a dog, we’ll help you welcome them to your home. After adoption we’ll keep in touch to see how you and the dog are doing. If you need any advice or support, we’re just a phone call away.

With our free behaviour support, our Canine Care Card and more, we’re here to support you and your dog for as long as you need us.

Discover more about this dogs needs and how you might be able to support them should your rehoming application be successful.

Registered Charity Numbers:1167663 and SC053144 & Company No. 09365971. Donations are tax exempt and Gift Aid can be claimed.

name: Kenny (and Barnaby)
description:
Meet Barnaby and Kenny!

This adorable duo are looking for their new home together. Barnaby loves to play and enjoys a good game of tug whilst Kenny prefers human affection. Both dogs love a calm home environment and have a lot of love to offer.

Since being together from puppies, these boys have a strong bond and can often be found resting near each other. They would benefit from being the only dogs in the home but could have social doggy friends to spend time or go on walks with. They would like a home without cats due to their desires to chase.

They are looking for a quieter home with plenty of space for them to play, explore and rest. Barnaby can be sensitive to loud noises and unpredictable sounds. He will need a calm steady home environment. They would like a home with older teenage children or an adult only home for the time being.

Due to his size Barnaby will need to be walked by someone physically capable of managing him. Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Barnaby may need.

name: Kiki (& Gypsy)
description:
Introducing our loveable girls, Kiki and Gypsy!

This adorable pair of sisters share the sweetest bond and are looking for a forever home where they can stay side by side. Kiki and Gypsy adore curling up together on the sofa, soaking up affection, and demanding belly rubs from their favourite humans!

They’re wonderfully loyal to their people and to each other, always checking in and staying close. They also love a bit of playtime, happily entertaining themselves with their favourite toys before settling back in for more cuddles.

Out on walks, Kiki turns into a little adventurer, eager to explore new paths and sniff out every exciting corner, while Gypsy loves the thrill of stretching her legs and having a joyful run.

They’re incredibly affectionate with their humans and with the people who come into their home, always greeting them with a wiggly bottom!

They can be left for short periods, and with steady, positive routines, this can be built up further over time. They will need two adults in the home and are unable to live with cats.

This lovely pair are ready to steal your hearts — could you offer them their forever home?

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Kiki and Gypsy may need.

name: Finny
description:
Finny is a friendly little dog who enjoys the company of people and is always happy to meet someone new. He has a cheerful nature and likes being involved in whatever is going on around him.

He loves his walks and particularly enjoys stopping to investigate all the interesting scents he comes across. Exploring the world through his nose is one of Finny's favourite pastimes.

Finny is looking for a loving home where he can enjoy plenty of attention, companionship and opportunities to get out and about. He's a sweet boy with lots of love to give and is ready to find a family of his own.

As a flat-faced (brachycephalic) dog, Finny may be more prone to certain health issues that can affect his wellbeing. We encourage anyone considering offering him a home to take some time to research the needs of brachycephalic breeds. Our experienced team will be happy to provide guidance on exercise, weight management, and any additional care or support Finny may require.

If you think you have the perfect home for Finny, please complete an application for him today.

name: Yuki
description:
This handsome lad can't wait to meet his new family! Yuki is a calm, loyal and independent boy who spends a lot of his time chilling and observing the world. Yuki has a lot of love to give and greets his favourite people with a wagging tail. If he's extra excited to see you he will bring you one of his soft toys as well!

Yuki is looking for a new family that have some understanding of Akita traits or who are willing to learn and understand the subtleties in his body language. Yuki will flourish in the right environment and will make a loyal, engaging companion.

Yuki loves getting out on his walks and can enjoy seeing other dogs, but his breed typical body language can come across quite aloof. Therefore, he is looking for a family that can read dog behaviour to navigate these interactions calmly and confidently and are happy to keep up with his ongoing training out on his walks and in the home.

Yuki could live with older kids that are used to larger bouncy breeds, he would be best suited to being the only dog in the home. He couldn't live with cats but could live with smalls if kept separate.

If you feel Yuki would be a great addition to your family, please fill in an application.

name: Padme
description:
This bouncy, energetic girl is always ready for fun and never says no to a game with her toys. She loves nothing more than charging around the run, proudly parading her prized possessions, especially her beloved soft, fluffy toys. Her playful spirit and joyful energy make her an absolute delight to be around.

Padme is looking for an owner who can help her transition into home life with a little patience and reassurance. With the right support, she’s sure to blossom into a wonderful and devoted housemate.

Out on walks, Padme is a dream on the lead. She strolls beautifully by your side, often staying close as if to share every special moment with you. She’s also a social butterfly, always happy to greet new people, leaning in gently to soak up as much affection as she can.

And her coat - what a bonus! Unlike the typical greyhound coat, Padme’s is irresistibly soft, almost like velvet, making her even more special.

With some continued training, lots of love, and a caring home, Padme will make an incredible companion, one who will bring laughter, warmth, and a smile to your face every single day with her bright and bubbly personality.

name: Gizmo
description:
This sensitive chap is Gizmo.

We’re looking for understanding and supportive owners that ideally have experience with rescue dogs as Gizmo is going to need a little help to settle back into pet life.

The biggest thing that Gizmo struggles with is being left alone, so there will need to be multiple people in his new home to keep him company whilst he settles and help him to learn how to be home alone – or better yet never have to experience being on his own! He needs confidence building when out on walks, however inside, he is a bouncy, playful and cuddly boy who loves to be in your arms having fuss.

We’ve found that Gizmo loves nothing more than routine as this helps him feel more confident, se we take him for walks, feed him, play with him, and spend time with him at the same time each day. The team here at the centre can advise on the best ways to set Gizmo and his new owners up for success.

When out and about Gizmo prefers to avoid other dogs, however with time, patience and suitable calm dogs he can build up to making friends as he has done here at the centre. In his new home though, he will need to be the only pet as he will likely chase cats and isn’t suitable to live with other dogs as he will need to be the main focus for his owners. He would be best suited to live with adults and teenagers who can appreciate that underneath this worried exterior there is a super loving and cuddly dog just waiting to come out.

If Gizmo sounds like the dog you’ve been searching for, please get in touch today!

name: Elsa
description:
Elsa is a beautiful white German Shepherd with a big personality and lots of energy. She loves being active, playing with toys, and getting out and about, so she’d suit an active home with space to run, ideally with a secure area for off-lead time. After which she’s more than happy to relax with a chew and unwind.

She can be unsure in new situations, so she needs a calm, patient owner who can help build her confidence. Elsa isn’t instantly affectionate but forms strong, rewarding bonds once she gets to know you, and she may need a few visits before going home.

Elsa can live with older secondary school aged children who are confident around energetic  dogs. She isn't able to share her new home with other pets.

Elsa is searching for her Anna, a calm, understanding companion who knows that good things take time. As Olaf would say, some things are worth waiting for!

If you think you could be the perfect match for Elsa, please fill in an application form.

name: Tiggy Wiggy
description:
Tiggy Wiggy is a young, fun-loving girl with a zest for life and a heart full of affection. Bursting with energy and enthusiasm, she loves getting out and about for adventures before curling up for a well earned snooze!

Tiggy loves to have a paddle in the stream and have off lead time in a secure run. She enjoys playing with her toys and getting a tasty chewy to settle down with.

Tiggy could live with teenage children who have been used to living with bouncy dogs before. She could potentially live with another large dog in her new home, that has lived with dogs before.

Tiggy wouldn't be able to live with cats or small animals due to her desire to chase!

If you have room in your life for a girl who Is full of character, then Tiggy Wiggy could be the perfect addition to your home.

name: Kayde
description:
Meet Kayde, a large dog with an equally large zest for life!

She is a gentle giant who's full of fun and as she gets to know you will shower you with love.

Her favourite thing to do is walk in nature and is very responsive with training... especially when you provide food.

Kayde does like interacting with other dogs but continued work and socialisation would benefit her going forwards. Cats are just way too much fun and she is likely to chase them so she is looking for a home away from cats.

Her new family should be aware that Kayde can sometimes be enthusiastic in meeting people or dogs, and she can jump up. She just needs more training but is incredibly responsive and will get the hang of things in no time at all.

She hasn't lived with children before, but could live with children that are used to large and sometimes enthusiastic dogs. An ideal home would be one that understands her intentions when saying hello to people and dogs, and can respect her size and strength even when on lead.

Kayde enjoys travelling, so once she is all settled into her new home she would be open to car journeys somewhere nice.

If you have the space in your heart and home to take on a sweet girl like Kayde, please apply!

name: Mabel
description:
Meet the lovely Mabel!

Mabel is a loving girl, once she feels comfortable and knows you, her affectionate and sweet nature shines through. Mabel is looking for a calm, quiet home where she can grow in confidence and become part of the family with lots of fuss on the sofa.

Mabel loves to get out on her quiet walks and would benefit from her new family helping her engage in her environment and enjoy all the sniffs.

Mabel could live older children who are comfortable at reading her body language and giving her space when she needs it. Mabel would benefit from being the only dog in the home to have all the fuss and attention. She wouldn’t be able to live with cats as they’d be too fun for her to chase.

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support that may be needed.

If you feel like you could give the lovely Mabel a home, please fill in an application today!

name: Mojo
description:
Miss Mojo is a vibrant, joyful girl with an infectious zest for life that shines through in everything she does. She thrives in an active home where her energy and enthusiasm can truly blossom, so if you love walks, outdoor adventures, and fun games, she may be your perfect match. Mojo is a clever, eager learner who is always ready to try something new, especially when tasty treats are involved.

While in our care, she has enjoyed making dog friends and walking alongside them, and she could happily share her home with another dog who matches her playful temperament. She could also live with a family, either with or without older children, however she will not be able to live with cats. Mojo may need a little support when learning to be left alone in a new home, but with patience and guidance, she will flourish into a loyal and loving companion. A home with a secure garden and high fencing would support Mojo with her off lead time as she will need to be exercised in public on lead.

Full of personality, she brings laughter, affection, and a touch of cheeky charm wherever she goes. She will suit adopters who are ready to invest time in her continued training, for example her lead walking - she can be quite strong sometimes so this is something an adopter needs to consider. With the right home, Miss Mojo will be a devoted best friend and an exciting partner for all of life’s adventures.

name: Alba
description:
This lovely lady is looking for a very specific home, so please ensure you read her profile before submitting an application.

Although Alba is young, she can be underconfident and find new environments and novel things a little worrying.

She is looking for a quiet home that can give her consistency, and with new owners who are happy to continue her training around building confidence, providing access to quiet walking areas, where she can continue to learn about the world. Any children in the home would need to be 13+ and be happy to be involved in her training.

Alba is incredibly tactile and enjoys the company of her friends. Alba enjoys exploring the great outdoors so would make a great companion for those looking to keep their steps up.  She could live with a calm canine roll model who can help her learn the ropes.

Alba is learning about the joys of toy play , so this can be a great way to build a bond with her. She is looking for a secure garden to explore and play, as this is her favourite thing to do! Once she's tired himself out, he enjoys nothing more than a cuddle on your lap as she is a very tactile lady.

New owners will need to be around for most of the day to help her settle in and learn how to spend time alone at her own pace. Alba is unable to live with cats due to her desire to chase them.

She will need help with house training, and support exploring things within a home.

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Alba may need”.

If Alba sounds like the companion for you, please apply today.

name: Frank
description:
This lovely boy is a retired greyhound, a gentle soul who is looking forward to discovering all the joys life has to bring.

He can be a little unsure at first, so would suit a patient family with children who understand the importance of giving him time and space to settle at his own pace.

As he builds confidence, his true personality shines through, He’s an affectionate boy who adores fuss and will happily soak up all the love you can give.

He could potentially share his home with another calm dog of a similar size, but he isn’t able to live with cats or small pets.

A home with a secure garden would be perfect for him to enjoy the occasional run around, and he loves a spot of solo play, followed by a well-earned snooze in a shady spot.

This sweet boy has so much love to give and is ready to become a cherished companion in his forever home.

name: Smudge
description:
Smudge is a sensitive little boy who is looking for a calm and understanding home where he can truly come into his own. Having lived a very sheltered life, Smudge is still learning about the world around him and can find new experiences a little overwhelming. Because of this, he will need patient, supportive and dedicated owners who are committed to building his confidence slowly and at his pace.

Smudge may be shy to begin with, but beneath his cautious exterior is a charming and playful character just waiting to shine. He thrives on positive interactions and, being very food-motivated, responds brilliantly to reward-based encouragement. Enrichment activities and puzzle feeders are some of his favourite things and are a fantastic way to keep his mind engaged whilst helping him grow in confidence.

Once he feels comfortable and trusts you, Smudge shows his fun-loving side—he adores his toys and enjoys games like tug and chase, making him a rewarding companion as his personality blossoms.

Smudge is looking for a quiet, settled home environment where he can adjust gradually:

•	A low-visitor household to help him feel safe while he settles

•	A secure private garden for exercise, as he is not ready for walks during his initial settling period

•	A designated safe space indoors where he can retreat and decompress

•	Patient owners who can support his confidence-building journey

Smudge is currently learning house training and is making good progress, but he will likely need continued support and understanding in his new home.

Due to his sensitivity to sudden noises and unpredictable movement, Smudge is not suitable to live with children at this time. A calm adult-only home would suit him best, where routines are gentle and predictable.

Smudge will thrive with kind, committed adopters who are happy to take things slowly and celebrate the small wins along the way. In return, they’ll gain a loyal and affectionate companion who, once settled, will bring plenty of joy, playfulness, and love to their home.

If you’re looking for a special dog to nurture and help grow in confidence, Smudge could be the perfect addition to your life

name: Kaiya
description:
This beautiful lady is on the lookout for a new home. She is a cool girl with bags of personality and stamina!

Kaiya is an active girl, loves adventures, sniffing and playing with her favourite toys.

She will benefit from a home with owners who have some experience with large breed dogs or have researched and have time to spend with her. Kaiya will also benefit from mental stimulation such as snuffle mats to keep her occupied.

Kaiya could live with a dog in her new home, we would look for a confident dog used to living with dogs and one of similar nature to her. This will all be dependant on meets here at the centre first.

Sadly due a high prey drive she is unable to live with cats and small pets.

We feel Kaiya could live with children in her new home, children used to large dogs and who are happy to give her space when she is settled or resting.

Ideally she needs to have someone around with her whilst she is settling into her new home.

If you can offer this gentle lady a home, please ask about her today!

name: Timmy
description:
Timmy is a handsome, gentle giant weighing in at almost 35kg, but don't let his size fool you, he truly has a heart of gold.

This steady, easy going boy is a pleasure to walk and enjoys strolling nicely by your side on the lead. Timmy is friendly and welcoming when meeting new people, always happy to say hello. If you're lucky, you may even be treated to the classic Greyhound lean, a sure sign that he's taken a shine to you and is enjoying your company!

Timmy has also been spending time in the office with the team, where he has shown just how well-mannered he can be. He's learned to settle beautifully on his bed and is content to relax while the world goes on around him.

When it comes to his forever home, Timmy isn't asking for much. He's looking for a family who can offer companionship, gentle walks, plenty of affection, and, of course, a very large comfy bed to stretch out on after a busy day of being adorable.

If you're searching for a calm, affectionate companion who will happily be by your side and reward you with endless love, Timmy could be the perfect addition to your family.

name: Rocco
description:
Rocco is a lively and spirited little chap searching for a home that can keep up with his zest for life. He has completely stolen the hearts of everyone he has met at Team Thirsk and is now ready to head off on his next exciting adventure. Rocco is an active, fun, and engaging companion who thrives on interaction and loves to be involved in whatever his people are doing.

He adores human company and would benefit from a home that has the time and energy to keep him happy and fulfilled. Long walks, a good run around, and plenty of playtime are among his favourite things. A secure garden is essential for this energetic boy, giving him a safe space to explore.

Rocco would like to be the only dog in the home and cannot live with cats, but he may be able to make dog friends with slow, careful introductions. He can be left for short periods and travels well, making him a great companion for outings. This sweet boy enjoys a belly rub, exploring new places, and could live with or without children.

name: Woody
description:
Woody is a gentle and loving companion who is ready to find his forever sofa.

Woody’s fosterers describe him as a wonderfully calm and affectionate boy whose absolute favourite thing in the world is people and company. Whether he is lying next to them on the sofa or relaxing on a cozy spot of his own, Woody is happiest when he is close to his human friends.  Food and walkies come close though!  He is house-trained, and sleeps soundly through the night in the lounge.

In his foster home, Woody enjoys spending time out in the garden playing with his toys. He has a keen eye for garden wildlife too!

Woody is a relaxed passenger in the car who travels calmly and quietly, making him the perfect partner for both daily routines and weekend adventures!

Woody could potentially share his home with another calm dog of a similar size, but he isn’t able to live with cats or small pets.

This sweet boy has so much love to give and is ready to become a cherished companion in his forever home.

name: Ronald
description:
Ronald is a handsome Labradoodle cross Rottweiler, just under 3 years old, with a lively personality and plenty of love to give. He is a busy, young adolescent dog who enjoys staying active and engaged, making him a great companion for an experienced and committed owner.

Ronald is a friendly and social boy who enjoys spending time with people and being part of the action. He thrives in an environment where he can take part in daily activities and build a strong bond with his family.

As an energetic dog, Ronald will need a good balance of both physical and mental exercise to help him stay happy and fulfilled. He particularly enjoys enrichment activities and would benefit from ongoing training and games that challenge his mind.

Due to his high prey drive, especially when it comes to squirrels and cats, he will require safe off-lead opportunities in secure fields where he can run freely without risk.

Because of this prey drive, Ronald would be best suited to a home without cats or small pets. His new family will also need to be mindful of this when out on walks and ensure he is managed appropriately.

Ronald can live with secondary school-aged children who are confident and respectful of his space and energy levels. He would suit a home that can provide structure, guidance and plenty of opportunities for exercise and enrichment.

Ronald has so much potential and will make a fantastic companion in the right home; one that can match his energy, continue his training and give him the time, patience and love he deserves.

If you think Ronald could be the perfect addition to your home, we’d love to hear from you.

name: Bailey
description:
Meet brilliant Bailey, a handsome older boy who is ready to find a calm and loving home to call his own.

Bailey is a friendly and affectionate boy who enjoys spending time with his favourite people. He has settled beautifully into his foster home, where he is the only dog, and is showing just how much he enjoys a quieter routine with space to relax. Once Bailey has made himself at home, he enjoys being close to his people, settling nearby and making the most of life’s simple pleasures.

Bailey may be older, but he still very much enjoys getting out and about. He loves his walks, exploring new places and especially enjoys swimming! He can be a clever, curious little chap and would enjoy owners who can offer him enrichment, gentle training and plenty of positive encouragement.

Bailey is generally neutral around dogs when out and about, but he has previously found living with other dogs difficult. For this reason, he is looking to be the only pet in his new home, where he can enjoy having his own space and one-to-one attention.

Bailey could live with secondary school aged children who understand that he may appreciate a quieter space when things get busy. He would prefer a home that is not too hectic, with owners who can offer him a predictable routine and help him settle at his own pace.

This lovely lad has so much to offer and is thriving now he has found a calmer environment. If Bailey sounds like the companion for you, please apply today.

name: Amethyst And Friends
description:
We will be expecting that potential adopters will be able to meet as soon as possible. We will arrange this with you if you are contacted. The puppies will be ready to go home on Thursday 23rd July and need to go within 2 days of this date.

These seven beautiful puppies came into our care at just two weeks old and have been lovingly reared by their mum and the team, giving them a wonderful start in life with lots of human interaction and care. Each puppy already shows their own little personality, but they all share a playful, affectionate, and curious nature.

Being Akita cross puppies, they are expected to grow into large (25kg+), strong, and intelligent dogs. Their future families should be prepared for a dog that will need both physical exercise and mental stimulation to thrive and would benefit from researching the breed.

We are looking for committed, loving homes that understand the needs of large-breed puppies. These pups will grow quickly and require consistent training, structure, and socialisation.

•	Families willing to continue training and socialisation

•	Homes with large dog experience (preferred, but not essential if willing to learn)

•	Owners prepared for long-term commitment

•	Puppies require constant supervision

•	Regular feeding schedules (3–4 meals daily initially)

•	Toilet training (including nighttime wake-ups)

•	Daily training and bonding time

Training and Socialisation

•	Positive reinforcement training is essential

•	Early socialisation with people, environments, and other dogs

•	Continued training into adolescence (large breeds can be strong and independent)

•	Basic obedience is a must (recall, lead walking, etc.)

•	As they grow, these puppies will need plenty of exercise

•	Mental enrichment (toys, puzzles, training) is equally important

•	Balance exercise appropriately while growing (protect joints)

•	Secure garden required for a large dog

•	Enough space for a growing dog

•	Safe, puppy-proofed environment

name: Jet
description:
Jet may be an older Staffy girl, but she is definitely young at heart! Full of enthusiasm and personality, she still loves making the most of every day and is looking for a family who can match her zest for life.

One of Jet's favourite things is getting out and about on her walks, where she can enjoy plenty of sniffing, exploring, and taking in the world around her. She's an active girl who loves having adventures with her people.

Jet is also a big foodie and never says no to a tasty treat! In fact, she'll happily show off her tricks in exchange for a reward, making her a fun and engaging companion to spend time with.

When she's not out exploring, Jet loves playing with her toys and will often bring them over to encourage you to join in the fun. Whether it's a game of fetch or simply some interactive playtime, she enjoys spending quality time with her favourite humans.

Jet is looking for an experienced home with an active family who can provide her with the exercise, enrichment, and companionship she enjoys. While she likes to socialise with other dogs when out on her walks, she prefers having her own space at home and would therefore need to be the only dog in the household.

If you're looking for a loving, playful, and loyal companion with plenty of life left to live, Jet could be the perfect match for you.

name: Lassie
description:
Lassie is a gentle and easy-going girl who is looking for a calm home where she can enjoy a slower pace of life. She walks beautifully on a loose lead and loves pottering around the garden, sniffing her surroundings and then finding a comfortable spot for a well-earned nap.

While affectionate, Lassie prefers to build relationships at her own pace. She may be a little reserved when meeting new people but soon warms up and enjoys gentle fuss and attention. She would thrive with adopters who appreciate that she doesn't need constant entertainment and are happy to let her settle gradually into home life. She enjoys routine and would be happiest in a quieter household without lots of visitors.

Lassie would love a secure garden where she can spend time outdoors at her own pace. Due to her typical sighthound prey drive, she will need a home without cats or small pets. She has limited experience with other dogs and would be best suited to a home where she does not need to share her living space with another dog.

Secondary school aged children could live with Lassie, providing they understand her need for space and gentle interactions. In return, this lovely girl will make a wonderful companion for someone looking for a quiet, loyal friend to share life's simpler moments with.

name: Moon
description:
Moon is a quiet, sensitive lurcher who has had a difficult time recently, and it shows. He’s feeling very sad and unsure of the world right now, but beneath that sadness is a soft, trusting soul who just needs the right people to help him feel safe again.

Moon is a gentle, thoughtful dog who watches the world carefully before deciding how he feels about it. He isn’t the type to rush into new situations — he needs time, kindness, and consistency. Once he begins to trust, you see the real Moon

He will need new owners who understand that trust isn’t instant. It’s earned slowly, with patience and soft voices. If you enjoy building relationships at a steady pace, Moon will reward you with loyalty and quiet affection

Moon does have some ongoing medical issues. These will be discussed fully with potential adopters so they can make an informed and confident decision. He is still very capable of enjoying life — he just needs owners who are willing to support him as he ages.

Moon isn’t a dog who will bounce into your arms on day one. He’s a dog who will choose you slowly, and that choice will mean everything. In the right home, with the right people, Moon has every chance of becoming a much happier boy — relaxed, secure, and finally able to enjoy his twilight years with dignity and love.

name: Dexter
description:
Dexter loves nothing more than spending time with people and being out and about on his daily adventures. Friendly, affectionate and full of character, he enjoys fuss, cuddles and accompanying his favourite humans wherever he can. Once settled, he is happy relaxing at home and enjoys being close to his favourite people.

Despite his age, Dexter is still an energetic chap who enjoys his walks, playtime with his toys and exploring new places. In foster he's settled in brilliantly, enjoying games of fetch and showing everyone just how much enthusiasm he still has for life.

Dexter has always been sociable with people and greets visitors warmly before settling down to relax nearby. He could share his home with teenagers and may be able to live with a calm, friendly dog of a similar size and temperament.

Dexter is looking for a home where he can enjoy regular walks, companionship and a predictable routine. He isn't a fan of cats and has been known to bark at or chase them, so his new home will need to be cat free.

His foster carers describe him as "very spritely for an old boy" and it's easy to see why. Dexter is a lovely companion who still has plenty of life, love and adventures left to share with his new family.

name: Chester
description:
Chester is a happy, active young Cockapoo with a big personality and an even bigger love for being with his people. He thrives on companionship and enjoys being involved in whatever his family is doing.

As an energetic young dog, Chester is looking for an active home that can provide him with plenty of exercise, playtime, and mental stimulation. He loves playing with his toys and has lots of enthusiasm for life, so he'll make a wonderful companion for someone who enjoys getting out and about.

Chester could happily live with a calm, confident dog that is comfortable with his playful nature and can match his energy while helping him continue to build his confidence and social skills. He could also live with older, dog-savvy children who understand how to interact respectfully with an active young puppy. Chester may also be able to share his home with a confident, dog-savvy cat that is comfortable living with an energetic young dog, with careful introductions and appropriate management.

Being a Cockapoo, Chester's coat will require regular grooming to keep it healthy and comfortable. He will need his new family to continue introducing him to grooming in a positive, patient way so it becomes a normal part of his routine.

As he is still a young dog, Chester will also benefit from ongoing training and guidance. He is eager to learn and will thrive with consistent, reward-based training to help him become the best companion he can be.

name: Ted
description:
Ted is an affectionate and friendly lad who loves nothing more than spending time with people. He’s a real people dog and is happiest when soaking up attention, enjoying a fuss, or joining his family for a gentle adventure. He settles well in the home and enjoys a relaxed lifestyle with plenty of company.

Having spent much of the last year without regular walks due to his previous owners becoming unwell, Ted is now looking for a family who can spend some time settling him into a routine. He has enjoyed getting back out and about and has shown that he is friendly towards other dogs, although he can be a little excitable when greeting them after such a long break from social interactions. Ted could potentially share his home with another friendly dog.

Ted has a lovely history around children and has previously lived with a young child, so he could live with children of primary school age and above. He enjoys being around people and is equally happy joining in with family activities or relaxing nearby after a walk.

Ted is searching for a kind and understanding home that can provide a consistent routine, plenty of companionship, regular walks and lots of love. In return, he promises to be a loyal and affectionate companion who will enjoy being by your side through everyday life.

name: Mars
description:
Mars is a wonderful greyhound who offers the perfect balance between playful energy and calm relaxation. He has a charming, quirky nature and an easy-going temperament that makes him a pleasure to be around.

Mars loves his toys and is always enthusiastic about mealtimes, often showing his excitement with tappy feet and chattering teeth. Out on walks, he is a delight to handle as he explores his surroundings through careful sniffing. He is also keen to greet other dogs and has been making excellent progress in his training to offer calm, polite hellos.

After his adventures, Mars is quick to settle down and enjoy a well-earned rest in his bed. With his affectionate personality, gentle manners, and endearing quirks, Mars is sure to make a wonderful companion in his forever home.

Mars could live with older children and another dog. He couldn’t live with small animals or cats, as they would be too fun to chase!

name: River
description:
Please meet River, she is looking for a very specific home. She will be a big project for any new owner.

Her new owners will need to be patient, supportive, and understanding as this little lady has been through a lot and has been in our care for over 200 days! She finds the home life and the world overwhelming at times, so whilst she settles in at home River will need to have a puppy pen to settle into which the team can talk more about.

She is making progress with housetraining, but it is likely that she’ll have an accident or two whilst she gets used to her new home. River could live with children aged 12 and over who are able to understand that she might take some time to make friends with them.

She needs to be the only pet in her new home as she is vocal and underconfident with dogs and doesn’t desire to have any interactions with them, and she may chase cats. So her new owners will need to continue to help her avoid dogs in the future and have low expectations of her meeting dogs.

River is looking for new owners who are around for the majority of the day to help her learn how to spend time on her own after she has settled in.

If you can commit to helping River learn how fun being a pet is, please get in touch today.

name: Daisy
description:
Daisy is a sweet and friendly young girl with bundles of energy. She loves to be on the go and thrives when she has something to do, whether that’s playing tug, chasing after a squeaky toy or engaging with training. Her playful, busy nature makes her great fun to be around.

As a young and energetic dog, Daisy is looking for active owners that can offer a consistent daily routine, time for ongoing training and plenty of physical exercise and mental enrichment.

Daisy is social with other dogs and enjoys their company. When on the lead she can become strong and pull towards them, so she will need owners who can manage this safely and continue her training to help her feel calmer and more focused..

She would be best suited to a home with a secure, fully enclosed garden where she can burn off some of her energy safely. Daisy may be able to share her home with another dog, pending suitable introductions. She is best suited to a home without cats.

If you’re looking for an active and affectionate companion and have the time to support her training and energy levels, Daisy could make a wonderful addition to your home.

name: Wilbur (and Edna)
description:
Say hello to the lovely Wilbur and his glamourous companion Edna, this delightful couple are hoping you’ll be the ones to take them home and snuggle up with them on the sofa!

This marvellous couple have been in a foster home recently and have had a golden report, ‘they’re both delightful and have settled in really well! Wilbur is very much a lap dog, and he takes his job very seriously – every lap in the house has been tested out by him. Edna loves to be sit next to you on the sofa and will tap you with her paw if you’ve stopped and she wants more. Wilbur is great at chasing pigeons off the grass and Edna is chief fly exterminator, what more could anyone want?!’.

They could live with older school age children who would like to play a part in their daily routine and maybe sneak them a treat or two – although they do need to lose a bit of weight! This delightful duo is ideally looking for a multi carer household so there are multiple people to love on them but most importantly, more than one lap! Edna and Wilbur will need to be the only pets in their new home so they can take centre stage and not have to share their people.

Although they are small, these two adventurers certainly like to get their steps in and have conquered many treks in their time, so if their new owners wanted to take them out to interesting and unfamiliar locations to explore, then Edna and Wilbur are ready and waiting with their harnesses on!

If these two are the charming canines you’ve been searching for, please get in touch today.

name: Edna (and Wilbur)
description:
Say hello to the lovely Edna and her ever faithful companion Wilbur, this delightful couple are hoping you’ll be the ones to take them home and snuggle up with them on the sofa!

This marvellous couple have been in a foster home recently and have had a golden report, ‘they’re both delightful and have settled in really well! Wilbur is very much a lap dog, and he takes his job very seriously – every lap in the house has been tested out by him. Edna loves to be sit next to you on the sofa and will tap you with her paw if you’ve stopped and she wants more. Wilbur is great at chasing pigeons off the grass and Edna is chief fly exterminator, what more could anyone want?!’.

They could live with older school age children who would like to play a part in their daily routine and maybe sneak them a treat or two – although they do need to lose a bit of weight! This delightful duo is ideally looking for a multi carer household so there are multiple people to love on them but most importantly, more than one lap! Edna and Wilbur will need to be the only pets in their new home so they can take centre stage and not have to share their people.

Although they are small, these two adventurers certainly like to get their steps in and have conquered many treks in their time, so if their new owners wanted to take them out to interesting and unfamiliar locations to explore, then Edna and Wilbur are ready and waiting with their harnesses on!

If these two are the charming canines you’ve been searching for, please get in touch today.

name: Arnold
description:
Meet Arnold, a 3-year-old retired racing greyhound who is ready for a cozy sofa and a lifetime of cuddles.

This handsome, big boy is the definition of a "gentle giant"—friendly, bouncy, and always eager to greet both humans and fellow hounds. While Arnold has plenty of love to give, his background means he is still learning about the big wide world.

Arnold is a social soul who truly enjoys the company of other dogs. Whether he’s walking side-by-side with his greyhound friends or engaging in some silly, bouncy play, he’s a polite lad who is just as happy to go with the flow if his companions prefer a quieter time.

When he isn’t out exploring, Arnold’s favourite hobby is napping, you’ll often find him sprawled out, enjoying a legendary greyhound snooze. He is a true "love bug" who can’t wait to find a family that will appreciate his goofy personality and provide him with all the affection he deserves.

name: Cha Cha
description:
Meet lovely Cha Cha, a gentle and sensitive lurcher looking for a calm and patient forever home.

Cha Cha can find the world a little overwhelming at times, so she’s looking for understanding owners who will give her the time and space she needs to settle in and build confidence at her own pace.

She could live with older children who can read her body language and respect her space when needed.

We feel Cha Cha would really benefit from living with another calm and well-matched dog who can help her feel more secure and show her the ropes. Ideally new owners would also be home for most of the day to help her transition from kennel to sofa.

Typical of her breed, Cha Cha has a strong prey drive, so she will need a home without cats or small furries.

A secure garden where she can have a run around and spend quiet time with her people as she adjusts into her new environment would be beneficial.

If you have a lurcher-shaped hole in your home and the patience to help Cha Cha blossom, please apply today.

name: Guinness
description:
Meet gentle Guinness.

This handsome boy is on the lookout for a quiet home, where he can go for nice walks and then take some time to rest at home.

Guinness is a sweet boy, he can be a little sensitive in new situations so new owners will need to be happy to go slowly and let him settle in at a slow pace and not rush him.

We feel he could live with a nice calm older dog, they will just need to make friends first here at the centre. Sadly he is unable to live with cats.

Guinness could live with children in his new home, they will need to make friends at the centre first and ideally children who will give him time and space with readjustment to a new home.

People who have had Pointers or working type breeds before will be beneficial but not a need. Guinness will just need owners who are able to give him both mental and physical stimulation to keep his brain engaged.

Guinness can be left during the day but initially we would say no more than 4hours whilst he getting to know his new family and surroundings. Time can be slowly built up for leaving.

Please ask about this lovely boy today.

name: Paco
description:
Paco is a friendly and sociable boy who loves spending time with both people and other dogs. He enjoys getting out and about and travels well in the car, making him a great companion for little adventures with his new family. Paco can get excited when meeting people and may jump up initially to say hello, but his affectionate nature quickly shines through.

Paco could live with children of primary school age and above who are comfortable around lively dogs. He may also be able to share his home with another friendly dog. Paco isn’t suited to live with cats, but he could live alongside small pets if they are kept separate.

He’s looking for a home with a secure garden as he has previously shown he’s quite the explorer and has been known to take himself off to the park! Paco would love a family who can keep him secure while still giving him plenty of opportunities to enjoy walks, playtime and companionship.

Paco will benefit from a calm and consistent settling-in period with a predictable routine and a quiet space where he can relax and feel safe while adjusting to home life.

Flat faced (brachycephalic) dogs suffer from additional health problems, which may impact their quality of life. We always recommend anyone thinking about adopting or buying a flat faced (brachycephalic) dog to thoroughly research the breed beforehand. Our expert team will be able to give you tips on exercise and weight management and advice on any support Paco may need.

name: Lulu
description:
Lulu is a sweet and affectionate girl who is searching for a loving forever home. She has a gentle nature and enjoys being around people, often forming strong bonds with those she trusts.

Whether it’s heading out for a short stroll or curling up for some quiet time, Lulu is happiest when she’s sharing her day with her humans. She has a friendly personality with a lovely mix of playfulness and calmness. Lulu doesn’t need a large amount of exercise, making her well suited to a more relaxed lifestyle, but she still loves to have fun.

She really enjoys playing with her soft, squeaky toys and will happily engage in games with her people. Lulu also has her playful, silly moments where she gets the zoomies, racing around with excitement and showing off her joyful, cheeky side before settling down again.

Lulu could live with older school-aged children who can understand and respect her boundaries. She would prefer to be the only dog in the home so she can enjoy all the attention, though she may be able to live with a calm, dog-savvy cat following sensible introductions.

Lulu would thrive in a home where she can have a consistent routine and plenty of companionship. In the right home, she will blossom into a loyal and loving companion who will bring plenty of fun, affection, and those happy zoomie moments into everyday life.

name: Steve
description:
Steve is a handsome retired greyhound with a heart full of love and a gentle nature.

This sweet boy absolutely thrives on human company, he loves to lean into you for comfort and enjoys every bit of fuss and affection you can offer.

Steve is still building his confidence around other dogs, so while he wouldn’t be able to share his home with one, he’s happy to meet canine friends out on walks and continue growing his social circle. He is looking for a home without cats or small pets.

Steve could live with children who understand he may need a little time and patience to find his paws and settle into home life at his own pace.

A secure garden would be just perfect for him to stretch his legs, enjoy a playful run, and then curl up for a peaceful snooze in a shady spot.

This loving boy is ready to start his next chapter, could you be the one to give Steve his pawfect ending?

name: Willow
description:
Meet wonderful Willow!

Willow is a fun-loving, young Bulldog Cross who is bursting with personality and energy! She’s a lively lass who enjoys playtime and exploring, and she’ll thrive in a home that can keep up with her active and enthusiastic nature.

She’s a sociable girl who could happily share her home with another dog, especially one that enjoys a good play and matches her confident, friendly style.

She takes comfort from companionship and would benefit from having company for much of the day, as she can find being left alone a little challenging. Because of this, she’s looking for committed adopters who are around for most of the day and can help her build confidence gradually.

She is able to live with children, but this will be dependent on successful meets at the centre to ensure everyone is a good match. Her playful energy means she’ll be best suited to a family who understand his needs and can provide guidance, routine, and plenty of positive outlets for his energy.

In return, Willow will  bring lots of fun, affection, and character into her new home.

If you’re looking for a loyal companion who will keep you smiling and love being part of the action, Willow could be the perfect fit!

name: Indy
description:
Indy is a friendly and affectionate Beagle with a curious nature and a true love for exploring the world around her. Like many of her breed, she enjoys following her nose, making every walk an exciting adventure full of new scents and discoveries. She’s a sociable girl who enjoys human company and thrives when she can be part of day-to-day family life.

Indy would suit an active home that can provide her with plenty of exercise, enrichment, and companionship. Her inquisitive nature means she benefits from continued training, particularly around recall, and she would love activities such as scent games to keep her mind engaged. A secure garden would be ideal, as Beagles are well-known for their determination when an interesting smell catches their attention.

She has a lovely temperament and could potentially live with another friendly dog following successful introductions, and she may also be suitable to live with older children who understand how to interact respectfully with dogs. With the right guidance and consistency, Indy will grow into a loyal and rewarding companion.

Indy is full of character and charm, and she’s sure to bring plenty of joy, energy, and companionship to her new home. If you’re looking for a loving dog to join you on adventures and become a valued member of your family, Indy could be the perfect match.

name: Freddie
description:
Meet the handsome Freddie,

Freddie is a sweet and gentle little dog who builds lovely bonds with his people. Once he feels safe, he enjoys sitting close by, often choosing to curl up next to you on the sofa and asking for affection with a gentle nudge. He also has a playful side and enjoys soft toys when he’s feeling relaxed and settled.

Freddie has been steadily building confidence in his foster home and is looking for a calm and understanding home where he is able to continue to build his confidence. Having lived a very sheltered life, the outside world can feel overwhelming for him, and he will need patient owners who are happy to take things at his pace. Given time and space, Freddie shows he can settle well and becomes a friendly and affectionate companion.

He will initially be happiest as a house and garden dog, with quiet routines and gradual introductions to new experiences. Short, calm walks can be introduced once he feels settled, as he can be sensitive to noise and busier environments.

Freddie may be able to live with a calm, confident dog who can help him feel more secure. He could share his home with children around the age of 13 and above, provided interactions are managed sensitively and he has space to retreat when needed. He cannot live with cats as he finds him too exciting to chase.

Freddie is a lovely boy who, with patience and understanding, will make a rewarding companion for someone willing to help him discover the world.

name: Flash
description:
*Please read before applying for Flash.*

Flash is a very handsome boy, as you can see by his pictures. He is very worried and under-socialised so is on the lookout for a very specific home.

He is looking for new owners who have got time, patience, and dedication to spend with him helping him get used to the big wide world.

Ideally owners who are used to high energy breeds, and nervous dogs so that you will have an understanding of his needs. With this in mind, Flash is also a clever boy so would be eager to learn and with time he will flourish.

Flash is best as the only pet for now, potentially in the future he could live with a dog but for now all the attention needs to be on Flash. Continued socialisation with dogs will be key for this young pup.

Ideally he can live with children, children who won't pester him and will have an understanding of his needs. Allow him time to settle and readjust.

If you feel you could offer this sweet soul a home, then please do apply today. With time, love and understanding this boy will become your faithful friend.

name: Coco
description:
Have you ever been so excited to see your friend that you zoom around in circles? Coco has!

Have you ever been so happy to see a dog that you try to get them to chase you? Coco has!

Do you ever just wish someone would just throw a ball or something? Coco does!

Sweet and social with both people and dogs, Coco is ready to make some new friends! She can be a little shy initially, but it doesn’t take long for her to make you her bestie. She could live with children who were able to understand that she needs to take her time getting to know people.

Coco has a big brain so is eager to learn, but she's no fool, she's not doing it for free - make sure you have some treats ready for her. Her favourite treats are: all of them.

Coco has spent time in a home living with dogs before for short periods, so we think with the right match she would be happy to do so more permanently. She is keen to make dog friends out on walks but can come across over-eager so needs some help with boundaries. Coco does have a history of chasing cats so may struggle with one in her new home.

Finally, and possibly most importantly, Coco loves a caravan holiday! So if you are looking for a happy-go-lucky girl who you can treat to a staycation, then please apply for Coco!

name: Lassie
description:
Meet lovely Lassie, a young German Shepherd looking for a patient and understanding home where she can continue to build her confidence.

We know very little about Lassie’s life before coming into our care, but since arriving at the centre she has shown herself to be a bright, affectionate and playful young dog. Although initially worried by her new surroundings, she is becoming more relaxed each day with gentle support. Lassie will need multiple visits at our Bromsgrove Centre to help build a bond with her new family.

As a typical adolescent shepherd, Lassie is energetic and enthusiastic. She enjoys spending time with people but can become overexcited, so she will benefit from ongoing training using positive reinforcement to help her develop appropriate social skills.

Due to her unknown background, Lassie will need support with house training and learning to spend short periods alone. She is not yet ready to be left for long periods and would suit a home where someone is around for much of the day while she settles in.

Lassie would thrive with an active family who can provide mental stimulation, training and appropriate exercise as she continues to grow. She can live with older teenage children who are comfortable around large, bouncy dogs.

Lassie may be able to live with another suitably matched dog, subject to successful introductions and compatibility assessments at the centre.

With patience, consistency and lots of love, Lassie has the potential to become a loyal and rewarding companion.

name: Toast
description:
Toast is a beautiful soul and a wonderfully affectionate girl, who truly thrives on human company. She loves nothing more than being close to her favourite people and will happily spend her time by your side, making her a lovely companion for someone looking for a loyal friend.

Now in her golden years, Toast enjoys a gentler pace of life and takes great pleasure in short, sniffy walks, where she can explore the world at her own speed. She’s much more about taking in the sights and smells than covering miles, making her an ideal partner for relaxed strolls.

At home, Toast enjoys a cosy routine and plenty of companionship. She would be happiest in a home where she doesn’t have to be alone for long and can share lots of quality time with her new family.

Toast could live with older teenagers who will understand Toast's desire for a gentle and quiet life. Toast would prefer not to live with other dogs or cats.

name: Alfie
description:
Alfie is a real people dog who loves nothing more than spending time with his favourite humans. He forms strong bonds with those he knows and is always delighted to meet new people, greeting everyone with his happy, friendly nature.

Playtime is one of Alfie's favourite things! He loves tossing his toys around and happily entertaining himself, and it's impossible not to smile as he zooms about having the best time.

On walks, Alfie prefers to take life at a slower pace. Rather than rushing from A to B, he likes to stop, explore, and investigate every interesting scent along the way. He's a sociable boy who enjoys saying hello to other dogs when he's out and about.

Alfie also has a wonderfully quirky habit of weaving between your legs to ask for a good back scratch! He can sometimes jump up for attention if he feels he's being overlooked, so he'll need owners who are steady on their feet and can continue to encourage polite greetings.

With his cheerful personality, affectionate nature, and fun loving spirit, Alfie is sure to fill his new home with laughter, love, and happiness. Could you be the family he's been waiting for?

name: Junie
description:
Junie is a gentle, sensitive girl who forms incredibly strong bonds with the people she knows and trusts. Once she's settled and feels safe, she'll absolutely adore you and happily become your little shadow, always wanting to be close by.

Because Junie is such a sensitive soul, she's looking for a calm, quiet, and predictable home where she can feel secure and supported. She isn't asking for much - just some steady companionship, a loving family, and a garden or peaceful walking route where she can enjoy gentle adventures at her own pace.

Having lived a rather sheltered life, Junie can find new experiences a little overwhelming. She'll thrive with a patient and understanding owner who is happy to let her build her confidence at her own pace. With kindness, reassurance, and plenty of love, Junie will truly blossom into the loyal and affectionate companion she's waiting to become.

If you're looking for a devoted best friend who will reward your patience with unwavering love and companionship, Junie could be the perfect match for you.

attributes.Breed: Dogue de bordeaux Cross American Bulldog

name: Coco
description:
This lovely girl is Coco!  She is looking for a forever home where she can enjoy all the love and attention she deserves.  She is an incredibly friendly, affectionate girl who absolutely loves being around people.  If you’re looking for a loyal companion who enjoys lots of fuss she could be the dog for you.  She does sometimes forget how big and strong she is and will jump up to say hello or when she wants some attention, so she will benefit from owners who can continue to encourage good manners and discourage the jumping up.  She does know her basic commands and would benefit from further training as she is eager to please.  She would thrive in a home that can give her plenty of love, attention, and continued guidance.  She would benefit from a private secure garden.

She has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, she can be a little unsure at first but once she has had a sniff she settles down.  We feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where her new adopter can continue her socialisation with other dogs in a controlled manner and build her confidence.

As She came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would like any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Drago
description:
Drago is an ex-racer that has come straight to us from the racing kennels. Due to always being in kennels Drago will need time to settle and get used to every day life in the home. With patience and love greyhounds make a perfect companion to any household.

Drago is a lively boy as he is still quite young and has a high prey drive. He would love a family that could take him out on lots of adventures to burn off his energy. Drago would benefit from some socialisation with new environments. He is a friendly boy with all of the staff and volunteers here on site and loves to give out kisses.

Drago can be rehomed with children over the age of 12 years old and can potentially be rehomed with another dog of a similar size and temperament – subject to a successful meet and greet.

Do you think you could give this handsome boy his forever home?

Please do not apply for a dog if you are going on holiday within the next 3 months.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Dakota
description:
Dakota is looking for her forever home, and she has so much love to give.  She’s an incredibly sweet, friendly girl who absolutely adores people.  If you’re happy to give her plenty of fuss, she’ll be in her element!  Despite her size, she is a gentle soul with a lovely nature and enjoys spending time with her favourite humans.  She already knows some basic commands and is very eager to please so would benefit from further training.  Being a young, strong dog, she can be powerful on the lead, so her new adopter will need to be confident and committed to continue her lead training.  Because of this, she will be best suited to an active home where she can enjoy plenty of exercise, adventures and mental stimulation.  If you are looking for a loyal, affectionate companion with a heart as big as she is, this lovely girl could be the one for you.  She will need a private secure garden.

She has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, and is very playful and can be too much for other dogs as she does get in their face so for this reason we feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where she can continue to learn manners and boundaries around other dogs will the full support of her new adopter with lots of positive reinforcement.

As Dakota came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Toki
description:
This gorgeous girl is Toki! She is looking for her forever home where she can continue to flourish.  At just 18 months old, she is full of life, love and endless enthusiasm.  She’s a very sweet and friendly girl.  She can be a little unsure when meeting new people, but with a little patience, she soon comes out of her shell and is more than ready for plenty of fuss.  Like many young Staffies, she’s full of energy and absolutely loves to play.  Toys are one of her favourite things, and she’s always up for a game.  She’s a fun, giddy girl who will keep you smiling with her playful personality.  She is still learning her manners and doesn’t quite understand personal space.  She loves attention and will jump up to say hello.  She’s also discovered that food on tables is very tempting and won’t think twice about jumping on the table and helping herself if given the chance!  Her new adopter will need to be committed to continuing her training and setting clear, consistent boundaries. She can be strong on the lead, so she will benefit from ongoing lead training and an active home that can provide plenty of exercise, enrichment and positive guidance.  She will need a private secure garden.

Toki has socialised  with other dogs whilst in our care and is very playful and giddy and doesn’t really know when to stop.  We do feel she could potentially live with another dog that would tolerate her full-on behaviour following a successful meet and greet. (must be neutered).

As she came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Lucy
description:
Lucy came into our care from another rescue kennels so we don’t have much history on her. Lucy was quite nervous and reserved when she first arrived and can still be a little nervous around new people. Lucy needs a patient and understanding home that can give her the time she needs to adjust to her new surroundings. Lucy is a very sweet and affectionate girl once she gets to know you. She has lots of energy due to only being a young dog so is after an active home with a family that can take her out on lots of adventures to burn off her energy. Lucy loves her toys and likes to bring them to you to say hello. We think that Lucy’s new family should have some experience with dogs so that they can help her grow and thrive.

Lucy has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care but does prefer people to dogs. Due to this we believe that Lucy would prefer to be the only dog in the home.

Lucy can be rehomed with Children who are 14 years and over – subject to a successful meet.

Please don’t apply for a dog if you are going on holiday within the next 3 months.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Olive
description:
This gorgeous girl is Olive!  She is a sweet, friendly girl with a heart full of love.  She absolutely craves attention and will happily soak up all the fuss and affection you can give her.  She’s a happy, energetic soul who loves to make people smile, especially when she bursts into a case of the famous Lurcher zoomies! Full of fun and always on the go, she will thrive in an active home who can keep up with her adventurous spirit.  She can get a little over-excited and does have a habit of jumping up for attention, so she will benefit from continued training and gentle guidance.  In return, you’ll have a loyal companion who simply wants to be by your side.  She is good on the lead, enjoys her walks, and gets on well with other dogs. She’s very playful with them so we feel she could potentially live with another dog of similar size following a successful meet and greet (must be neutered).  If you are looking for a loving, fun-filled companion with plenty of personality, this wonderful girl could be the perfect dog for you.  She’s ready to find a forever home where she can share all the love, laughter and zoomies she has to offer. She will need a private secure garden so she can safely have off lead time.

As Olive came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 8+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Sparky
description:
This little character is Sparky!  He is looking for an understanding and committed home where he can continue to learn and build his confidence.  We think he has had very little socialisation in his early life, so he can be extremely nervous when meeting new people and experiencing unfamiliar situations.  However, once he gets to know and trust you, he is a sweet, affectionate and friendly boy who enjoys lots of fuss.  He is a lively young lad with plenty of energy and loves to play fetch.  One of his favourite things is carrying his toys around with him, and will proudly come over to show what he has got.  As he lacks basic training, he does not currently know how to walk on a lead and will require patient, positive reinforcement training to help him learn.  He can be quite barky at times, especially when unsure of situations, and he does jump up a lot when he gets excited.  When over stimulated, he can become a bit nippy.  He is also know to nip at ankles when the staff leave his kennel, so any potential adopter must be prepared to work on his impulse control and training.  Due to these behaviours we would like any children in the home to be 12+ years.  This lovely boy came into us after being found confined in a crate with 2 other dogs, so it looks like he has missed out on many of the normal experiences and training opportunities that young dogs should have.  With time, patience, consistency and kindness, this loveable youngster has the potential to become a wonderful companion.  He would benefit from a private secure garden.  He will need an experienced owner.

He has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care but he does prefer his own space so we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Samuel
description:
This handsome boy is Samuel!  He is a fun-loving, affectionate lad who is looking for an active home.
