He is an incredibly friendly and sweet-natured boy who loves being around people and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention.  As a typical young dog, he has lots of energy and enthusiasm, so he’ll thrive with owners who can give him plenty of exercise, playtime, mental stimulation and continued training.  He loves nothing more than a game of fetch and will happily spend time chasing his favourite toys.  He’s always ready for an adventure and would make a fantastic companion for someone with an active lifestyle.  Being a young dog, he can become overexcited and will jump up to greet people.  He can also be a little nippy during these excited moments, so he’ll need patient owners who are committed to helping him learn calmer manners through positive reinforcement.  He is a strong boy on the lead and would benefit from further lead training.  In his kennel, he does chase the hose and brushes – it’s all part of his playful, cheeky personality.  This lovely lad is looking for a home that understands young, energetic dogs and is willing to invest the time and training he will need.  He will need a private secure garden.

He has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care and can be a little nervous and unsure at first, so we would like him to be the only dog in the home where he can slowly build up his confidence around other dogs with the full support of his new adopter.

As he came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him and as he can be a little nippy when he is overexcited we would like any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Bruce
description:
This handsome boy is Bruce!  He is a sweet-natured lad with plenty of love to give.  Full of character and enthusiasm, he absolutely loves playtime and can often be found carrying his favourite toy around or trying to persuade you into a game of fetch.  When he is excited, he can become quite giddy and mouthy, so he would benefit from adopters who understand his playful nature and can continue to guide him with calm, consistent boundaries.  Despite his excitable side, he already knows his basic commands and enjoys learning and engaging with people. He thrives on companionship and would make a wonderful addition to an active home that can provide him with lots of walks, adventures, enrichment and affection.  He generally prefers the company of female dogs and can sometimes be a little dominant around male dogs, so for this reason we feel he could be rehomed with a female dog following a successful meet and greet.

This loveable boy has so much to offer and is ready to find a family who will appreciate his fun-loving personality and loyal nature.  If you’re looking for a playful, affectionate companion with plenty of zest for life, he could be the one for you.

As Bruce came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Coco
description:
Coco came into our care as a stray so we don’t have any background information on her. She is a sweet but shy girl at times. She does get excitable once she gets to know you. With time and patience she will be a great companion for any home. She loves her walks and going outside for plenty of sniffs. As she came in as a stray, she would benefit from some basic training to help her flourish.

Coco can be rehomed with children 12+ – subject to a successful meet.

Coco has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care but can be a little nervous at first. We believe that she could potentially be rehomed with another dog of a similar size and with a calm temperament.

Please don’t apply for a dog if you are going on holiday within the next 3 months.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Pink
description:
This lovely girl is Pink! She is a friendly, affectionate girl who is sure to seal your heart.  She absolutely loves human company and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention, with bum scratches being one of her favourite things in the world! She has a playful side and loves chasing around with her toys, especially if it squeaks.  She’s always ready for a game and enjoys interacting with people.  Water is another of her passions, and unfortunately does chase the hose whilst the staff are trying to clean, but once the cleaning is done she loves to splash about in our paddling pool.  She can be quite vocal in her kennel, especially when she is looking for attention, but once out she does settle down.  She does know her basic commands and walks nicely on the lead, enjoying her outings making her a wonderful companion.  This sweet, playful girl is ready to find a home where she can enjoy plenty of affection, playtime and adventures.

Pink has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, although she does bark at first, once she has had a sniff she soon loses interest.  For this reason, we think she would best to be rehomed as the only dog in the home where she can get 100% of the attention.

As Pink came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Roseitta
description:
This gorgeous girl is Roseitta! She is a sweet, affectionate girl who loves being around people and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention.  She can be a little unsure when meeting new people at first, but she quickly comes around especially if you have tasty treats.  She is a playful girl who absolutely loves a game of fetch and will happily spend time chasing her favourite ball.  Once she’s settled, her fun-loving personality really shines through.  When she first arrived with us, she required eye drops and was exceptionally good and cooperative with her treatment, allowing them to be administered without any issues.  She was very nervous when attending the vets for her vaccination, and as a result, adopters should be aware that she may need to be muzzled for veterinary visits.  With patient handling and positive experiences, her confidence will continue to grow.  She is a wonderful, loving dog looking for a home that will give her the time, understanding and affection she deserves.  In return, she will make a loyal and devoted companion.  She will need a private secure garden.  Previous experience of the breed would be preferred.

Roseitta has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care, but she doesn’t like other dogs in her face so we think she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where she can get 100% of the fuss and attention.

As Roseitta came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children/visiting children to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Jax
description:
This stunning boy is Jax! we are looking for an experienced home for this fantastic lad.  He is a friendly and affectionate boy who absolutely loves attention and enjoys a good fuss.  He has a wonderful nature and is always happy to spend time with his people. This clever lad is exceptionally well behaved at the vets, taking examinations and handling in his stride, which is a testament to his temperament and trainability.  Like many dogs, he has his own little quirks – one of his favourites is enthusiastically ragging his blankets and rearranging them to his liking!  As a typical Belgian Malinois, he is highly intelligent, energetic and thrives on both physical exercise and mental stimulation.  He loves charging around our compound and would be happiest in a home where he has plenty of opportunities to stay active and engaged.  Because of his breed traits, he will need an owner who has previous experience with Belgian Malinois and understands their needs.  He is not a dog that will be content with a short walk around the block, he requires ongoing training, enrichment and a lifestyle that allows him to use both his body and brain.  He will need a private secure garden.

He has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care, so we feel he could possibly be rehomed with another dog (similar size) following a successful meet and greet.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

As Jax came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Sunny
description:
Sunny is a strong happy boy who loves attention and a cuddle off all of the staff and volunteers he meets. He has a lot of energy so can be boisterous at times so his new family will need to do some basic training with him so that he can learn some manners. He loves his walks, toys and treats! He’s a happy go lucky guy who is looking for his new forever home and a comfy sofa to sleep on. Sunny would benefit from an enclosed garden that he can have off lead time in to burn off some energy in between walks.

Due to his boisterous behaviour we believe that Sunny would be best suited to be rehomed with children who are 16 years and over – subject to a successful  meet.

Sunny has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care but can be full on for some dogs. We think that if the other dog is a similar size or over and can match his energy levels he could potentially be rehomed with another dog – subject to a successful meet.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Loki
description:
Meet Loki! This handsome young boy is just 10 months old and is looking for a patient and understanding home to help him continue growing in confidence.  He can be timid and nervous when meeting new people, but with a little time, kindness and reassurance he soon comes around and starts to show his true personality.  His adopters will need to continue building his confidence in new situations through positive reinforcement and gentle encouragement.  Once he feels comfortable, he is a fun-loving, giddy youngster who is full of energy and enthusiasm.  He loves to play, enjoys interaction, and can get quite excitable.  He is known to jump up for attention, so ongoing training and consistency will help him learn good manners.  As you would expect from a young dog, he is still learning.  He can be vocal in his kennel and is currently very strong on the lead, so he will benefit from further lead training and guidance from his new adopter.  With the right home willing to invest time in his training and confidence building, this sweet young lad has all the makings of a fantastic companion. He would benefit from a private secure garden with an experienced owner.

Loki is very under-socialised around other dogs and is quite unsure around them so we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where his new adopter can slowly introduce him to other dogs in a controlled environment with lots of positive reinforcement.  Puppy training classes would be great for him.

As Loki came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him and as he can be a little unsure around new people we would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Buddy
description:
Meet our little gentleman Buddy!  Unfortunately Buddy is Blind.  As he came into us as a stray we are unsure if he was born like this or his eyes have been surgically removed.  He lives his life to the fullest despite this.  He is a very friendly boy who minds his own business.  He is incredibly smart and a quick learner and picks up his environment very quickly.  He loves going for walks and interacts very well with other dogs but can be quite humpy with them.  He is a lovely affectionate boy who has adapted remarkably well to life without his sight and approaches the world with confidence.  He may be able to live with another dog that would tolerate his humpy behaviour whilst his new adopter put training and management in place following a successful meet and greet.  Prospective owners should understand the commitment involved in caring for a completely blind dog, previous experience would be preferred.  In return you will gain a wonderful companion with a fantastic outlook on life.  He would benefit from a private secure garden where he can safely have some off lead time.

As Buddy came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Rogue
description:
Meet the gorgeous Rogue!  She has settled into our care very well, with no issues during processing, feeding, cleaning or her first vet appointment for her vaccination.  Initially, she was a little nervous and understandably cautious in her new surroundings, but after a few days she began to build trust and her lovely personality started to shine through.  She is a sweet and affectionate girl who enjoys attention and treats, quickly learning where her favourite snacks are kept!  Once she feels comfortable, she will happily approach you for fuss and interaction.  We would recommend that her potential adopters have a few meet and greets before the adoption takes place to help her settle and build her confidence with them.  However, she is easily won over with patience, kindness and a few tasty treats.  She is sure to become a wonderful companion and much loved member of the family.  She would benefit from a private secure garden.  Experience with large breed dogs would be an advantage.

Rogue has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel following a successful meet and greet could possibly live with another calm dog of similar size (must be neutered).

As Rogue came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her and as she can be a little timid at first we would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Ben
description:
Ben came into our care as a stray so we don’t have any background information on him. He was very nervous and reserved when he first arrived but his confidence has slowly been building. Once he gets to know you he is a sweet excitable boy. We are unsure if he has ever lived in a home before so would need time to settle and get to know his new surroundings. He is unsure walking on a lead so basic lead work training and commands would be a must for him. Due to his size and breed he would need an active home that is willing to take him on lots of adventures and provide enrichment to help keep his brain stimulated.

Due to Ben being nervous when he first arrived we think that it would be best for him to go with children who are 16 years and over – Subject to a successful meet. Potential adopters need to be able to commit to multiple meets so he has chance to get to know you before going home.

Please don’t apply for a dog if you are going on holiday within the next 3 months.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Daisy
description:
Daisy was previously a racing greyhound who has been brought up in kennels. She is a smaller girl in comparison to a typical greyhound but still does have quite a high prey drive. She makes up for her size with her big personality, she is a very sweet and loving girl but can be a little cheeky at times. As like most greyhounds, Daisy enjoys a good walk and sniff in the woods, with the occasional time off lead in an enclosed area to burn off energy but will equally spend lots time relaxing on a comfy cosy bed. Daisy is after someone that is looking to give a dog lots of love and companionship, which she hasn’t previously had in her life. She in return will give you unconditional love.

Daisy can be a little nippy at times so we feel that it would be best for her to be rehomed with children who are 14 years or older – subject to a successful meet.

Daisy is currently paired and used to living with another greyhound in kennels. She has also interacted well with other dogs whilst on walks so we believe that she could be rehomed with another dog of a similar size and temperament – again subject to a successful meet.

Due to Daisy being an ex-racer it wouldn’t be suitable for him to be rehomed with cats or small furries.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Treats
description:
Treats is a retired racing greyhound who has spent all of his life in kennels and is now ready to find his forever home. Treats is an affectionate boy who is after someone who wants to give him the life that he deserves full of adventures and love. Treats is an energetic boy who loves going for his walks and having off lead time zooming around the enclosed compound area. Once he has burnt off his energy he is ready to snooze away the day on his comfy bed.

With his out going personality and loving heart, he would fit into many different households that can offer him kindness, exercise, and plenty of cosy spots to curl up in. In return, this gorgeous soul will bring warmth, devotion, and endless companionship.

We believe that Treats can be rehomed with children who are 12 years and over – subject to a successful meet.

Treats is currently paired and is used to living with other greyhounds in kennels, he has also walked and interacted well with other dogs during his time as the home so could possibly be rehomed with another dog of similar size and temperament – again subject to a successful meet.

Due to treats being an ex-racer it wouldn’t be suitable for him to be rehomed with cats or small furries.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Ranger
description:
This handsome boy is Ranger!  He is guaranteed to bring fun, energy and plenty of personality into his new home.  Affectionately known by the staff as their ‘Social butterfly’ he loves meeting new people and is always ready to make new friends wherever he goes.  He is a friendly, giddy and playful boy who approaches life with enthusiasm.  His excitement can sometimes get the better of him, and he can be quite boisterous, often jumping up to greet people.  Ongoing training and consistent boundaries will help him learn more appropriate ways to say hello.  Like many hounds, he is very vocal in his kennel and isn’t shy about letting everyone know he’s there! He thrives on interaction and enjoys being involved in the action, so will need a very active home.  Potential adopters should be aware that he may display typical hound traits like unreliable recall and great at escaping so he will need a private secure garden and careful management when outside of the house.  He is also very strong on the lead and will benefit from continued lead training and handlers who are confident managing a powerful dog.  This lad is full of energy and would suit an active home that can provide plenty of exercise, mental stimulation and opportunities for adventure.  He’ll make a fantastic companion for someone who enjoys an outdoor lifestyle and is prepared to continue his training.  In return, you’ll gain a fun-loving affectionate character with a huge zest for life and enough enthusiasm to keep a smile on your face every day.

Ranger has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care although he can be very full on with them and extremely playful.  We feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where he can get 100% of the training he needs.

As Ranger came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 12+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Csilla
description:
This gorgeous girl is Csilla.  Bursting with energy and enthusiasm she is looking for an active home that can keep up with her fun-loving personality.  One of her greatest passions is her toys!  She absolutely loves to play and will happily entertain herself – and you – for hours.  She knows exactly what toy she wants, will proudly bring it over, and drop it at your feet ready for another game.  If you’re looking for a dog who enjoys interactive play, she’s your perfect match.  She also enjoys long walks and exploring the world around her.  Due to her strength and excitement, she can be strong on the lead at times, so her new adopter should be prepared to continue with her lead training and provide her with plenty of physical and mental stimulation.

When it comes to other dogs, she prefers to take things at her own pace.  She can be nervous and avoidant around other dogs and values her personal space.  She is happy to take part in social walks with other dogs, provided they respect her boundaries and don’t pressure her to interact.  She would benefit from continued positive socialisation.  For this reason we feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home with an experienced owner that will bear in mind she prefers her own space when out on walks.

This affectionate, playful girl is searching for a home that understands her needs, respects her boundaries, and can provide the active lifestyle she craves.  She will need a private secure garden.

As Csilla came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Hermione
description:
Hermione is a beautiful and sensitive girl that is looking for a very specific home.  She is an extremely timid and nervous girl who currently finds the world quite overwhelming and will often avoid people altogether.  While she is slowly beginning to build trust with familiar staff, she still has a long way to go and will need a patient, understanding adopter who is willing to let her progress at her own pace.  Hermione isn’t the kind of dog who will settle in a few weeks, she will need time, consistency and a calm environment to truly feel safe.  One thing that does bring her comfort is the presence of other dogs.  She is noticeably more confident around them, so a home with a calm, friendly resident dog (must be neutered) could really help her continue to grow in confidence.  Hermione is looking for a quiet, adult only home with someone experienced with supporting extremely nervous dogs.  She will need a secure environment, gentle encouragement and lots of kindness, but in the right home, she has the potential to blossom into a truly rewarding companion.  She will need a private secure garden.

The potential adopter will need to commit to multiple regular meet and greets for Hermione to get to know them before the adoption can take place.

Please only apply if you have the experience and commitment to work with Hermione.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Pudding
description:
This handsome boy is Pudding.  He is a friendly, affectionate lad who loves nothing more than a good fuss and plenty of attention from his people.  He is a big, strong boy with a bouncy personality and can be a little over-enthusiastic when meeting people.  He does have a habit of jumping up for attention and sometimes forgets just how big he is, so his new adopter will need to be prepared to continue working on his manners and help him channel his excitement appropriately.  On walks, he does sometimes rag his lead at first as he gets over excited, but does settle down and walk nicely and more calmly, he does enjoy getting out and about.  Due to his size and exuberant nature, we are looking for an adopter with experience of large, strong and bouncy dogs who can provide consistent guidance, ongoing training and plenty of exercise and mental stimulation.  In return, you will gain a loving, loyal companion who thrives on human interaction and is sure to bring lots of fun and affection into his new home.  He will need a private secure garden.

Pudding has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel he could potentially live with another dog of similar size following a successful meet and greet.

As pudding came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Brandy
description:
This handsome lad is Brandy.  He is a lovely boy who is searching for an understanding home where he can continue to grow in confidence and enjoy his life to the fullest.  He can be slightly nervous when meeting new people, but once he gets to know you, he thoroughly enjoys human interaction and loves a fuss.  He forms good bonds with people and has plenty of affection to give.  In kennels, he can be very vocal and has shown some kennel guarding behaviours, but once he is out he is more settled.  He does have a tendency to jump up when excited and does forget how big he is, so he would benefit from continued training and consistent boundaries in his new home.  He is a bright dog who responds well to positive guidance.  Around other dogs, he prefers to have his own space and generally avoids close interactions.  However, he is happy to participate in social walks alongside other dogs and walks calmy in their company.  So we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home.  Due to his breed traits and prey drive he cannot live with cats of small furries.  This sweet-natured boy is looking for patient adopters who can provide ongoing training, understanding and plenty of love.  He will need a private secure garden.

As Brandy came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Quill
description:
This handsome boy is Quill.  He is a sweet and friendly lad.  He can be a little nervous when meeting new people for the first time, but once he gets to know you, his affectionate nature shines through.  He absolutely loves attention, enjoys plenty of fuss and will happily roll over for belly rubs.  When excited he can be a little mouthy.  He would benefit from continued guidance and positive reinforcement to help manage his excitement.  Around other dogs, he can be vocal when first meeting them as he is unsure.  However, once he has had a chance to have a sniff and become familiar, he settles down and is much more relaxed, so we would like Quill to be the only dog in the home where he can take his time building up his confidence with other dogs outside of the home.  He is a loving and affectionate boy with lots of character and would make a wonderful companion.

As Quill came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Gandalf
description:
Meet Gandalf!  A true gentle giant with a big heart to match his size.  This affectionate boy loves human company and enjoys plenty of fuss and attention.  He thrives on interaction and will happily spend time playing with his toys. Caucasian Shepherds are highly intelligent and naturally independent thinkers, bred to work without direction, making them confident and strong-willed dogs that require experienced handling. He would thrive in a home where he has space and structure.  Gandalf will need continued training and socialisation. A breed experienced home is essential. Due to his size and strength he needs to be the only dog in the home.  A private secure garden is a must for this gorgeous boy.

As Gandalf came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children/visiting children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

Please only apply if you have previous experience with this breed and are fully committed to fulfilling all his needs and requirements.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Scooby
description:
Meet our handsome boy Scooby, who is slowly learning that the world isn’t such a scary place after all.  When he first arrived with us, he was extremely timid and unsure around people. Since, then he has made lovely progress in the care of our kennel staff.  He will now happily take treats from familiar people and is beginning to enjoy gentle strokes and attention once he feels comfortable.  One thing that brings out his personality is playtime – he absolutely loves his toys.  Because of his nervous nature, he will need patient adopters who are willing to give him the time he needs to build trust.  Multiple meet and greets will be required before any adoption can take place so he can get to know his new family at his own pace.  He gains a lot of confidence from other dogs, so we feel he would thrive in a home with a calm and friendly resident dog who can help show him the ropes and continue building his confidence. He will need an experienced owner that has previously had very timid dogs.  With time, understanding and patience, this sweet boy has so much potential to blossom into a loyal and loving companion.  Scooby will need a private secure garden.

As Scooby came into the home as a stray, we have no previous history on him and as he is extremely timid we would prefer any children/visiting children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Ivan
description:
This handsome lad is Ivan!  He’s a friendly, affectionate boy with a big personality and an even bigger heart.  Full of energy and enthusiasm, he’s always ready for fun and loves nothing more than chasing after tennis balls.  He’s a very excitable and giddy dog who doesn’t always realise his own size, so he can jump up when greeting people or when he’s excited.  He is also quite vocal at times, especially when he wants your attention.   He knows his basic commands and is eager to interact with people, but he will need continued training and guidance, particularly when out walking as he is very strong on the lead.  Due to his size and strength, he would benefit from an experienced and active owner who can continue his training and provide him with plenty of exercise and mental stimulation.  Ivan would benefit from a private secure garden where he can safely have off lead time to play fetch with his tennis balls.

Ivan has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel he potentially could be rehomed with another dog of similar size and energy levels following a successful meet and greet.

As Ivan came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 16+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Buddy
description:
This loveable boy is Buddy! He is the perfect mix of playful energy and affectionate companionship. He’s a friendly, easy-going lad who absolutely adores people and loves being the centre of attention.  Full of character, he’s excitable, giddy and always ready to make you smile.  Whether it’s cuddles on the sofa or a game in the garden, he just wants to be involved in everything you do.  He is bursting with energy and would thrive in an active home where he can enjoy plenty of exercise, playtime and mental stimulation.  Fetch is one of his favourite games, and he will happily keep you entertained for hours chasing after a ball.  Buddy is incredibly affectionate and loves a fuss, he will make a loyal, loving companion.  One thing to note : He is quite the escape artist, so he will need a private secure garden and owners who are mindful of doors, gates and fencing.  When Buddy came into us he had an Entropian (an eye condition) which has now been repaired and he is healing nicely.

Buddy has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel he could potentially live with another high energy dog following a successful meet and greet.

As Buddy came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Buster
description:
At 12 years young, this handsome boy is proving that age is just a number! He is still full of life, loves getting out and about and really enjoys his walks.  He is always ready to explore and be part of the action.  Don’t let his age fool you, this boy can still be quite bouncy and excitable, especially when he’s happy or knows something fun is about to happen.  He has a playful spirit and plenty of personality, bringing lots of joy and energy wherever he goes.  He is looking for a home that will embrace both his golden years and his lively nature, somewhere he can enjoy regular walks, plenty of attention and a comfy spot on the sofa to relax after a busy day.  Buster is a lovely boy and has so much love left to give and would make a wonderful companion for someone willing to open their heart to an older dog. Buster can be quite strong on the lead so will need continued lead training.  Buster has now been in kennels for 94 days and is now desperately looking for his quiet retirement home.

Buster has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care but we feel he would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home where he can get 100% of the love and attention.

As Buster came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Mercy
description:
Mercy came into our care as a stray, very shut down and scared. With time and patience staff managed to win her round and she slowly came out of her shell. Little by little we are seeing her lovely personality shine through. With those she trusts, Mercy has shown to be very affectionate and sweet lady.

She is still a little unsure, especially around men. However, this is something we are currently helping her with, and her new family must be willing to continue this.

With Mercy being nervous and unsure she is looking for a very patient and understanding adult only home, where she can develop and settle in at her own pace. Her new family must have the time to be able to come and meet her multiple times so she can get to know you before she goes home.

Mercy is currently also having socialisation training around dogs to build up her confidence. She is still unsure of other dogs so needs to be the only dog in the home.

Please don’t apply for a dog if you are going on holiday within the next 3 months.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Marley
description:
Marley is an independent boy who needs a patient and understanding owner, experience with Frenchie’s may be beneficial. He can be a little unsure of new people at first but his confident grows once he gets to know you. He is also unsure of new situations, so will need someone who is able to give him the space and time he needs to settle.  Marley enjoys going out for little walks in the woods and walks very nicely on a lead. He likes to keep busy with enrichment and chews for mental stimulation.

Due to Marley coming in as a stray we believe that it is best if he goes to an adult only home, no children please.

Marley has interacted well with dogs whilst in our care so he could potentially live with another dog of similar size and temperament – subject to a possible meet.

Please don’t apply for a dog if you are going on holiday within the next 3 months.

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: King
description:
Meet King!  Don’t let his age fool you – this stunning boy is still full of life with tons of energy and personality.  He’s a very active and boisterous boy who loves to be involved in everything going on around him.  He can get a little overexcited at times and does jump up, so he will need an owner who can continue working on his manners and help him channel his enthusiasm in a positive way.  One of his more unique traits is his communication style. He has a habit of making low growling or grumbling sounds, which can seem confusing at first.  His body language tells the real story.  He’s happy, friendly and just expressing himself in his own quirky way.  Understanding his signals will be key, and once you do, you will see what a character he truly is.  He already knows some basic commands and shows good potential to keep learning with consistent guidance. A definite favourite of his is grooming time – he absolutely loves the attention and will happily soak up every second of being brushed and pampered.  We are looking for an active and experienced adopter for King.  He will need plenty of exercise and mental stimulation each day.  He will need a private secure garden.

king has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel following a successful meet and greet could potentially live with another playful dog of similar size.

As King came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him and as his communication skills can be quite confusing we would like any children/visiting children in the home to be 16+ years.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Otis
description:
This handsome boy is Otis.  He is a sweetheart from head to tail.  He’s an incredibly friendly and affectionate boy who absolutely thrives on human attention.  His favourite place? curled up on your knee soaking up all the fuss and cuddles he can get.  Unlike may dogs, he’s not particularly interested in toys, he much prefers spending quality time with his people.  That said, he does have a playful side, especially when water is involved! He enjoys splashing about, although he can get a little carried away and does chase and chew the hose during kennel cleaning time.  Otis can get a bit giddy and boisterous when he is excited.  When it comes to other dogs, he can be nervous and unsure, so he would benefit from a home where he can feel safe and supported. A dog free home would suit him best, where his new owner can slowly build up his confidence around other dogs in a controlled environment with lots of positive reinforcement.  Otis would benefit from a private secure garden.

As Otis came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on him so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

If you have a holiday booked in the next 3 months please wait until you return to apply for a dog.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Rue
description:
Meet Rue!  Rue was a little timid when she first came into us but it didn’t take her long to come around and show us her crazy and fun loving side.  Rue absolutely loves playing with toys especially her plastic ring, she will chase around the compound with it all day if she could.  She is a real character and will come over for some fuss and then she’s off again with her ring. When she’s not zooming around the compound you will find her going on long walks exploring and having a sniff about.  She is strong on the lead so will need continued lead training.  We are looking for an active home for Rue that have previous experience with larger boisterous dogs.  Rue would benefit from a private secure garden so she can safely have off lead time with her toys. Continued training and socialisation is a must for this gorgeous girl.

Rue has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care and although is very playful with them she is very boisterous and has no boundaries so for this reason we think she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home and the adopter to continue her socialisation around other dogs so she can learn how to behave respectfully around them.

As Rue came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 14+ years.

*Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Tails
description:
This stunning girl is Tails.  We are looking for a committed and knowledgeable home for her where she can truly thrive.  As a typical Husky she is full of energy and very intelligent so will need an owner who understands the unique needs of this incredible breed. She is very high energy and an independent thinker with a typical Husky stubborn streak. She will need a home that has an active lifestyle where she can have plenty of exercise, enrichment and ongoing training.  Tails has so much potential and will make a loyal, fun and rewarding companion in the right home.  We are looking for someone patient, understanding and ready to invest time into building a strong bond with her.

Tails has socialised well with other dogs whilst in our care so can potentially be rehomed with another dog of a similar temperament – subject to a successful meet.

As Tails came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her, so we think it would be best for her to go to an adult only home.

Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Pearl
description:
This talkative girl is Pearl.  She is a very affectionate, confident and friendly girl that is always up for lots of fuss and strokes.  She can be very vocal when she wants your attention so probably not suitable for an apartment/flat.  She enjoys her walks everyday having a sniff about and saying hello to everyone she meets.  She is very strong on the lead so will need an experienced owner that is used to powerful dogs. Pearl would benefit from a private secure garden.

Pearl has socialised with other dogs whilst in our care, she can be a little unsure at first but does come around after a bit, so we feel she may be best suited as the only dog in the home where she can take her time getting used to other dogs with lots of positive reinforcement from her new adopter.

As Pearl came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children in the home to be 12+ years.

Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Mabel
description:
Mabel came in to our care as an ex racer, meaning she does have quite a high prey drive when out on her walks! She does walk beautifully until she sees a squirrel and then she’s off! She is a very chilled out girl in her kennel and does love to give you a scare when she’s under a blanket hiding!

This lovely girl is looking for a home where she can relax and enjoy her retirement years curled up in a bed or sprawled out on a couch! We do feel she could live in a home with another dog as she has interacted well with other dogs whilst being in our care. This would be subject to a successful meet. However shecannotlive in home with small animals such as cats, due to her high prey drive. We also feel she could potentially live in a home with a family where children are 12 years and older – subject to a successful meet.

Although Mabel does love being in her beds, she really does enjoy being out and about on her walks with her greyhound pals! So she would appreciate a home that would give her the best of both worlds – a lovely walk followed by snuggles with her favourite people! Please apply today to give her a forever home that she deserves so much!

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Shadow
description:
Shadow is a very cheeky boy has been super friendly to our staff and volunteers since he came in to our care. With him being an ex racer, he does have a high prey drive so can try to chase squirrels whilst out for his walks! He was paired in his kennel prior to her going home and will walk well with other greyhounds. He does seem unsure around other breeds of dogs, especially smaller breeds and will be quite vocal towards them. The staff and volunteers have been doing socialisation training with him here and he is improving slowly, his new family would need to continue this to help with improve further.

Shadow will have spent most of his life in kennels with his racing background, so he may feel a little nervous going in to a home environment. We also do not know if he is house trained or toilet trained due to this. He loves to lie in his comfy beds after his walks – we sometimes catch him upside down showing his belly off!

We think Shadow would prefer to be the only dog in the home, with him being a little unsure around other dogs at moment. However, he could potentially live with another greyhound if a meet and greet with him went well and depending on the other dogs temperament. He could also potentially live with children 12 years and older – subject to a successful meet.

Shadow would love a home where he could relax and enjoy his retirement years and rest on a comfy bed (or couch) of his own!

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Ginger
description:
When Ginger first came into our care she was very nervous, but with patience, kindness, and support from our staff, she has made lovely progress and is slowly learning that the world isn’t such a scary place after all. New people, places and noises can make her a little unsure so we feel she is in need of a quieter and less busy household with someone who is willing to take that extra time to get to know her and give her the time that she needs to feel safe. Ginger is especially nervous of men. Because of this, Ginger will need several meets with her potential adopters to help her feel comfortable and build a trusting bond.

Once she feels comfortable it’s like watching a completely different dog, her true personality really shines, she’s playful, super silly, full of energy and LOVES a kiss and a cuddle!Ginger is a real foodie and will do most things for a treat (especially a carrot!) she has also mastered the puppy eyes which she will use against you with every snack or meal you eat!She a really curious girl on her walks, she’s loves exploring what nature has to offer and stopping to sniff every new smell she can!

Ginger has interacted really well with other dogs whilst being with us and she loves to make new furry friends, however we do think because of her initial nervousness and her need for attention/reassurance she would benefit from being the only dog in a home so she can get all the love, cuddles and treats all to herself!!

Ginger has previously been in a foster home for 3 weeks, during this time we have found out that she is good with stairs, loves getting comfortable on the sofa and is pretty much toilet trained – with the odd couple of accidents. She likes to make her presence known and will bark when people knock on the door or she hears other dogs barking. She can be left alone for short periods of time without issues but does prefer people to be around. She flinches and gets scared of sudden movements or noises, we think this is due to her previous mistreatment. Gingers love language is definitely physical touch so she is after someone who loves cuddles! Ginger lived with a 14 year old during her foster stay and was really good with them. She is calm and gentle and loved the attention. Due to this we feel that Ginger would be able to be rehomed with children over 14 years old that are very dog savvy and have grown up around dogs.

With the right home, time, and understanding, Ginger will make a wonderfully loving and loyal companion. Could you be the one to give this special girl the patience and love she deserves?

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Willow
description:
This stunning girl is Willow.  Willow was very nervous and unsure for the first few weeks she was with us.  She was very vocal when the staff went in her kennel and would hide in the back.  The staff have worked hard with her to gain her trust and she has come out of her shell now and is showing her playful friendly side.  We are looking for an experienced owner for Willow that have had nervous large breed dogs previously and will work with her on her confidence, doing this at Willow’s pace and not too rushed.  She is a very independent girl who will come to you when she wants some attention, her toys are her number 1 priority in life.  Willow is very food orientated and will sit nicely for her treats.  This said she is a little bit chunky at the moment so is on weight management food to help her loose a few pounds, and well done to Willow and the staff as she has managed to lose 3kg up to now!  She will need continued training and socialisation. Willow will benefit from a private secure garden where she can have off lead time to relax and have a sniff about.

Willow has socialised ok with other dogs whilst in our care but we feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog in the home as she does prefer her own space.

As Willow came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer an adult only household.

Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Tula
description:
Meet Tula.  Tula can be a very scared and timid girl with new people.  The staff have spent lots of time with her to gain her trust and she has shown herself to be an affectionate and friendly girl.  She does still bark at some strangers so we are looking for an experienced owner that has the experience and knowledge to work with her on this and build her confidence with new people and new situations.   She loves having a run around our compound and going for long walks . Tula wouldn’t be suitable to live in a flat/apartment and will need a private secure garden for off lead time in between her walks.

Tula has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care so we feel she could be rehomed with another dog of similar size following a successful meet and greet.

As Tula came into the home as a stray we have no history on her and the fact she is so timid and unsure we would prefer her to be rehomed to an adult only household.

Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Bruce
description:
Bruce is a very active and playful boy who absolutely loves chasing around our compound with his toys.  He can be quite boisterous and bouncy when he is excited so would definitely benefit from continued training and socialisation. He can be quite a strong boy on his lead too.

He will need an active home where he can get all the exercise and mental stimulation he will need every day. Bruce would benefit from a private secure garden where he can have off lead time with his toys in between his walks.

Bruce has interacted well with most dogs whilst in our care but can be very dominant with them so we feel he would be best suited to be the only dog in the home.  The new adopter will need to continue his socialisation with other dogs to improve his interactions with them.

As Bruce can get nervous and overwhelmed meeting new people, especially men, we feel that it would be best for him to go to an adult only home. He will need time to get to know you before going home. His new family will need to be able to commit to multiple meets.

Bruce is a wonderful, vibrant boy with lots of love to give to his new family! He has spent a while in kennels now so would love to find his forever home and someone to be the person to throw his ball endlessly! Do you think this could be you?

*Applicants that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge

name: Clover
description:
This gorgeous girl is Clover!  We are looking for an experienced owner for her.  Once Clover has bonded with you she shows off her loving side, it just takes some time for her to trust new people. Once she has got to know you she doesn’t leave your side.  Clover is a very boisterous and bouncy girl who does forget just how big she is when she is jumping up at you to say hello. She would definitely benefit from puppy training classes.  She can be very silly at times but in the nicest possible way and will definitely keep you smiling.  Her new adopter must give her all the support she will need whilst she gets used to new people and places and will have to commit to regular meet and greets before the adoption could take place.  Clover will need a private secure garden.

Please only apply for this gorgeous girl if you have the experience and can commit to the training and socialisation she will need.

Clover has interacted well with other dogs whilst in our care but we do feel she would be best rehomed as the only dog so she can get 100% of the training and socialisation she will need.

As Clover came into the home as a stray we have no previous history on her so would prefer any children/visiting children to be 14+ years.

Applications that are successful will be provided with further information on each dog to the best of the charity’s knowledge.

name: Orchid
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a doggy friend in my new home. I haven’t met cats so I’m unsure.

I'd like a secure garden to potter around in.

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I am spayed and ready to find my forever home.

Orchid is a dinky little girl who has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. She is a very worried girl at the moment, who is finding everything quite overwhelming and clearly isn't used to much handling or socialisation. She isn't yet confident enough to approach us on her own and will often run away when unsure. She has now started to approach to accept a treat, so is already making encouraging progress. Despite her worries, she does get excited to see you and so with TLC we think she will thrive in the right home.

She has recently moved to a foster home where she is getting to experience home life for the first time and is settling in well. She has never lived in a home before, but has started to learn all about home life and is even starting to take herself out to the toilet. She sleeps soundly all night in a crate with one of her friend.

Orchid will be looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle in at her own pace and would benefit from adopters with experience of ex-breeding dogs. She could share her home with older, dog-savvy teenagers who can appreciate and understand that she will need a gentle and quiet introduction to the home, along with her own space to settle in.

She gets on wonderfully with other dogs and relies on their company for comfort, so she will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

Orchid doesn't yet walk on a lead and harness and will need to practice first around the home and garden until she is feeling brave enough to venture further. She is a gorgeous little girl who clearly loves the company of other dogs, and we feel she will truly thrive once she is settled into a loving home. With time, reassurance, and patience, she will blossom into a gentle and affectionate companion.

She is a little underweight at the moment, so we are trying to build her up gradually.

Orchid has been given the Gift of Life by Anne Breen

Orchid has now been in foster for a week and is an absolute delight but still frightened and does not allow you to stroke or touch her just yet. She will approach you for treats and as the weather begins to cool I will be encouraging her to accept gentle strokes. She adores her treats and will stand and bark when I'm preparing her food.

She visited the vet to have her second vaccination on Monday and she was quite distressed by this. She is a little underweight at the moment, so we are trying to build her up gradually.

Orchid sleeps in a crate with my other foster dog and happily settles for the night. So far she has been very good with house training and wanders into the garden when needing to toilet.

Overall Orchid is a quiet little girl and once she has progressed a little bit more she’ll make a wonderful addition to her forever home.

Orchid is an extremely sweet girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is on the more timid side and, although she is easy to handle, she is not yet confident enough to approach us on her own and will often run away when unsure. She is also not quite ready to take treats from us just yet. Despite her worries, she does get excited to see us and so with TLC we think she will soon start to come out of her shell.

Orchid gets on wonderfully with her kennel friends and relies on their company for comfort, so she will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

As Orchid has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, walking on a lead, and the other basics of home life. She will benefit from a calm and patient approach as she builds confidence at her own pace.

Orchid could share her home with older, dog-savvy teenagers who can appreciate and understand that she will need a gentle and quiet introduction to the home, along with her own space to settle in.

Orchid is a gorgeous little girl who clearly loves the company of other dogs, and we feel she will truly thrive once she is settled into a loving home. With time, reassurance, and patience, she will blossom into a gentle and affectionate companion.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Daisy
description:
I’d like a home filled with love and kindness!

I need a larger, male, doggy friend in my new home. I haven't met cats!

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

Please be patient with me, I need to learn house training and lead walking.

I had an entropion on both lower eyelids, but these have now been surgically repaired

02-06-26 Daisy is a scared Cocker Spaniel who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start.She is finding human interaction very difficult at the moment and will often move away if we get too close. Daisy is worried by handling, so she will need careful and gentle management while she learns that people can be kind. Having had very little life experience, much of the world is still new and overwhelming to her, and she will need patient adopters who are willing to let her progress at her own pace.

At the moment, Daisy is feeling a little less brave and is finding comfort in the company of her kennel friends. We feel it would be best for her to be re-homed with another large, male dog for support and companionship. Having a confident canine friend by her side will help her feel more secure as she settles into home life.

As Daisy has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. Her adopters will need to be understanding and prepared to guide her through these new experiences with patience and reassurance.

Daisy prefers to enjoy her meals on her own, so mealtimes are carefully managed here and should continue to be managed in the same way in her new home. Feeding her separately helps prevent any confusion over whose food belongs to whom and allows her to feel more relaxed. Treats should also be given thoughtfully and managed in the same way to ensure a positive experience for everyone.

Daisy could share her home with quiet, older dog-savvy teenagers aged 16 and over who can appreciate and understand that she will need a gentle, quiet introduction to the home and her own space to settle in.

Daisy has arrived with bilateral entropion of the lower eyelids, which have now been surgically repaired by our vets. She is also underweight, so we will be helping her reach and maintain a healthier weight during her stay with us. This will need to be continued in her new home to ensure she remains happy and healthy.

Although Daisy is finding life quite frightening right now, there is a lovely girl underneath those worries. With time, patience, and the support of a canine companion, we are confident that she will begin to blossom and discover that the world is not such a scary place after all.

Daisy has been given the Gift of Life by Lynn Carrivick

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Snowy
description:
I would like a home filled with love and kindness!

I need a laid back dog who is slightly larger than me in my new home.

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I am learning to walk on a lead and harness, but it's still very new for me.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have had 1 tooth extracted and am being treated for a ear infection.

Snowy is a very sweet little lady who has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. When she first arrived she was quite worried and overwhelmed, but was trying so hard to be brave! She is still feeling quite timid, but once she gets to know she really enjoys having a fuss and is happy to be handled. She is an inquisitive and loving girl and once she's in a home we don’t think it will take long to come out of her shell and show her true personality.

Snowy would love to find a calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs and where she can settle in at her own pace and realise what it means to be a loved family member. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy older teenagers.

She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs, so she will need at least one kind, calm and confident resident dog in her new home to help her feel safe and settled. We have found that she is best suited to living with laid back dogs who are slightly larger than her. She can become a little jealous when other dogs are receiving attention and is more laid back with dogs who are a little bigger than her.

As Snowy has never lived in a home before, her adopters will need to be patient and understanding while she learns about home life and house training. She has started to learn all about walking on a lead and harness and while this is still very for her, she is doing so well.

With her sweet nature and growing confidence, we are sure it won’t take her long to settle into family life and flourish in a loving home.

Snowy has had 1 tooth removed at the time of her spay and has prevously been treated for an ear infection.

Snowy has been given the Gift of Life by Raychel Mount

This week Snowy has met a few new friends and has been out for her first walk! She took it all in her stride. We were very impressed with how well she has done.

She is a timid lady but really enjoys having a fuss when she gets to know you. She would love a home where she has at least one confident resident dog to help her to settle in. She is currently sharing her kennel with a laid back Scottie dog and gets along well with her, although she ideally needs a friend who is larger than her, she can be quite shy if they are too much larger than her.

She really is a sweetheart who is just looking for her chance at a new and happy life.

Snowy is a wonderful lady who has come to us from a breeder. She is happy to be handled and will shyly approach for fuss and attention, showing that she is trying so hard to be brave and really wants to make friends. Snowy is an inquisitive and loving girl and once she's in a home we don’t think it will take long to come out of her shell and show her true personality.Snowy would love to find and calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs and where she can settle in at her own pace and realise what it means to be a loved family member. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy older teenagers.She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs, so she will need at least one kind, calm and confident resident dog in her new home to help her feel safe and settled. We have found that she is best suited to living with laid back dogs who are slightly larger than her. She can become a little jealous when other dogs are receiving attention and is more laid back with dogs who are a little bigger than her.As Snowy has never lived in a home before, her adopters will need to be patient and understanding while she learns about house training and walking on a lead. With her sweet nature and growing confidence, we are sure it won’t take her long to settle into family life and flourish in a loving home.Snowy has had 1 tooth removed at the time of her spay and is being treated for infected ears.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Lollipop
description:
I could live with older, dog-savvy teenagers who respect my space.

I need a doggy friend in my new home. I haven’t met cats so I’m unsure.

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

Please be patient with me, I need to learn house training and lead walking.

I have a grade 2 luxating patella and have had 7 teeth extracted. Read below for more

02-06-26 Lollipop is a lovely Bichon Frise who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is an inquisitive and funny little girl who is already showing glimpses of her playful personality. Lollipop will happily come over to lick our fingers before darting away again, as if she's still deciding whether people are quite as interesting as her kennel friends.

She definitely prefers the company of other dogs at the moment, but she is happy to follow their lead and takes great comfort from having them around. As Lollipop gets on wonderfully with her kennel friends, she will need a kind resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

As Lollipop has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. Her adopters will need to be patient and understanding as she adjusts to a whole new world and gains confidence in her surroundings.

Lollipop could share her home with older dog-savvy teenagers aged 16 and over who can appreciate and understand that she will need a gentle, quiet introduction to the home and her own space to settle in.

She has arrived with a Grade 2 luxating patella but her mobility appears unaffected and this may improve as she builds muscle tone. Lollipop has also arrived with severe periodontal disease and has had 7 teeth extracted.

Lollipop is an active and spritely little girl with a bright future ahead of her. With the support of a canine friend and a patient family, we hope her confidence will continue to grow and her charming personality will shine through even more.

Lollipop has been given the Gift of Life by Jodie Stanley

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Lily May
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I can live with a male dog in my new home, or be an only dog and am fine with cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I walk well on a lead and would enjoy one or two walks a day!

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have been spayed and am being treated for a ear infection.

Lily May has come to us from a breeder and is now hoping to find her own special forever home. When she first arrived, she was a very worried little girl who was feeling quite overwhelmed by her new surroundings. Its clear she hasn’t had much socialisation before, so she will need lots of love and TLC as she settles into her new life.

Lily May did move briefly to a foster home, where she began to find her paws and settle into home life. Unfortunately she didn't get on with one of the female resident dogs in her foster home so she has had to come back to the rescue where she has settled back in well. She has since shown that she can be picky with which female dogs she likes to share her space with and so for this reason she will now be looking for a home with a male resident dog as she much prefers the company of male dogs, or she can live as an only dog in a home where someone is around a lot of the time. She was in foster with a cat and so could possibly be homed with a dog savvy cat.

Lily May will be best suited to a calm and quiet home with adopters who have experience with scared ex-breeding dogs and who will help her settle in at her own pace. We would consider homing her with dog savvy teenagers.

She is still very timid, but is taking everything gently in her stride. She can already walk on a lead, so she would enjoy one or two walks around the local area when she is settled in.

She meets other dogs well on walks and is even getting comfortable greeting their owners as well, showing just how brave she is!

As she has had limited experience in a home, Lily May will need patient and understanding adopters to gently introduce her to everyday routines. She will need support with learning about house training, however, she was already starting to do really well with this in her foster home.

She remains sensitive to everyday noises and movements, but is beginning to cope better with gentle reassurance and support. Lily May does tolerate being handled, and this will continue to improve as her trust develops.

Lily May is a sweet little girl who simply needs patience, love and reassurance. With the right family by her side, she will continue to grow in confidence and become a devoted and loving companion.

She has been treated for an ear infection, and has followed up with the vets that have said it has cleared up now.

Lily May is growing in confidence every day. She will happily come over to say hello and really enjoys a gentle fuss and being handled. She's doing well with her harness training and is making great progress on her walks.

Lily May loves spending time with her kennel mummies, and one of the sweetest parts of her day is greeting them with a happy, wagging tail whenever they come into her kennel. It's been wonderful to watch her confidence blossom, and she's becoming more affectionate every day.

I have seen Lily May make wonderful progress during her time with us. She is an incredibly sweet-natured dog who has formed a lovely bond with her kennel companion, Coan, and clearly enjoys his company.

Lily can be dominant around other dogs at times and would be best suited to a home with a compatible male dog. She walks beautifully on both a lead and harness, making her a pleasure to take out and about. She also loves attention, affection, and treats, which makes building a bond with her very rewarding.

Overall, Lily May is an absolute superstar. She is a lovely dog with so much to offer and can be handled when needed, continuing to show what a wonderful companion she will make in the right home.

Lily May has arrived in her foster home and is beginning to find her paws as she settles into her new surroundings. She is currently a very timid little girl, but is taking things gently in her stride. She has already accepted a harness well, and we will now slowly begin introducing lead walking at her own pace, which we hope will help build her confidence and support her ongoing progress with sensitivity.

Lily May would be best suited to a calm and quiet household where she can continue to build her confidence without pressure. She will need to live with another kind and confident, male dog, who will help reassure her and show her the way as she adjusts to home life.

She is still sensitive to everyday noises and movements, but is learning to cope with gentle support and reassurance. Lily May does tolerate being handled, and this will continue to improve as her trust grows.

She is also currently living happily with a cat in her foster home.

Lily May has come to us from a breeder and is now hoping to find her own special forever home. Lily May is a very worried girl who is feeling rather overwhelmed at the moment. She clearly hasn't had much socialisation before and so will need lots of love and TLC as she settles in to her new life.

Lily May will be looking for a calm and quiet home with patient adopters who have experience of ex-breeding dogs and understand that she will need time to settle at her own pace. She will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home to be her friend and take her under their wing, but this dog would need to be male as she doesn't want to share her space with a female dog.

As she has never lived in a home before, Lily May will need kind and understanding adopters who can gently introduce her to everyday life. She will need support with learning all about house training and will need to practice walking on a lead in the safety of the garden before she is ready to explore further afield.

Lily May is a sweet little girl who simply needs patience, love and reassurance. With the right family by her side, she will grow in confidence and become a devoted and loving companion.

She is currently being treated for a ear infection.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.
