name: Nicole
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

Nicole has come to us from a breeder and is now searching for her forever home. When she first arrived, she was understandably feeling very scared and overwhelmed. It was clear she hadn’t had much handling or socialisation before, so human hands were quite worrying for her.

She has recently moved into a wonderful foster home where she is learning all about home life for the first time ever. She is settling in well and has a wonderfully inquisitive nature. She is still very worried about being handled and can bark when she's unsure, but she is making so much progress already. She will now take treats from her fosterer and absolutely loves her food.

Nicole will be looking for a calm and quiet adult only home where she can settle in at her own pace. She would really benefit from adopters who have experience with nervous ex-breeding dogs and who understand the patience, time and reassurance she will need as she learns to trust. She loves other dogs and takes great comfort from their presence, so Nicole will need another kind and confident resident dog in her new home to take her under their wing and help her feel safe and secure.

As Nicole had never lived in a home before moving to her foster home, she will need patient adopters who are ready to help her continue to learn everything about home life. She will need to learn new skills like house training and will also need to learn all about walking on a lead and harness.

With time, kindness and plenty of TLC, Nicole will begin to grow in confidence and let her personality shine.

Our vets have noted that Nicole has a slightly unusual heart rhythm, so she underwent a heart scan at our vets which showed some mild valve changes. At this stage she does not require any treatment and is expected to live a normal, happy life, although we would recommend routine heart checks and monitoring for any future symptoms.

Nicole has been given the Gift of Life by Shirley Bourne

Nicole has been in foster for a week and is a sweetie. She has a wonderfully inquisitive nature and loves investigating everything around her, which makes us chuckle! She is still very nervous about being handled and will bark when she's feeling unsure, but she is already making progress. She now takes treats from us and absolutely loves her food. She is also mostly clean overnight.

Nicole has come to us from a breeder and is now searching for her forever home. She is a lovely little girl who is understandably feeling very scared and overwhelmed to find herself here. It’s clear she hasn’t had much handling or socialisation before, so human hands are quite worrying for her at the moment, but we will continue gently helping her learn that hands can be kind.

Nicole will be looking for a calm and quiet adult only home where she can settle in at her own pace. She would really benefit from adopters who have experience with nervous ex-breeding dogs and who understand the patience, time and reassurance she will need as she learns to trust.

She loves other dogs and takes great comfort from their presence, so Nicole will need another kind and confident resident dog in her new home to take her under their wing and help her feel safe and secure.

As Nicole has never lived in a home before, she will need patient adopters who are ready to help her learn everything about home life from the very beginning. She will need to learn new skills like house training and will also need to learn all about walking on a lead and harness.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Olive
description:
I'd like a calm and quiet, adult only home.

I could be the only dog or live with a male resident dog. I can't live with cats.

I would like a secure garden that I can explore.

I walk well on a lead and would enjoy one or two walks a day!

I have lived in a home so I know the basics.

Olive originally came to us from Romania as a puppy with her siblings and was quickly adopted. Sadly, she was recently returned as she was finding life in a busy city and busy household too overwhelming.

She will be looking for a calm and quiet, adult-only home where she can settle at her own pace. We feel she would really benefit from adopters with experience of Romanian dogs. Since being back with us, Olive has shown us just how sociable she is with other dogs and now happily shares her kennel with a lovely male dog called Cooper. The two get along brilliantly and spend much of their time together, often alongside Olive's best friend, Buck. We now feel Olive would happily live with another friendly male dog, although she can also be homed as the only dog. She does occasionally show some guarding behaviours around food with other dogs, but this is easily managed by feeding separately or giving her plenty of space at mealtimes. She cannot share her home with cats or small furries.

Olive can struggle with busy environments and traffic, so we feel she would be much happier in a quieter area where she can enjoy more rural walks. She can also bark at loud noises and strangers visiting the home, so this is something her adopters will need to be willing to work on with her. Her adopters will need to be patient, calm and consistent, helping her gradually build confidence and feel secure in her new environment.

Olive is house trained but may need gentle reminders as she adjusts to a new routine. She walks nicely on the lead and would love an active home where she can enjoy plenty of walks and adventures. She is a clever girl who already knows basic commands such as "sit" and "paw". Olive is a very sweet girl who enjoys being close to her people, happily sitting beside you, though she isn't overly fond of cuddles.

When Olive first arrived back with us, she was quite nervous and wouldn't approach us, but she has grown enormously in confidence during her time here. She now eagerly greets her kennel mummy every morning and has become an absolute joy to care for. She loves going out for walks with our volunteers, which has really helped her confidence continue to blossom, and she absolutely adores chasing balls around our big play yard. She is often at the centre of our larger play groups, encouraging all of the other dogs to run around and play with her. Watching Olive run freely and enjoy herself is truly heartwarming.

After spending the last seven weeks getting to know Olive, we can honestly say she has been an absolute angel. She has settled beautifully into kennel life, but it breaks our hearts knowing she has experienced the comforts of a home and is now waiting for another family to find her. She deserves so much more than kennel life and has so much love to give.

With time, love and TLC, we know Olive will blossom into the most wonderful companion. She really will make one very lucky family complete, so please don't leave this special girl waiting any longer.

Olive has tested negative for Brucella Canis.

Olive has now been with us for 7 weeks, and what an absolute angel she has been, it’s a pleasure being able to care for Olive everyday but it makes me so sad knowing that she has lived in a home and is now waiting in kennels for her forever home to find her!

Olive is always at the centre of the big play group, encouraging all the other dogs to run around and play with her, it is so heart warming watching Olive run freely!

Olive does now also share a kennel with a lovely male dog called Cooper, and the two get along brilliantly. They spend much of their time together, along with Olive's best friend, Buck.

From getting to know Olive during her time with us, we are now happy that she  could happily  with another male dog. With a few gentle introductions, she builds lovely relationships with male dogs and enjoys their company. She also gets on well with female dogs, but if there is already a resident dog in the home, we feel Olive would be happiest sharing her space with a male companion.

Olive can show a little guarding behaviours around food time when it comes to other dogs, but as long as she is fed at distance she is absolutely fine!

Olive deserves so much more than kennel life, she will really make your life’s complete!

Olive has now been here a whole month and has sadly had little to no interest!

I’ve been working with Olive since she first arrived, at the beginning, she was a very nervous girl who wouldn’t really approach me, but over time she has grown so much in confidence. Now, every morning when I arrive, she’s waiting at the front of her kennel to greet me.

Olive also now enjoys going for walks with volunteers it’s really helping her confidence build! She also absolutely loves chasing a ball down our big yard!

Olive does socialise really well with other dogs here at the rescue, however we feel she would be happiest as the only dog in the home where she can have all the attention she deserves, but she could share her home with a calm male dog.

Olive will make the most perfect companion for someone, please don’t leave her waiting much longer.

Olive originally came to us from Romania as a puppy with her siblings and was quickly adopted. Sadly, she has recently been returned as she was finding life in a busy city and busy household too overwhelming.

She will be looking for a calm and quiet, adult only home where she can settle at her own pace. We feel she would really benefit from adopters with experience of Romanian dogs. Olive would be happy to be homed as the only dog but she could also live with a male resident dog, although she can resource guard her food from other dogs so her adopters would need to ensure they were fed separately. She cannot share her home with cats or small furries.

Olive can struggle with busy environments and traffic and so we feel she would be much happier in a quieter area, where she can go on more rural walks. She can also bark at loud noises and strangers visiting the home, so this is something her adopters will need to be willing to work on with her. Her adopters will need to be patient, calm, and consistent, helping her gradually build confidence and feel secure in her new environment.

Olive is house trained but she may need gentle reminders as she adjusts to a new routine. She walks nicely on the lead and would love an active home where she can enjoy plenty of walks and adventures.

Olive is a clever girl who already knows basic commands such as “sit” and “paw”. She is a very sweet girl who enjoys being close to her people, happily sitting beside you, though she isn’t overly fond of cuddles.

With time, love and TLC, we think she will blossom into a wonderful companion, so please don't leave her waiting too long.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Luca
description:
I’d like an active home & I can live with older dog savvy teens!

I’d like to be the only dog in the home. I can't live with cats or small furries!

I will need a large secure garden with minimum 6ft fencing.

I walk well on a lead and would enjoy an active lifestyle!

Please be patient with me, I haven’t lived in a home before.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home! I have had 1 tooth extracted.

29-05-26 Luca is an incredible boy who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a fresh start. He already knows his name very well, so his new adopters should keep this the same to avoid confusing him as he settles into his new life.

Luca was previously used as a fake security dog and has spent most of his life living outside, but beneath his big appearance he is a real softie. He is easy to handle and enjoys human company, though he has understandably found kennel life quite stressful and will often plead with us to come back when we have to leave him.

Luca would be happiest and thrive best as the only dog in the home, where he can enjoy all the attention, settle more easily into a routine, and build a strong bond with his new family. He will need adopters who have plenty of time to spend with him and who have experience of the breed or similar breeds.Luca cannot live with cats or small furries and he cannot be around small dogs.

As Luca has spent most of his life outdoors, he will need to learn about house training and the other basics of home life. He will need patient adopters who can help guide him through these new experiences as he adjusts to living in a home environment.

Luca can already walk on a lead, though he does pull at first and so this is something his adopters will need to work on with him. He is a very active boy, so he would love an active household where he can enjoy plenty of adventures and become a loyal companion once he is settled in. He is a strong dog and can both jump and open gates, so he will need a very secure garden and confident handlers who are able to manage his strength safely.

Luca is an intelligent, affectionate, and loyal boy who simply wants to be close to his people. In the right home, with structure, companionship, and plenty of love, he will make an incredibly rewarding companion.

Luca has had 1 tooth extracted at the time of his neuter.

Luca has been given the Gift of Life by Lynne Hughes

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

name: River
description:
I need an experienced, adult only home with no visiting children.

I would prefer to be an only dog at the moment. I can't live with cats.

I would like a secure garden in a quiet area.

I can walk well on a lead and would like an active home.

I need experienced adopters to work on my reactivity with me.

I am spayed and ready to find my forever home.

River originally came into our care in 2020 from a breeder and was adopted into a loving home. Sadly, her family later found they were unable to continue meeting her needs, and River has now returned to us.

River is a highly intelligent, affectionate girl who forms very strong bonds with her people. According to her previous home, once she feels safe she is extremely loving, enjoys close contact and seeks reassurance from those she trusts. She loves cuddles, gentle scratches, and being near her humans, and is very in tune with their emotions, often offering comfort when someone is feeling down.

River is a clever and enthusiastic learner who already knows a wide range of cues and enjoys training, enrichment and mental stimulation. She loves her toys, especially balls and tug toys, enjoys scent games such as “find it”, and is very food-motivated, peanut butter is her absolute favourite! She settles well at home, enjoys routine and structure, and is clean and non-destructive.

River enjoys her walks and would suit an active home with access to quieter walking routes. She walks nicely on a halti and enjoys being out and about, but can find busy environments overwhelming and may be reactive to dogs, vans or motorbikes when anxious. She will need confident handling and continued training, and should remain on lead unless in a secure area.

Although affectionate and loving, River is a sensitive dog and can struggle with unfamiliar people, visitors and loud or sudden noises such as fireworks. When frightened she may bark or retreat. For this reason, River is looking for acalm, quiet, adult-only homewithvery few visitors, ideally in arural or semi-rural setting. Her adopters must beexperienced with reactive dogs, patient, and willing to work at her pace while respecting her boundaries.

We are advised that River can occasionally display guarding behaviours towards her trusted humans, such as growling if another person enters the room while she is settled. This will need calm, confident and experienced management.

River is currently looking for a home as anonly dog, although she has previously lived with another small dog and may be able to live with a calm companion following careful introductions.

River may not be suited to everyone, but for the right home she will be an incredibly loyal, affectionate and rewarding companion who has so much love to give.

A bit about River from her previous home:

Quiver, Trevor, Clever Trevor, Trev, Piggy, Pig, Squirrel, Egg Nog Dog

Riverwill need more than one meet and greet with her prospective adopters to allow her time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Just wanted to share quick update for our wonderful River! Her kennel mum has been working on showing River that visiting the vets isn’t always a scary ordeal. Today, I popped in with her to see how it was going. I was SO impressed!

Although she was hesitant to come in, once in the vets she settled enough to have the biggest cuddles (River gives the BEST cuddles). We spent 5 minutes or so telling her how beautiful and amazing she is and made sure to head out before she got too worried.

River is honestly one of those special, once in a lifetime dogs. Once she bonds with you, she has this incredible ability to make you feel like the most important person in the world. She gives you her whole heart. Whoever adopts her will be so lucky to have her in their life!

Our gorgeous girl River is still on the search for a new home and family and hopefully her forever one. River is continuing to do very well here at the rescue and has progressed so much since being back with us.

Having lived in a home River knows a lot of commands and has been very well taught. She's clean in her kennel and isn't destructive at all. Although, she has lived in a home before she does still struggle at times with busy environments and people she doesn't know. When shes greeted by someone she doesn't know she will bark out of uncertainty but if you offer her a treat she tends to make friends with you pretty quickly. There were only a couple of people who could work with River initially when she came back to us due to her being so overwhelmed and worried but now half the rescue can work with her so shes made massive progress and I couldn't be any prouder of her.

She walks lovely on the lead and absolute loves going out for walks or down the big yard where she also loves to play fetch. We have been training River to understand that going to the vets isn't all that scary. We have been desensitising her by walking her up the vet steps and just sitting there for a moment praising her. Before, she wouldn't even go up the steps so for her to do this and sit there for a moment is massive for her. Our next step will be to open the vets door and go from there. River is such a special girl and when she trusts you she loves you with her whole heart and she genuinely does make you feel so loved. Being Rivers Kennel Mum is honestly the best and having all that love and affection from her is just utterly amazing.

River is continuing to do incredibly well here and is trusting more and more people she meets each day. It’s been lovely to see her confidence grow as she builds good relationships with other people. As part of her ongoing desensitisation, we’ve been walking River around the front of the rescue and gradually up towards the vets. This helps her learn that these environments are safe and nothing to worry about. Throughout this training we give her plenty of praise and tasty treats, and she is responding really well.

Considering everything she’s working through, she’s doing brilliantly. River is muzzle and Halti trained, as she can be reactive to certain dogs and can be strong when she first heads out on walks. We are working on her reactivity daily, and she is improving.

Her future adopters will need to continue with this training to help her keep progressing and feeling secure. River is an absolute darling. She has such a loving nature and truly makes you feel so loved when you spend time with her. With the right support and understanding, she would make a wonderful companion and a very special addition to the right home

River is a gentle girl who has previously lived in a home and is already kennel clean, giving her a great foundation for her next chapter. She can be a little cautious with new people at first, but with time and patience, she soon shows her sweet, affectionate side. Once she trusts you, River forms a lovely bond and just wants to be close to her people. She’s looking for a kind, understanding home that will give her the reassurance and love she deserves.

name: Rachel
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

i have never been on walks, but may be too scared and will need some time.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have had a pea size cyst removed from my right shoulder at the time of spay.

Rachel is a very scared girl who has come to us from a breeder. She is understandably overwhelmed by her new surroundings and clearly hasn’t had much socialisation before. However, since being with us, she has slowly started to show small signs of progress as she begins to realise she is safe. Although she still finds being handled very frightening, we are beginning to see small but meaningful improvements in her trust, and she is looking for a home with adopters experienced with traumatised ex-breeding dogs who can provide the reassurance and stability she needs to continue building her confidence and learning about trust and kindness.

She will need an adult-only home that can offer a calm and predictable environment where she can take things at her own pace. Rachel will also need at least one kind and confident resident dog to help guide her and provide comfort, as she can still become easily overwhelmed, but she takes great reassurance from the presence of other dogs and shares everything beautifully with her kennel companions.

Rachel has never lived in a home before, so she will need very patient and understanding adopters while she learns about house training, walking on a lead and accepting gentle handling. The big wide world still feels quite scary for her, but she is beginning to show curiosity in safe environments, and gentle time in the garden will be a great way to help her slowly explore and build confidence. She follows a daily routine beautifully, happily running out to the exercise yards and returning to her kennel when it's time to come back in. She also tries her best to keep her kennel clean, and we feel she is likely to pick up house training very quickly once she has regular access to a garden.

Rachel is only acting out of fear, but we are continuing to see glimpses of a softer side as she begins to relax in familiar surroundings. Although she still tries her best to avoid being touched and may attempt to bite when she feels frightened, her reactions are already less severe than when she first arrived. Recently, Rachel needed to have her thick coat clipped during the warm weather, which unfortunately had to be done under sedation as she is not yet ready to be groomed. Despite how frightening this experience must have been for her, she coped far better than we ever expected, allowing herself to be carried to the vets and held for her injection once she was safely in our arms. We couldn't have been prouder of how brave she was. As her coat grows back, we will continue gently introducing her to grooming so she can slowly learn that being brushed doesn't have to be scary.

Rachel has so much potential and, with time, patience and kindness, we truly believe she will continue to make progress and learn that she is safe and loved. She really is an easy dog to care for in every other aspect – she is wonderful with other dogs, thrives on routine and never puts a paw wrong. She just needs a truly understanding home where she can continue building on the trust she has started to develop and finally blossom into the dog she is meant to be.

Rachel came to us with a pea-sized cyst on her shoulder, which was removed at the time of her spay.

Rachelwill need more than one meet and greet with her prospective adopters to allow her time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Rachel has been given the Gift of Life by Tracy Davidson

Rachel’s progress continues slowly and steadily and even though she still doesn’t like to be touched, she is so much more content and calm in general. She’s now more than happy to hang out with me and lay down near me, and just this week she actually went to sleep next to me with her tummy exposed!!! I know that may not sound like a lot, but it’s HUGE for a dog like Rachel with such severe trust issues.

The past couple of weeks at the rescue have been extremely warm and Rachel started to struggle a little bit with having such thick fur, so I decided to get it shaved off. Rachel is still not ready to be groomed, so I did have to take her into the vets to be sedated. This is the first time I’ve had to actually pick her up since I started going the crate and touch work with her, but it had to be done. She does still try her very best to get away and she does still try to bite, but it is less severe than what it was when she first came in. Getting her into my arms was the most difficult part, but after I had hold of her I was able to walk over to the vets carrying her, and hold her while she had her injection without a fuss. I was so, so proud of her. She’s now a bit bald, but so much more comfortable and cooler. When her fur starts to grow back I am going to start introducing her to a brush so she can get used to it, as her coat does grow quite fast and it’s very dense which means it gets matted easily. It’s not ideal for a dog like Rachel needing to be groomed regularly, but I’ll try my very best to ease her into it.

Looking after Rachel on a daily basis is very easy. She follows routine beautifully - running to the play yards and back into her kennel when it’s time to come in. She tries her best to be clean with her toileting and I think if she had access to a garden regularly, she would pick it up really quickly. She also shares everything with her kennel friends perfectly and she never puts a paw wrong with other dogs. Aside from hating being touched, she really isn’t a problem at all. I really am hoping that she’ll start to get used to it soon, and I think she will … but being in a home would help massively. So I hope she doesn’t have to wait too much longer for that to happen.

For the past month, Rachel has officially been my “project dog”. This means I’ve been spending a lot more time working directly with her, and I set a goal for both of us when I started. Because Rachel’s biggest fear is human hands, that became my main focus — helping her feel more comfortable with being touched and gently stroked.

In the first week, I wanted to get her used to my touch without her panicking. When Rachel thinks she’s about to be handled, she’ll run around the kennel, jumping from bed to bed to avoid me, and will bite to protect herself if I get too close. I felt that all the running and panic was only reinforcing her fear, making things harder for both of us. So, I introduced a crate to safely contain her in a smaller space, allowing me to begin gentle handling in a more controlled way.

My hope was that she would start to realise that a soft fuss on her head or body isn’t actually that scary — that the anticipation was worse than the reality. Each day for that first week, I set up a crate at the back of her kennel and spent a short session gently touching her, just once a day to ease her into it.

On the first day, it took a little while for her to go into the crate, but she eventually went in with her friend. She was actually very good and allowed me to gently stroke her face for a few minutes, before becoming a little unsettled and starting to nibble my hand. By the second day, she went into the crate much more quickly and again allowed strokes around her head and under her ears. As I carefully moved further down her body, she became uncomfortable and nibbled a bit harder to let me know she’d had enough.

By the third day, she went straight into the crate, and I focused more on her body. She allowed longer strokes before becoming unsettled. The fourth day was very similar, but on the fifth day there was a real breakthrough — she let me stroke her tummy and chest without any nibbles at all.

After every session, I made sure to sit at the front of her kennel for a few minutes and give her treats so we always ended on a positive note (and to make sure she was still my friend!). Rachel is known to hold a grudge, so maintaining that trust was really important, and thankfully this work hasn’t damaged our relationship at all.

In week two, my goal was to remove the crate and see if she would choose to stay settled in a bed while I handled her. I wasn’t sure how it would go, but she absolutely amazed me. On the first day, she settled into a bed at the back of the kennel while I sat on the floor and gently shuffled towards her — and she stayed still, allowing me to stroke her. I kept things consistent over the next few days without pushing her too far.

By the fifth day, I introduced something new: scissors around her head. She needs a fringe trim, so I wanted to begin desensitising her to the sensation and presence of them. She did incredibly well — so well, in fact, that I managed to snip a tiny bit, which I hadn’t even planned to do. Because she was coping so calmly, I took the opportunity, and it paid off.

In week three, I continued trimming her fringe gradually, little by little. After a few days, I was able to clear the hair from around her eyes.

That’s where we are now. There’s still a long way to go, and Rachel will need plenty more time, patience and love, but I’m so pleased with how far she’s come. We’ve definitely made real progress, and I’m excited to keep building on it with her.

Rachel has been at the rescue for just over a month now and in that time she’s gained a lot of inner confidence, and has blossomed into a very cheeky and happy girl who always seems to be up to mischief!! She absolutely loves toys and gets very excited when I put them in her kennel every morning, and she also loves playing with them in the yards. She’s also very partial to starting ‘parties’ AKA getting all the dogs to have a massive game of chase with her, which she finds very amusing. Her favourite thing in the world though … food!!! She is one of the foodiest dogs I’ve ever met and is always at the front of the queue for breakfast, dinner, and treats.Even though Rachel has made progress in many areas, one thing she still simply can’t handle is being touched by us humans in any way. Her fear of our hands is so strong and she will try to bite to protect herself. This doesn’t mean she’s a nasty dog in any way, it’s just complete panic and fear. It’s something I’m trying to de-sensitise her to and I’m working with her as much as I can, but I do think it’s going to take a long time and a lot of consistency. We don’t know what she’s been through in her life that’s made her the way she is, so all we can do now is prove to her that humans can be kind, and hope that one day she’s able to put her past behind her.Rachel will need a very experienced home with people who will be extremely patient with her, understand her, and work with her through her worries and fears. She’ll need at least 1 resident dog to learn from, and absolutely no children. Things that are normal to most dogs such as going for a walk, being brushed/groomed, or even just having a little fuss, are alien to Rachel, so it’s important her adopters take all of this into consideration and be okay with it.

It may be a long and slow road to get Rachel to where she needs to be, but I promise it will be so worth it. There is no better feeling than a dog like her showing you love and trust for the first time!

Rachel is a very scared girl who has come to us from a breeder. She is a very scared girl at the moment who is quite overwhelmed to find herself here and clearly hasn't had much socialisation before. She is looking for a home with adopters with experience of scared ex-breeding dogs who can provide the reassurance and stability she needs to help build her confidence as she learns about safety, trust, and our intentions.

She will need an adult-only home that can offer a calm and predictable environment where she can take things at her own pace. Rachel will also need at least one kind and confident resident dog to help her settle in and take her under their wing, as she can be easily overwhelmed at the moment.

Rachel has never lived in a home before, so she will need patient and understanding adopters while she learns about house training and how to walk on a lead. Right now, the big wide world may feel too overwhelming for her, so gentle sniffs in the garden would be a good place to start.

Rachel is only acting out of fear, and we have not yet been able to see the girl she will become once she feels safe. She deserves so much love, she just isn’t sure how to accept it yet and will need a truly understanding home to help her blossom.

Rachel has come to us with a pea sized cyst on her shoulder that has been removed at the time of her spay.

name: Glitter
description:
I would like a home filled with love and kindness!

I’d like to be the only dog in the home. I haven’t met cats

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I'm new to lead walking and need some practice!

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have had 3 teeth extracted at the time of my spay.

Glitter has come to us from a breeder and is now searching for her forever home. She is the sweetest girl and it's so hard to believe that she's still waiting to find her forever home! She is a beautiful girl and her personality is even more beautiful. She’s the type of dog that lights up every room she walks into, and every staff member at the rescue loves her very much. You could say she’s a bit of a celebrity around here! She loves greeting everyone and saying hello to them and we think she is going to absolutely love being somones special girl. Glitter absolutely loves humans and is at her happiest when she’s sat on our laps having big snuggles and will even fall asleep with her little head resting gently on you.

Glitter shares her kennel here with other dogs, but, she can show some guarding behaviours when other dogs come over to have some affection too. Because of this, we think she’d prefer to live as an only dog so she wouldn’t need to share her humans and can have all the love and attention for herself. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy teenagers or older children.

She has never lived in a home before, so Glitter will need patient and understanding adopters who can help her learn all about home life and new skills such as house training and other day to day routines. She has started learning how to walk on a lead, but may need some practice in her new garden before venturing out into the big world! With kindness, reassurance and plenty of love, we feel she will soon start to settle in.

Glitter is a lovely girl who just needs a chance, and we can’t wait to see her flourish in a loving forever home.

She is a little overweight at the moment, but with a good diet and gentle exercise, she should start to shed the excess. She has had three teeth extracted.

Glitter has been given the Gift of Life by Carolina Olsen

I cannot believe Glitter is still waiting at the rescue for her forever home. I thought she would have been snapped up straight away!!!! Just look at her beautiful face. And her personality is even more beautiful. She’s the type of dog that lights up every room she walks into, and every staff member at the rescue loves her very much. You could say she’s a bit of a celebrity around here.. when I walk her around, everyone stops to say hello to her and she loves it!! She is such a little darling and she really is being wasted in kennels. I can only imagine how much she’s going to love home life.

Last week I took her into our on-site cabin which is like a home environment. She got up onto the sofa with me straight away and snuggled in so closely. She ended up falling asleep with her head rested on my lap and I felt so bad that I eventually had to wake her and put her back in her kennel. If you’re looking for a little best friend and a loving, loyal companion who will be the light of your life, please consider this precious girl. She deserves to be absolutely cherished because I can promise you that she will return the love ten fold. Please just give her a chance!

Glitter absolutely loves humans and is at her happiest when she’s sat on our laps having big snuggles.

She would stay there all day if she could! However, she does tend to show guarding behaviours when other dogs come over to have some affection too. Because of this, we think she’d prefer to live as an only dog so she wouldn’t need to share her humans.

She does walk on a lead but may need some practice in her new garden before venturing out into the big world!

Glitter has come to us from a breeder and is now searching for her forever home. She is a very sweet girl who is a little overwhelmed to find herself in new surroundings, but she is already enjoying a gentle fuss and absolutely loves a tummy tickle.Glitter loves other dogs and would be very happy to share her new home with another kind resident dog. In the right circumstances and with adopters who are around most of the time, we would consider homing her as an only dog.She would be best suited to a calm and quiet home, where she can get lots of love while she settles in at her own pace. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy teenagers or older children.She has never lived in a home before, so Glitter will need patient and understanding adopters who can help her learn all about home life and new skills such as house training as well as how to walk on a lead. With kindness, reassurance and plenty of love, we feel she will soon start to settle in.Glitter is a lovely girl who just needs a chance, and we can’t wait to see her flourish in a loving forever home.She is a little overweight at the moment, but with a good diet and gentle exercise, she should start to shed the excess. She has had three teeth extracted.

name: Coan
description:
I would like a home filled with love and kindness!

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I have met cats okay.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I walk well on a lead and will enjoy to go for walks with my new family.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I have had to have my left eye removed and only have a few teeth remaining.

Coan is a lovely boy who has come to us from a breeder to find his forever home. When he arrived, he was suffering with glaucoma and unfortunately did need to have his left eye removed, but he has recovered well and hasn’t let this slow him down. He has also had a dental and now has very few teeth remaining and has been treated for a ear infection.

Since arriving at Many Tears, Coan has come on leaps and bounds. He has taken everything in his stride and, now that he is feeling much better, he is more than ready to move on to the next stage of his life in a loving home. He has recently moved to a wonderful foster home where he is settling in well and learning all about home life for the very first time. He especially loves his food and eats with great enthusiasm and has affectionately been described as a greedy boy, so food will definitely be the way to his heart.

He is a very sweet little man who has grown in confidence and is now much more comfortable around people. While he can still be a little sensitive at times, he is enjoying gentle fuss and is showing just how lovely he truly is.

Coan will be looking for a calm and quiet home, where he can settle in at his own pace. He could be homed with older, dog-savvy teenagers who understand the importance of giving him space while he adjusts. He relies on other dogs for reassurance, so he will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in his new home to help him continue building confidence. He loves to play with the other dogs in his foster home and would love his new friends to want to play with him.

As he had never lived in a home before moving to foster, he will need kind and understanding adopters who will be patient as he adjusts to home life and starts learning all about house training. He is becoming increasingly confident wearing a harness and when he was at the rescue he would happily enjoy going for walks with both staff and volunteers as long as he had another dog by his side, which is wonderful progress.

Coan is a truly sweet older gentleman who has stolen everyones hearts during his time with us and is more than ready to find his perfect forever home.

Coan has been given the Gift of Life by Carol Unsworth

Coan has now been in his foster home for a week and has settled in well. He has shown himself to be a happy and sweet boy.

One thing is certain.. Coan absolutely loves his food! He eats with great enthusiasm and will happily check if anyone else has left any behind once he's finished, although he is always polite and never pushes other dogs away.

He has formed a lovely friendship with his fellow foster dog, Rooktail. Rooktail initially encouraged him to play, and now Coan is happily returning the favour. He clearly enjoys a good play session, complete with zoomies and a bit of rough-and-tumble fun.

Although he is generally a calm and easy going dog, Coan certainly doesn't behave like a typical 10 year old! He loves to scamper about, roll around and enjoy himself like a much younger dog.

While he can still be a little wary of new things, he isn't a scared dog. Instead, he's a sweet, happy chap who is growing in confidence every day, and it's been lovely to see his personality shining through.

Since arriving here at Many Tears, Coan has come on leaps and bounds, and not just because he looks like a lamb!

Coan has taken everything in his stride and now he is recovered from having a few teeth and an eye removed, he is so ready to move on to the next stage of his life which is a loving home.

Coan is very comfortable with people and even more so with dogs.

This older gentleman is becoming very confident on a harness and will happily walk out for volunteers as well as staff.

Until today, it was unknown how Coan would be around cats and after a short trip to the cattery, it became apparent he wants to be a friend to anyone or anything. He was so comfortable being with the lovely cat Gem and she enjoyed his company too!

After saying goodbye, he was really happy returning to his kennel, confirming that he would be happiest with a resident dog although this will be monitored as he is quite comfortable with people so in the right circumstances, he could go as an only dog in the future depending on his progress.

It's always sad to see dogs in kennels but it's even sadder to see an older dog in kennels, especially when they are as ready as Coan is to be in a family.

Coan is a lovely boy who has come to us from a breeder to find his forever home. When he arrived, he was suffering with a glaucoma and unfortunately did need to have his left eye removed, but he is recovering well and hasn't let this slow him down. He has also had a dental and doesn't have very many teeth remaining.

He is a very sweet little man, but he is still a little nervous around people and will need some time and patience to build his confidence. He isn't yet used to being handled, so will need calm, understanding adopters who are happy to let him go at his own pace and not rush him. With time, kindness, and reassurance, he will begin to feel safe and show his true, affectionate nature.

Coan relies heavily on his kennel friends for confidence, so he will need at least one other kind and patient resident dog in his new home to help him settle in and learn that the world isn’t so scary.

As Coan has never lived in a home before, he will need support with all the basics, including house training and learning to walk on a lead. His new family should be prepared to gently guide him through these new experiences.

He could be homed with older, dog-savvy teenagers who understand the importance of giving him space while he adjusts.

Coan is a truly sweet boy, he just needs a patient and loving home where he can take his time, grow in confidence, and feel safe.

name: Merv
description:
I’d like a home filled with love and kindness!

I can be an only dog or live with other dogs. I have never met cats.

I'd like a secure garden to potter around in.

I walk well on a lead and will enjoy one or two walks a day!

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home!

29-06-26 Merv is a cheerful boy who has come into our care from a breeder and is now looking for a loving home where he can enjoy the life he deserves.

Merv is a real character with a wonderfully happy-go-lucky personality. He loves meeting people and is always pleased to make new friends. He has settled in brilliantly with his female kennel companions and enjoys their company, while also showing what a gentle and easy-going boy he is. Merv is a clever dog who already walks nicely on a lead, although convincing him to stand still long enough for a photograph is another story!

Merv gets on wonderfully with other dogs, so he would be happy to share his home with another friendly dog. However, he is confident enough that he could also be the only dog in the home, provided his adopters are around for much of the day to keep him company and include him in family life. We would also consider homing him with older dog savvy children.

As Merv has never lived in a home before, he will need to learn all about house training and the other basics of living in a home. With patience, consistency, and positive reinforcement, we hope he will settle into his new routine quickly.

Merv can already walk on a lead, so once he has settled into his new home, he will enjoy exploring the local area on daily walks.

Merv is certainly a foodie, and his love of snacks is reflected in his figure! His new family will need to help him maintain a healthy weight with sensible portion sizes, plenty of exercise, and perhaps a few less treats than he would choose for himself.

Whoever adopts Merv will be gaining a truly lovely companion with a fantastic personality. Just one word of advice... make sure you keep your food safely out of reach, because if there's a snack to be found, Merv will almost certainly find it first!

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Pushkin
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult-only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I will be spayed before finding my forever home.

18-06-26 Pushkin has come to us from a breeder and is now searching for a loving forever home. She is initially quite nervous and unsure, but once you spend some time with her she starts to come out of her shell and even begins to enjoy a fuss.

Pushkin is a sweet girl who simply needs understanding and the chance to build her confidence at her own pace. She will be looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle in gradually and continue to blossom. She takes a great deal of comfort from the company of other dogs and becomes quite worried and stressed when left on her own. Because of this, she will need at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home to take her under their wing and show her that the world isn't such a frightening place.

Pushkin has never experienced life in a home before, so her new family will need to be kind, patient and understanding as she learns all about home life and new skills like house training. She will also need to learn all about walking on a lead and harness. She will need an adult-only home that has ex-breeding experience and can allow her to settle in at her own pace. She will need a garden fence of at least 6ft to ensue her safety while she settles into her home.

With plenty of love, reassurance and the support of a canine friend, Pushkin will continue to grow in confidence and become a wonderful companion. She is a lovely girl with so much potential, and we can't wait to see her enjoying the happy life she truly deserves.

Pushkin has been given the Gift of Life by Cherry Lewis

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Holly
description:
I would like a home filled with love and kindness!

I'd like to live with my best friend Heelie. I haven’t met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I can walk well but would prefer slower, shorter strolls.

I have lived in a home so I know the basics.

HOLLY WOULD LIKE A HOME WITH HER BEST FRIENDHEELIE

06-06-26 Holly is a fabulous lady who has come to us from a home with her best friendHeeliethrough no fault of their own. Sadly, their owner became too unwell to care for them, and their family made the very difficult decision to help them find a new home.

Holly is a very happy and expressive little girl who has clearly been much loved throughout her life. She is friendly, enjoys being around people, and is happy to be handled. She will also happily take treats from our hands and has settled into rescue life with her cheerful attitude intact.

She has arrived with her best friend, Heelie, who was originally adopted from us several years ago, and the pair are very much bonded. Holly is the braver of the pair, but they take comfort from one anothers company and will be looking for a home together where they can continue to enjoy life side by side.

As Holly has come to us from a home, she will be familiar with the sights and sounds of a home environment. However, she may need a gentle reminder of her house-training skills as she settles into a new routine and environment. She can live with dog-savvy older children who are sensible and gentle.

Holly can already walk on a lead but would prefer slower, shorter strolls. A slower pace of life would be ideal for Heelie and Holly, where they can enjoy plenty of comfort, companionship, and relaxed days together.

With her happy-go-lucky personality, affectionate nature, and love of both people and her best friend, Holly is a delightful little dog who is ready to begin the next chapter of her life with Heelie by her side.

Holly has arrived slightly overweight, so she will need a good diet and gentle exercise to help her reach and maintain a healthy weight.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

name: Nyssa
description:
I would like a calm and quiet home where I can settle in.

I need a doggy friend in my new home. I haven’t met cats so I’m unsure.

I'd like a secure garden to potter around in.

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I have some hormonal alopecia, but this should grow back with time.

Nyssa is a gentle little soul who came into our care from a breeder in search of a fresh start. When she first arrived, she was finding the world quite overwhelming and needed plenty of time, patience, and understanding to help her feel safe. After spending a few weeks with us, Nyssa has already made such lovely progress and has really started to come out of her shell. She’s no longer the shy and worried girl we first met, but instead a curious and comical little lady who has absolutely stolen our hearts.

She has recently moved to a wonderful foster home where she is learning all about home life for the first time ever. She is settling in well and is learning all about toilet training and doing well with this. She has discovered the garden and absolutely adores spending time pottering around it, she has also discovered a love for toys!

Nyssa is still building confidence with being picked up and handled, but she is happy to take a treat from you and accept a gentle fuss. She recently visited the grooming room for a little trim due to the warm weather, and she was an absolute dream throughout, standing beautifully even for the fiddly bits around her eyes.

Nyssa gets on wonderfully with every dog she has met at the rescue and really enjoys being in their company, so she will need to be homed with a kind and confident resident dog who can continue to guide and reassure her as she settles into home life.

She doesn't yet walk on a lead and harness and this is something she will need to practice around the home and garden until she gains confidence to venture further.

Nyssa arrived with some hormonal alopecia, which should continue to improve now that she has been spayed. She also has a slightly undershot jaw, but this doesn’t cause her any problems at all.

Nyssa is a truly darling little girl who just needs a loving home willing to continue the wonderful progress she has already made. Whoever welcomes her into their family is going to gain a very special companion.

Nyssa has been given the Gift of Life by Nicola Harper-Gibbons

Nyssa has been in foster with us for a week and has settled in really well. She is still a little wary of being touched but is happy to take treats and is beginning to enjoy a gentle fuss. She follows our other foster dog Nicole everywhere, so she would really benefit from having a kind, confident resident dog to help show her the ropes in her new home. She is mostly clean overnight and absolutely adores spending time in the garden.

Nyssa has been at the rescue for a few weeks now and in this short space of time she has really come out of her shell. She’s no longer a shy or worried girl, but instead a curious and comical little lady who has absolutely stolen my heart!!

She’s discovered toys and LOVES them, as well as yummy treats, and gentle fusses under her ears too. If she can make this amount of progress in just a couple of weeks here at the rescue, I can only imagine how well she’s going to do in a loving home environment.

She’s still not sure about being picked up or handled, but she’s already getting more used to it and will now plonk herself in one of the beds to be picked up. I took her into the grooming groom yesterday to take her coat a little bit shorter due to the weather being so warm, and she was an absolute DREAM .. perfection actually! She stayed still the whole time - even when I was shaving around by her eyes. 10/10 for her!!

Nyssa has so far been perfect with every dog she’s met at the rescue, and she really enjoys being in their company. Don’t keep her waiting for too much longer because she’s such a darling.

Nyssa is a very worried girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is finding things a little overwhelming at the moment and will need time, patience, and understanding to help her come out of her shell. She isn't feeling brave enough to approach you just yet, but she will let you gently stroke her.

She will be looking for a calm and quiet home with adopters who will let her settle in at her own pace. We would consider homing her with gentle, dog savvy teenagers.

Nyssa gets on wonderfully with her kennel friends and relies on their company for comfort, so she will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

As Nyssa has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, walking on a lead and harness and the other basics of home life. A calm and consistent approach will help her feel safe and gradually build her confidence.

Nyssa has arrived with some hormonal alopecia, which should grow back now that she has been spayed. She also has a slightly undershot jaw, but this doesn't cause her any issues.

Nyssa is a gentle little soul who just needs time and kindness. With the right support, she will slowly begin to feel safe and show the lovely companion she has the potential to become.

name: Sally
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a larger, resident male dog to help me settle in and I have met cats fine.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I am learning to walk on a lead and harness, but it's still very new for me.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

Sweet little Sally has come to us from a breeder to begin her search for a loving forever home. When she first arrived, she was understandably feeling a little worried to find herself here, but she has been trying so hard to be brave and has really started to come out of her shell.

Sally is growing into the funniest and cutest little dog, and for such a small girl she certainly has a big personality that is beginning to shine through. Her confidence continues to grow, and she now happily enjoys receiving fuss and attention from people she trusts. Sally has even started her harness training and is although it's still very new to her, she is enjoying going on walks and exploring.

Sally is a super little dog who is just waiting for someone to give her the time, patience, and love she needs to continue building her confidence. With the right home and lots of kindness, we are sure she will blossom into a wonderful companion.

She will be looking for a calm, kind and understanding home with adopters who are around most of the time and who will allow her to settle in at her own pace. She has never lived in a home before, so everything in a home environment will be new to Sally, so she will need gentle guidance as she continues learning about everyday life and learns new skills such as house training.

Sally absolutely loves other dogs and gains a lot of comfort from having canine friends around her. She will need another dog in her new home to help her feel confident, but she would prefer the company of a smaller male dog. Sally can be a little bossy with female dogs, so she is best suited to living with a male companion. She has met a cat at the rescue fine (please see the video in her gallery!) and so maybe possibly be able to be homed with a dog savvy cat.

This sweet girl just needs someone to give her the chance she deserves, and we are sure she will repay your love tenfold.

Sally has a lick granuloma on her left front leg, which is healing, and an old scar on her left hip. She has had an umbilical hernia repair at the time of her spay and is being treated for bilateral conjunctivitis.

Sally has been given the Gift of Life by Carolina Olsen

Sally is just the growing into the funniest, cutest of little dogs. For a small being she has a big personality and it’s really starting to shine through. She grows ever confident, and happily takes fusses from me and today successfully begun harness training.

Sally is a super little dog just waiting for a loving home to help continue bringing her out of her shell. Sally will need a friend in her new home but prefers the company of smaller male dogs, she can be a bit bossy with female dogs and is best suited to an adult only home.

Sweet little Sally has come to us from a breeder to begin her search for a loving forever home. She is a very gentle girl who is understandably feeling a little worried to find herself here, but she is trying so hard to be brave.

Sally will gently approach you for a little sniff and is already showing what a sweet nature she has, she just needs time, patience and reassurance to help her confidence grow. With the right home and lots of kindness, we are sure she will blossom into a lovely companion.

She will be looking for a calm, kind and understanding home with adopters who are around most of the time and who will allow her to settle in at her own pace. Everything in a home environment will be completely new to Sally, so she will need gentle guidance as she learns about everyday life and new skills such as house training and how to walk on a lead.

Sally absolutely loves other dogs and gains a lot of comfort from them, so she will need at least one other kind and gentle resident dog in her new home to be her friend and show her the way. Sally will need a larger, male dog in her home and cannot live with females or smaller dogs.

This sweet girl just needs someone to give her the chance she deserves and she will repay your love tenfold.

Sally has a lick granuloma on her left front leg, which is healing and an old scar on her left hip. She has had an umbilical hernia repair at the time of her spay and is being treated for bilateral conjunctivitis.

name: Gracie
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a doggy friend in my new home. I haven’t met cats so I’m unsure.

I'd like a secure garden to potter around in.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

Please be patient with me, I need to learn house training and lead walking.

I have been spayed and had an inguinal hernia repair on both sides.

Gracie is a sweet but scared girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She can be quite worried at the moment and will often retreat to her bed when someone enters the kennel. She is not yet comfortable around people and will need time and patience to help her settle and feel safe. Once she begins to relax, she will accept gentle fuss and will now give her kennel mum a gentle sniff of her hands.

She will be looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle in at her own pace and would benefit from adopters who have experience with nervous ex-breeding dogs.

Gracie prefers to stay close to her kennel friends and will often hide behind them for reassurance. Because of this, she will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

As Gracie has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. A calm and consistent approach will help her gradually build confidence.

Gracie has had inguinal hernias repaired on both sides.

Gracie is a gentle and sensitive little girl who just needs time, understanding, and the support of another dog. With patience and kindness, she will begin to feel safe and grow into a loving companion.

Gracie has been given the Gift of Life by Kelly Smallwood

Gracie has been making progress since being here, it’s a slow process but a process nonetheless and I am super proud of her.

In her kennel she has two friends, Grape and Kiki. Kiki is a bit more confident pointer and I think through seeing Kiki happily come to me for fusses and greet me at the front of the kennel it has made Gracie realise that I’m a safe person.

Although she still isn’t coming up to me on her own, she has started coming to the front of the kennel when I’m standing just outside it, I show her my hand and she gives it a little sniff, which might not sound like a lot, but to me it’s a big deal it shows she wants to say hello and it shows her confidence is growing.

Gracie is beautiful soul, and I know in time she will blossom into a more confident lady, it may take time but she’ll be worth the wait.

Gracie is a sweet but shy girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She can be quite worried at the moment and will often retreat to her bed when someone enters the kennel. She is not yet comfortable around people and will need time and patience to help her settle and feel safe. Once she begins to relax, she will accept gentle fuss.

Gracie has arrived with an inguinal hernia, which has been repaired at the time of her spay.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Trixie
description:
I'll need an adult only home with adopters with experience of ex-breeding dogs.

I am looking for a home where I can be the only dog & get all the love and attention.

I need an extra secure garden and home that can accommodate my needs.

I am still learning to walk on a lead, and will need my adopters to be extra secure.

I am clean in my foster home, but may need some reminders in my new home!

I have been spayed and am ready for my forever home! Read below for more info!

Trixie is a very sweet lady who has come to us from a breeder in search of a new start. Although her tail is constantly wagging, she will shyly approach for a gentle fuss and tends to stay away and watch from a distance until she feels safe. Once she builds trust, she is affectionate and will enjoy a snuggle and a gentle fuss.

In foster, she has shown herself to be a nervous girl in new environments who still needs encouragement to engage with new people. She will often hang back and observe, but once more secure she will come forward for interaction and enjoys fuss and attention from her fosterer.

Despite the fact that she is still a nervous girl, she also isn't keen on sharing her home with other dogs and so we feel she would be happiest as an only dog in her new home. Although she is in foster with other dogs, she can be jealous when her fosterer is showing attention to other dogs and has reacted towards them on occasion. She is fine with socialising with other calm dogs, but can be reactive towards barking or yappy dogs ordogs that are in her face too much or too boisterous.For this reason, we feel she would be best suited to an adult only home with adopters who have experience of nervous ex-breeding dogs. She has met cats fine, but due to her tendency to become jealous, we would not look to home her with cats.

In the garden, she has shown signs of digging and has previously tried to escape a foster home, so very secure boundaries are essential. Her new home will need to have 6ft minimum fencing height and her new adopters will need to keep her on lead in the garden until she gains confidence outside. She might enjoy having a sand pit to play in so she can enjoy digging in appropriate areas.

Trixie has been mostly clean in the house in her foster home, and seems to be star at toilet training. However, she may need some reminders in her new home as she settles in new surroundings.

She is beginning to walk on a harness but can still become startled and may try to back out if frightened. She needs experienced adopters, a secure garden (6ft+ fencing) as she has been digging in her foster garden, and additional safety measures indoors.

She will need a calm, secure, adult-only home and a gentle introduction to home life.

She has had a small lump removed from her left mammary gland and is recovering well.

Trixie has been given the Gift of Life by Tanya Player

Trixie truly is a gem of a girl with a heart of gold. A special and sweet lady, who longs for safe hands (and laps) to snuggle into. This super dog needs a little time and a lot of security,  give her that and you will have a bond for life.

Trixie is looking for a home that is quiet, gentle, patient and super secure on a practical level. Trixie is incredibly spooky and even if she trusts you, the slightest of frights in her environment and she will bolt. Because Trixie is a high flight risk she would be suited to a dog experienced home, ideally with knowledge of flighty and/or ex-breeding dogs.

It’s important that her new people have a secure plan for getting her out of the car on arrival home after adoption and are able to keep entrance and exits to the house escape safe. In return for your kindness Trixie will be the cutest of best friends. Trixie is looking to be the only dog and is suited to an adult home with no visiting children.

Trixie has been in foster with me for three weeks now and is settling into our routine fairly well.

When she first arrived, Trixie was very human-oriented and showed signs of jealousy towards other dogs, which led to a few altercations. She would often growl if another dog came close to me, as she wanted all the attention for herself. This is something we’ve been working on, but we still feel it is best if she is homed as an only dog in her new home.

From her interactions with my resident dogs, it’s clear that Trixie isn’t comfortable with small, yappy dogs. She does seem to prefer female human company, although she is continuing to build confidence more generally. She currently sleeps downstairs in an open, covered crate, which she uses as her safe space.

For the first couple of weeks, Trixie has slept downstairs quietly through the night with no accidents. Over the last few nights, she has been a little more unsettled and has had a couple of accidents, which seems to be linked to wanting more human attention.

Trixie is fed on dry Arden Grange and is currently fed separately, as she will try to eat other dogs’ food if given the chance. She was also caught eating poo, so I have started soaking her food in hot water and letting it cool, which she seems to prefer.

In her first week, Trixie did try digging in the garden and looking for ways to escape, but this behaviour has now settled. She clearly recognises me and gets very excited when she sees me, running over happily for fuss and attention.

She has been out on walks and does reasonably well when accompanied by another dog, but she is still quite flighty and easily startled. For now, she should only be walked in secure areas, as she will try to back out of her harness if she feels unsure and has demonstrated this once already.

Trixie can be complex at times, so she will need a calm, experienced adopter who understands nervous, ex-breeding dogs and can handle her with patience and care. It will be important to give her time to settle at home and build trust before introducing too many new experiences, especially.

Trixie absolutely craves human attention and is happiest curled up next to me on the sofa. As her confidence continues to grow and her jealousy is managed, she has all the makings of a very loyal and loving companion who simply wants to feel safe and adored.

Trixie is now in foster and is settling in well. She travelled well and was clean on arrival with no issues noted in the car or on entering the home.

Once in the foster home, Trixie has shown herself to be a very nervous little girl who is still unsure of new environments and needs encouragement to engage with people. She will often hang back and watch, but once she feels a bit more secure she will come forward and seek interaction. On these occasions she has chosen to snuggle close to me and will sit gazing and enjoying contact, although she does need to be gently encouraged each time as confidence doesn’t yet seem consistent.

It has quickly become apparent that Trixie is quite a complex girl in the home. She is very focused on attention and has already shown signs of jealousy when sharing human interaction. On one occasion, when another foster dog came onto the sofa, Trixie reacted strongly and became defensive. She can also be particular about other dogs in general and can be especially reactive to barking dogs, and has shown a clear dislike of a small dachshund in the home. For this reason, she will now be looking for a home as an only dog where she can get all of the attention for herself.

Outside of this, she is an active little girl and has already shown she is a determined digger, having previously managed to dig under fencing and patio areas. This will need careful management going forward with very secure boundaries required.

Overall, Trixie is settling in but is still very much finding her feet.

Trixie is a very happy lady who has come to us from a breeder in search of a new start. Although her tail is constantly wagging, she will shyly approach for a gentle fuss and doesn’t take long to build her confidence once she begins to feel safe. She is an absolute sweetheart who loves people and enjoys snuggling up close once she has settled, showing just how affectionate she truly is.Although Trixie gets on well with other dogs, she does show some signs of jealousy and so for this reason she will be looking for a home where she can be the only dog.As Trixie has never lived in a home before, she will need patient adopters who can help her learn about house training, lead walking, and all the everyday basics of home life. She is making lovely progress, but the world is still very new to her and she can be a little sensitive at times. Her adopters must need to understand ex-breeders, and that they have never truly experienced all of what a home has to offer, and that this can be very overwhelming for them.Although she is doing well and beginning to walk on a harness, Trixie can still be easily startled and may become flighty if she is spooked. She can try to back out of her harness when frightened, so it is important that her adopters have experience with nervous dogs and understand how to keep her safe. She is currently walked on both a harness and a slip lead for added security, and she will need a fully secure garden. For her safety, only adults in the home should walk her on lead.Her home will need to be extra secure, with a 6ft+ fence as well as security inside of the home that can prevent her from spooking and attempting to escape. She is a lovely and amazing lady, that is just nervous of her new surroundings, and needs someone to keep her safe, and provide her with love and security. In return, she will give you plenty of cuddles and loyalty.She has had a small lump removed from her last left mammary gland and is recovering well.
