name: Gabriel
description:
I need an adult-only experienced home that understands me.

I can be an only dog or live with other dogs. I cannot live with cats.

I may be shy now, but once I have settled I would love a nice garden I can play in!

I love going for walks, and would love an active home!

I know some tricks & walk well on the lead but will need help with housetraining.

I have been neutered and am ready to find my forever home.

Gabriel originally came to us from a breeder, and for him the world felt like a very frightening place. He was worried of people, the new environments, and unfamiliar experiences left him feeling overwhelmed, and even something as simple as having a lead put on was a huge challenge. He needed time, patience, and gentle encouragement to begin to feel safe.

Gabriel was adopted for a short period, but unfortunately it was not the right fit for him, and he was returned after displaying unwanted behaviours in the home and unfortunately making the wrong choices when he felt uncomfortable in a situation, which did result in Gabriel biting. He will need a quiet adult-only home without resident or visiting children to ensure he can get the structure he is needing. Gabriel can struggle with visitors coming to the house so will need a home without too many visitors and the ability to put him in a different room when people do some over. He will need adopters who are willing to meet him multiple times to develop a bond with him!

Gabriel requires a highly structured, boundary-focused home with experienced owners who are confident, calm, and consistent. He is not a dog who should be allowed on the sofa or cuddling up with toys and we would not want him to be set up for failure. Instead, he needs clear rules, his own space, and guidance that helps him feel secure and understand what is expected of him. His adopters will need to understand that he has potential to guard high value items or sleeping spaces and will need to work with him. Respecting his boundaries is essential to his wellbeing and success.

He will need both physical and mental stimulation every single day, whatever the weather. Regular walks, enrichment, and brain games are a must to keep his active mind engaged and to help him remain balanced and settled.

Despite his difficult start, Gabriel has made remarkable progress. These days, he can’t wait for his walks and becomes visibly excited when he sees his lead come out. He now allows his harness to be put on and walks beautifully, thoroughly enjoying his outdoor adventures.

Gabriel is a very clever boy and a true foodie, which makes training both rewarding and enjoyable. He already knows cues such as “sit” and “paw” and loves learning new skills. He thrives with clear guidance, consistency, and ongoing training, and will do best with an experienced owner who enjoys working with dogs and building a strong, structured relationship.

Gabriel forms very strong bonds with his people and is deeply loyal once trust is established. Because of this, he has shown signs of separation anxiety and will need adopters who are home for the majority of the time. He is not suited to being left alone for long periods and will thrive best with someone who can make him a central part of their daily routine. While he is not a dog who seeks constant physical closeness, once a bond is formed he does enjoy affection and gentle fuss in his own way.

He could live as the only dog, enjoying one-to-one attention, or potentially alongside a calm, confident resident dog who respects his space. He will need a home with no cats or small furries.

Gabriel has worked incredibly hard to overcome his fears and has already come so far. He is now searching for a forever home that will offer him understanding, structure, commitment, and the chance to live a fulfilled life. For the right, experienced person, Gabriel will be a loyal and rewarding companion.

Gabrielwill need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

This week we’ve had a heatwave which has meant alot of our rescue dogs have needed their ears checked incase they’ve had any mucky build up - Gabriel was one of the unlucky few that have needed some TLC in this area!!We’ve recently had a new meds person who has been visiting Gabriel to do his ear cleans & he has been an absolute angel. He’s even been given the nickname Angel GabrielIt’s just a testament to Gabriel that yes he can be unsure of new people, but once he knows you he truly is such a wonderful boy. He just needs someone to take that chance on him, show him the love he’s desperate to have & he’ll return it tenfold.Gabriel has been waiting such a long time now, and seeing a boy so full of love & hope is heartbreaking when we can’t reassure him that his time will come soon!!!

Today, I worked with Gabriel again for the first time in months. I was a little worried he’d have forgotten, however, it’s safe to say the bond previously made still remains.Gabriel is such an excited little boy. He lives for treats. He’s such a clever and obedient boy when the treats come out. Usually, he’s full of energy and excitement. Once the treats are out, he’s still and sits like the perfect little boy we know he can be.As he can be very busy, I’ve always thought it essential to take him out on walks, the longer the better. As mentioned in a previous update, he loves walks! Once you pull the lead out, you get a similar reaction to the treats.The longer the walk the better. Walks are so good for this gorgeous boy. They keep him stimulated and help shave off the pounds he gains from everyone giving him treats!On the whole, I was so happy to see my boy again. So happy, so loyal, keen to go on walks and he listened so well. I’m so proud of the progress he’s made since I last saw him!Gabriel still can be a challenge and, he does not like everyone. Once you’ve made that bond with him though, it stays. Don’t overlook this boy. He’s such a beautiful, intelligent and fun dog!

This boy has my heart, once you form that special bond with him I do believe it's for life. He is very loving and enjoys a cuddle and a fuss from people he feels comfortable around, as his adopter you'll hopefully get that bond through spending time with him and letting him go at his own pace.Gabriel, as most bockers do, lives for his food and treats, whenever I run him in from being in the yard I always leave a small treat in his bed or somewhere in the back for him to find, and he absolutely loves this he gets so excited knowing he'll get a treat and runs straight to the back of his kennel to find it.He loves his walks and his time in the big yard. He's the curious, adventurous type who just wants to investigate everything, a 10 minute stroll up the road can take us an extra 20-25 minutes to stroll back because he likes to explore, but I don't mind, seeing him happy makes me happy I'd never want to stop him from exploring the world.Gabriel would need an experienced, adult only home (view profile for explanation), he would need someone who is active and can provide him with mental and physical stimulation daily. He is a very smart boy and can pick things up quite quickly which is why, with the help from some treats I think he would pick up toilet training rather quickly.Please don't overlook this handsome guy just because he's not your small and fluffy type dog, Gabriel is a special boy with a lot of love to give, and for that right person/people he would become a true companion and best friend.

Gabriel, a stunning Bocker with a huge personality and an even bigger heart. This handsome boy is full of life and enthusiasm, always ready to embrace whatever the day brings. Gabriel thrives on affection and absolutely loves a good fuss and cuddle — he’s a real people-dog who enjoys being close to those he trusts.

Gabriel is a clever lad with so much potential. He’s already showing us just how intelligent he is as he responds to basic commands! With the right guidance and training , we know he’ll flourish and continue to grow in confidence, not that he lacks any confidence! He also walks beautifully on the lead, making him a joy to take out on adventures.

Sadly, Gabriel has been returned to us, and we are now searching for the right forever home where he can truly settle and feel secure. Gabriel would be best suited as an only dog, in a calm and quiet home environment. We are looking for a home with minimal visitors and no children, as busy households can feel overwhelming for him.

Gabriel can display guarding behaviours, so he will need experienced owners who understand this and can manage the home environment appropriately to support him. With the right knowledge, structure, and patience, Gabriel will thrive and continue to build trust.

Gabriel has so much love to give to the right person. With understanding, consistency, and kindness, he will make an incredibly loyal and devoted time

Gabriel is a stunning Bocker who is full of life, love, and character! This gorgeous boy is bursting with energy and adores nothing more than a good fuss and a cwtch.He’s incredibly smart, and we just know it wouldn’t take long for him to be trained to a very high standard – he’s already showing us how bright he is every day. Gabriel also walks beautifully on the lead, making him a joy to take out and about.Now all he needs is a forever family who can give him the love, attention, and adventures he deserves. With his brains, energy, and big heart, Gabriel is sure to make someone the most wonderful companion.

Gabriel has come to us from a breeder and has made incredible progress since arriving with us! When he first came, he was nervous around new people and worried about having a lead put on, which made walks and new kennel staff introductions a real challenge. Now, he gets excited for walk time and happily allows us to put on both his lead and harness with no issues.This handsome boy is a true foodie and loves learning new tricks. He already knows “sit” and “paw,” so with trust and patience, teaching him more tricks should be a fun and rewarding experience. Gabriel needs a confident, experienced, owner who will provide clear boundaries and structure, as he can be cheeky if he thinks he’s in charge as well as an adult-only home where he can continue to learn. Gabriel has the confidence to be an only dog in a home with can allow him the dedication and time he needs, or he can be homed with a resident companion that he can continue to learn and grow from, but will need a home with no cats or small furries.Once Gabriel knows and trusts you, he is affectionate, loyal, and loves nothing more than sharing his goofy charm. Underneath his shy moments, he is a beautiful boy with a heart full of love. He is looking for someone who will continue his training, set clear rules, and enjoy all the affection, loyalty, and joy he has to give.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Eddie
description:
I will need an adult only home with experienced adopters.

I could be an only dog or live with others. I cannot live with cats or small furries.

I’m active and would love a large, secure garden to play in.

I can walk on a lead and would like an active home.

I will need adopters who can work on my training with me.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home!

Eddie originally came to us from a breeder and was lucky enough to be adopted, but sadly he has been returned to our care. Eddie was not getting along with the resident cats and also began to show signs of resource guarding, particularly around food, which will need ongoing management and understanding in his new home.

Eddie is a clever, affectionate young lad who thrives on learning and enjoys a cuddle on his own terms. He already knows sit, down, paw, and touch, and responds beautifully to training. He’s crate trained, sleeps through the night in his crate, and is very food motivated, which makes him a fast learner. He has even been taught “give” to help him swap items like socks for a higher-value reward.

However, Eddie has some behaviours that need to be carefully managed. He does resource guard around food and for this reason he cannot have treats that take a long time to eat unless he is outside and nobody disturbs him while he's eating. He does also resource guard when humans are cooking (especially meat) or eating. He will become very focused and cannot be distracted until the food has gone so must either be crated or closed in a separate room during any kind of food time or he will growl, bark and can choose to lunge and make the wrong decision in his fixation.

Eddie can also become nippy when overstimulated, so his adopters must be calm, consistent and confident in setting clear boundaries. Due to this, he is looking for a calm and quiet adult-only home with no visiting children, and ideally with adopters experienced in similar behaviours. Eddie cannot live with cats or small furries and should be kept on a lead in any areas where they might be present as well as where any sheep or livestock may be present.

He is confident enough to be homed as an only dog but could also live with another kind, calm dog who is laid back. When food isn’t around, Eddie is a lovely boy who is affectionate, bright, and eager to please.

Eddie has so much potential and just needs someone who can offer him the structure, understanding, and stability he needs to shine. He is so popular here at the rescue and his kennel mum especially adores him. He is so much fun to spend time with and so loving and affectionate. He absolutely adores having cuddles and equally adores running and zooming around our big play yard, which is so funny to watch! If you think you could be the right match for Eddie, please do put in an application - he’s waiting patiently for the right person to come along.

He is currently resting for a limp, and will be re-examined by the vets in a weeks time.

Eddiewill need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

"I absolutely adore this boy! He always puts a smile on my face when I see him and he always greets me by putting his front paws up on me for a cuddle. One of my favourite things is when I’m sitting in the back of the kennel and he comes over for fusses and cuddles, giving me kisses in return.

He is full of energy and is super playful and loves to run around the big play yard. He has such a fun, goofy personality that is impossible not to love. He’s also a very clever boy who listens incredibly well. If I call him in the yard, he comes to me, and he will automatically sit politely for treats and meals.

Eddie is happy to share toys both in the kennel and the yard - although squishy balls are definitely his exception! On walks, he has always been good saying hello to other dogs, though he does prefer females. Since his kennel buddy Topanga was adopted, he has been introduced to Maggie and has been doing brilliantly with her, respecting her space while also playing chase together in the yard. Eddie’s a ladies man what can I say.

He really is just the softest baby. Once you’ve built that bond with him, it’s a bond for life. Like any other dog he just wants to feel safe and loved. To me he truly is a perfect angel that unfortunately keeps getting overlooked."

Another month, another update for Eddie because my poor boy is still waiting for his forever home. His kennel buddy Topanga was adopted yesterday leaving him yet again, behind.

I don’t know what it is about him that people keep looking over. He is such a great and special dog, and if someone would just take that chance and get to know him then you’d see that too. He’s cuddly, full of energy, is very trainable when using treats, and he’s a beautiful boy. In my opinion anyone would be lucky to have him as a part of their family.

Summer is coming, he should be with his family, spending days at the beach and exploring new places. Surely you don’t want him to miss out on that? He’s a young boy full of life, which needs to be lived outside of kennels.

Please give him a chance it could be one of the best decisions you’ve ever made.

Anything I say or write about Eddie really doesn’t do him justice. I am his kennel mum so I might be a little biased but you’ll just have to take my word for it that he is an amazing boy.

Eddie is so much fun and has a loving personality, he really is just a big baby. I can honestly never praise him enough and always feel proud to talk about him. I love this boy to bits and all his quirks.

So instead of rambling on in a written update I thought I’d just show you show videos of me and him having cuddles to show you what he’s really like, a side that you could see for yourself if you took him home. All I want for him is for him to find his forever home with people he belongs with and understand him and will show him respect and most importantly love.

To view the videos of me and Eddie having a cwtch, scroll down below his updates.

I've worked with Eddie before and was fortunate enough to have spent this weekend working with him again.

As it has been said before apart from his food possessiveness (which could be worked on using a variety of techniques) Eddie is a superb and loving dog.

Eddie is also an athlete who loves to run and run so he would suit an active home in particular. Eddie is quite strong willed but with consistency and proper boundaries in place, he responds and behaves himself.

Be under no illusion, Eddie is a special dog and special dogs need special homes to thrive in. In the right home, I'm sure Eddie will do just that.

I class this special boy as my work dog, we have such a good bond that we have built over the last few months. He really is a special boy.

Eddie keeps getting overlooked. It could be because he barks at people he doesn't know through the kennel on show-arounds, it could be because of his size/breed, or it could be because people haven't had experience with Bockers and their traits before. Either way, I feel for him.

I've said this to many people - once Eddie knows you and is comfortable with you. He is a big baby; he always comes over for a fuss and a cuddle when I walk into his kennel. In the big play yard, he likes to run and run, but when he sees I'm kneeling down and giving his kennel buddy Topanga a fuss, he comes running over and pushes himself in for a cwtch.

He is honestly a great dog; anyone would be lucky to have him. He loves being outside, he's very energetic, he's very smart and can pick things up quite quickly, and again, once he knows you, he's easier to handle.

He is food aggressive; he's fine when giving treats to him and Topanga at the same time, but when it comes to meal times, he must be fed separately, and his new people must work with him rather than against him. It's about mutual respect; you show him that and he'll show you the same.

I know there's someone out there for Eddie who'll be able to provide him with what he needs and can show him the love I show him every day I see him. My aim is to find that person and get Eddie into his forever, loving home.

My partner and I recently took Eddie out for a few hours to Margam Country Park to get him out of the kennels, and let me tell you, he was the bestest boy. We easily got him into the boot of the car although he seemed a little concerned and was very quiet whilst in there wondering where we could possibly be taking him, but he was so well behaved, he just sat/laid there for the whole journey.When we arrived at our destination he started to perk up again and was very interested as to where we were. We opened the boot and he jumped out excitedly, and immediately started sniffing and rolling around in the grass, you know he's happy when he rolls around and he did this so many times during the walk.During his afternoon out he walked past plenty of other doggies and people and he was amazing everytime we walked passed them, he listened to us and he was no trouble at all.We went to the farm area to see what he would be like around the animals there, he did get a little overexcited when he saw the alpacas, to him they possibly looked like big dogs, but he wasn't barking at them just pulling to get a better view of them and when I told him Eddie, no, he listened and we walked away.His tail didn't stop wagging the whole time he was his walk, he was so happy and it warmed my heart to see, needless to say my partner and I were extremely proud of him, we bought him a doggie ice cream but he didn't want to, guess there was too much for him to see and sniff.He spent the afternoon rolling in grass, getting plenty of cuddles from the both of us and experiencing what it is like in the outside world with people who genuinely love and care for him.On the way back, again he was fantastic think he was worn out as he layed down for most of the way back. As soon as we got him back we popped him straight into his kennel where he was greeted by his kennel friend Topanga who was super excited to see him as he was her, honestly I think we were sadder than he was to drop him back off.This was an amazing experience for him and it's something we will continue to do with him until he gets adopted. He is such a special boy who we love dearly and we hope he can find his forever home soon.

Eddie is a smart, amazing dog with a wonderful personality and so many great qualities. He’s growing in confidence, enjoys affection and walks, and is eager to be part of a loving home. However, Eddie does come with a few complex needs that will require patience and understanding.

He can become overstimulated at times, which may lead to reactive behavior. From his previous adoption experience, we’ve learned that Eddie cannot live with cats or small furries.

Eddie gets along well with other dogs and would thrive in a home with a calm, confident resident dog who can help show him the ropes. That said, he’s also confident enough to be the only dog in the home.

He has shown signs of resource guarding, so he’ll need a confident and committed adopter willing to continue his training and help him reach his full potential. Fortunately, Eddie is very food-motivated and already knows a few tricks, so training should be both effective and fun.

All in all, Eddie is an incredible companion with so much love to give. With patience, guidance, and plenty of affection, he will flourish, and he’ll repay your efforts with loyalty and love in abundance.

If you think you could be the person to help Eddie grow and thrive, please get in touch. He truly is a special boy.

Eddie originally came to us from a breeder a few weeks ago and was lucky enough to be adopted, but sadly he has been returned to our care. Eddie was not getting along with the resident cats and also began to show signs of resource guarding, particularly around food, which will need ongoing management and understanding in his new home.

However, Eddie has some behaviours that need to be carefully managed. He does resource guard around food and for this reason he cannot have treats that take a long time to eat unless he is outside and nobody disturbs him while he's eating. He does also resource guard when humans are cooking (especially meat) or eating. He will become very focussed and cannot be distracted until the food has gone so must either be crated or closed in a separate room during any kind of food time or he will growl, bark and can choose to lunge and make the wrong decision in his fixation.

Eddie can also become nippy when overstimulated, so his adopters must be calm, consistent and confident in setting clear boundaries. Due to this, he is looking for acalm and quiet adult-only home with no visiting children, and ideally withadopters experienced in similar behaviours. Eddie cannot live with cats or small furries and should be kept on a lead in any areas where they might be present as well as where any sheep or livestock may be present.

He had started adjusting well to home life, was doing nicely with his lead walking and house training and we believe he will truly thrive in the right environment.

Eddie has so much potential and just needs someone who can offer him the structure, understanding, and stability he needs to shine. If you think you could be the right match for Eddie, please do put in an application - he’s waiting patiently for the right person to come along.

Eddie has been seen by the vets for sore ears and has received treatment to keep them clean and free of infection.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Margate
description:
I need an experienced adult only home with no visiting children.

I would prefer to be an only dog, though may be able to live with a calm female.

I’m active and would love a large, secure garden to let off steam.

I have lots of energy and need an active home that loves adventures.

I can resource guard and will need adopters who can manage this.

I’m fully vaccinated and neutered. I have received treatment for an ear infection.

Margate has now spent over2 and a half years in kennels. That’sover 900 daysof waiting. Waiting while thousands of people have walked past his kennel. Waiting while his friends have come and gone. Waiting for someone to truly see him.

Everyone here adores Margate - he’s smart, affectionate, and bursting with personality. But love from staff isn’t the same as a forever home, and Margate is more than ready for his next chapter.

Since staying with us, Margate has received a dental treatment and had a cyst removed from his back left leg.

Margate came to us from a breeder in November 2023. He'sincredibly intelligent,loves a challenge, and will do absolutely anything for a tasty treat. He picks up tricks like lightning - he knows “sit”, “paw” and “speak” and he’s always ready to learn more. He thrives when his brain is engaged and would be an excellent candidate for scent work or activities like cani-cross (on-lead only!). He’s been described asone of the smartest dogs we’ve ever worked with.

He'svocal, a bit cheeky, and loves to tell us when it’s breakfast time - you can hear his excited barks all the way from the car park. He may come off as bouncy and bold, but beneath the surface, Margate is a sensitive soul who, sadly, has beenlet down by humans before. He can get worried easily, especially in overwhelming environments like the vet, where he becomes a bit of a puddle of anxiety (though he's muzzle-trained and manages with support).

Margate does require avery specific kind of home, and we won’t sugar-coat it - he’s not a “starter dog.” He needs:

Margate ispossessive around food, toys, and bedding, likely stemming from insecurity in his past. These behaviours are manageable - we’ll give guidance - but his adopters will need tounderstand and work with him, never punish or push him beyond his limits. Setting him up for success is the key.

Margate will test you - not because he's difficult, but because he's clever, spirited, and sensitive. But once you have his trust, you’ll havea bond that’s unbreakable.

Because Margate isn’t a dog you can bring home on a whim. He’s forsomeone extraordinary. Someone who sees past the behaviours and looks into his big, soulful eyes and says,“I’ve got you now.”

Margate has come so far during his time with us - he’s learned manners, made friends, gained confidence, and shown us theincredible potentialhe holds. But he deserves so much more than life in a kennel, no matter how much we love him.

Margate isnot for everyone, but if he’s for you - truly for you - then you are in for the mostrewarding journey. He’ll love you with his whole heart, and you’ll wonder how you ever lived without him.

If you think you could behis person, please don’t wait another day.

Margate’s forever is long overdue. Let’s not make him wait another year.

Margate will need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

“My boy, Margate… I see all of you, and I’ll love you like you’re my own until your forever person comes along. I’ll be so jealous of whoever gets to share their life with you.”

Margate is, hand on heart, one of the most phenomenal dogs I’ve ever met, and after nearly nine years at Many Tears, I’ve met a lot of dogs! He is going to be someone’s absolute best friend and the most wonderful adventure companion.

His profile already covers all of his basic needs, so I won’t repeat those here, but I can honestly say that not a single day he’s spent in kennels has been wasted.

He has grown, learned, and loved so much during his time with us. His confidence has blossomed, he absolutely adores people, and he has made some lovely doggy playmates. He knows exactly which treats are his favourites, and he’s settled beautifully into his routine. He heads out to his yard at 9am, then off to reception to spend time with more people (and, of course, collect more treats!).

Throughout the day, different staff members make time to visit him for extra cuddles and attention, and he often spends my entire lunch break relaxing in the yard on sunny days. This is something he enjoys every second of.

Margate is such a genuinely lovely guy, which makes it all the more surprising that he’s had to wait so long. He has the sweetest nature and seems to want nothing more than to be everyone’s best friend. While he’s here, he’s made some great female play friends and absolutely loves spending time with them every day, soaking up the companionship and fun.

In one month's time Margate will have been waiting a full two years for his forever family to find him. He's been in our care for a full 23 months, that's around 700 days, he's never been adopted or gone to foster so he's not been returned to us, he just has been in the rescue for all that time, waiting.

The first picture is his original booking in photo, a very worried boy who arrived knowing nothing about what we would do for him and how much love he'd come to find in this place that should be a pit stop until their forever home is found. But he slowly grew to love people, he started to let his true personality shine through and he started to learn how to be the goodest boy in so many ways. We flash forward to today and I can still see his insecurities at times, these may never truly go away, but we help him through it all and he is one of the best dogs I have ever known.

I love him as I love my own adopted dogs and I try to let him have as much time and love as I can give him to help him not feel like a single day in kennels is wasted. Because this is two years of his life that he won't get back, so I damn well hope that I, and everyone else who has had a part in his journey, makes every single moment count!I know he'll always have his quirks and he does need a specific type of home with people who understand and can positively manage his guarding behaviours so that he doesn't feel the need to show these often. But he's such a good boy, he's so loved by all of us at the rescue, he's so smart and beautiful and funny and loving and such good company. I love every speckle of white he's grown on his face in these two years, I love every snuggle we have and every single moment he looks to me to let me know that everything is going to be okay.He needs the right home but more importantly, he deserves the right home, the right people. If I could move heaven and earth to give him his perfect life, I would.I do want him to leave the rescue, but only to people that can fully commit to keeping him safe and listening to every single guideline and boundary that we give them. One of which is that HE MUST be kept on a lead at all times when out of fully secure areas, his prey drive is very high and he deserves to be given everything he needs to be kept safe from being harmed, which means keeping him on a lead!I shall continue to love him with every day that he's in my care, as well as every day after he finds his perfect home and I wave him off.I'll be so happy that he was chosen but he's going to leave a huge hole in my heart. He's truly one in a billion.If you feel you may be the right fit, please read his profile completely and fill in a very detailed application form. We will be very particular about where he goes, because that is the least he deserves from us.My handsomest boy, Mr Margate

It's strange really, the bond you have with a dog on your section, especially if they're a long stayer.

They aren't your dog and yet you love them as though they are, you cuddle them and tell them how amazing they are, you pour your heart and soul in to them and try your very hardest to make each and every day special in some way because their time in kennels shouldn't be a waste or simply a means to an end.. it should matter and they should be living their life whilst waiting for a family of their own.

This is hard to do when there are so many dogs to care for, but you try to do the best for them and to make sure they know how truly special they are.

This is very much the case for my best friend, Margate (AKA Maggie, Margaret, Marjorie, Marge and he responds to all these names). He is the epitome of special, he does need a specific home with people who don't put him in bad situations, but when the right people come along, they'll be adopting a dog that will give them an absolutely smashing best mate and although I am desperate for his forever home to find him, the thought of saying goodbye to the most special boy with a heart the size of the moon and a brain that's double that still. I will feel as though I'm missing a huge chunk of my heart.

That's the job though, and in a strange way, that sadness is the most rewarding part. To be a part of finding them the perfect match that will not love them in spite of their quirks, but because of them. Someone who will choose them day in, day out with a certainty that just grows with every passing day. The love a dog shares with you, no matter for how brief or long, as many will know, stays in your heart forever.

I shall miss my best mate, Margate. His family will love him the longest, but I loved him first and I am so so grateful for that.

If you are interested in being the exceptionally lucky human that shares their life with this beautiful soul, then please read his profile and apply if you feel that you can give him all he needs.

Another day for Margate in kennels, after another week, another month... I'm just hoping that he doesn't have to wait until the another year mark.

Margate has been waiting for seventeen months for his ideal home to find him, that's 506 days in the same place, with people who adore him, but they're not just his people. He has to share his time with so many other dogs that are waiting for their forever homes, but none have been waiting as long as him.

He does require a special home, with people who can help him thrive and who will not fail him! But as long as they understand his needs and set him up for success, they'll get so much in return.

Margate needs a cat, child (resident and visiting) and small furry free home. He needs mental stimulation and he needs a good deal of exercise too, he is smart, so so soooo smart and this is where his humans will have to try and be smarter.

Margate learns everything very quickly and will need to have clear, set boundaries in his new home from the get-go. He will need to be given his own bed, that he learns is his bed and that he isn't allowed on the sofas or other furniture, especially to begin with. He will need a home that understands how to enrich his life, to use his brain and that LOVES a good walk. He could walk for miles. He could potentially do cani-cross as he loves to run, but Margate needs to be kept safe at all times, so he will need to be kept on a lead in anything he does (unless in a secure rented dog field area). He has a high prey drive and a nose that rivals the very best sniffer dog. If you had treats in your pocket at some point this year, he'll know.

The best set up for Margate would be a home that could adapt a room to be his area that is free of food and where he could go to sleep at night, if visitors came round or if you had to pop out to go shopping etc. He needs this to be his area that helps him feel safe and secure.

Although he can act like a big tough guy, underneath it all he is extremely sensitive and can get very worried by things.

How can we blame him for being this way? He's been let down by humans over and over again, he has had thousands of people walk by his kennel during his stay and no one chooses him.. I know that in a home that FULLY understands him and puts everything in place to set him up to succeed, then he will be the bestest boy you could ever wish to know. His heart is the size of the whole universe and he just needs someone to see him and commit to him, then they'll see what I see too. My boy, Margate, I see all of you and I'll love you like you're my own until your forever person comes along. I'll be so jealous of the person who gets to share their life with you.

Disclaimer - although he picks up agility things quite quickly, this is not something he should do to a competitive level as the environment of the events would not benefit him.

It is now over a year since Margate joined us here in Many Tears.  That’s 365 days of seeing the same kennel, and the same routine and watching his friends leave whilst he gets left behind. This year it’ll be the second Christmas in kennels and whilst others are snuggled up in a warm home and spoilt with presents, it’ll be another day in kennels for him.  Everyone here at the rescue loves Margate!

What can I say that already hasn’t been said about this wonderful boy ??

He’s a real character, smart and witty. Every morning I hear him from the staff car park and you know that he’s announcing breakfast time for the whole rescue!  At times he can be loud and bouncy but deep down he’s just a little baby who loves a good snuggle.

Margate really has improved over the year, learnt new tricks, muzzle trained, met loads of new friends and loves meeting new people! He’ll happily walk past volunteers and once instructed to sit and wait he will receive a fuss.

So far Margate has only needed the muzzle for the vets as a precaution, but he tends to turn into a puddle of mush as he’s actually quite scared of the vets.

Margate does have issues regarding high value treats, toys and beds. This is resolved by removing any food in the area, removing any toys from the yard before play time and using multiple blankets for bedding. This is something that can be discussed and helped with for potential adopters.

All Margate needs is for adopters to understand his requirement’s and who are willing to work with him in a home environment.  He is such a beautiful wonderful boy who really is wasted here in kennels?

Margate is still absolutely thriving and learning every day. He is such a clever boy who enjoys his training and the company of his kennel friend. He has come such a long way, but in a home he could truly excel as long as his new humans know how to keep his training progressing and his mind busy. I really hope he breaks free soon.

Margate is one of those dogs that once you know him, you fall head over heels in love with him. He is one of the smartest dogs I've ever worked with, picks things up incredibly quickly and tries very hard to please you. He would be the perfect only dog for someone who wants to pour lots of time in to training to build a strong bond with him and who will understand that he will train you if given a chance. He's a super special guy with so much love to give, he just needs the right person to choose him and fully commit.

Margate is a super cool bocker. He loves long walks and his time in the yard. He thrives with anything to get his brain working, so stimulation games are an excellent way of working Margate. The best way to work with him is definitely food, he will do anything for a tasty treat. Be careful though as Margate can be food possessive. As you can see by the pictures he is such an affectionate boy who loves a good fuss, to go with that he is just absolutely handsome! Margate has been in kennels for some time now and he does cope well with being in kennels, but he would just absolutely thrive in an active home with someone who is willing to keep him busy and stimulated. While he's been with us he's always had a female kennel friend but he would also be an excellent only dog, so you have the best of both worlds with Margate, but due to his exuberant nature he might grate on any resident dogs a bit . Margate is a stunning young man with bags of personality and he loves all the attention and will need an experienced owner as he is a rambunctious soul. Margate is an absolute star and deserves the best life possible, if you feel you can offer him his best life get in contact. Be sure to read all the previous updates on Margate also to get the best overall idea of this smart little man. This wonderful boy has been in kennels for far too long now and is ready to find his forever home and new loving family. Will you give him the chance to progress to the next chapter in his life?

Margate is a unique boy. Firstly, Margate will need an owner with some experience under their belt, to make sure his rehabilitation is successful. It is probably best that he is homed as an only dog, as Margate struggles to get along with some dogs due to his boisterous personality, which can be overwhelming. This doesn't mean he can't be homed with another dog, as he has had a few kennel friends at the rescue that he gets along great with, he just prefers human attention. Margate will not trust strangers 100% straight off the bat , he's always excited to see people and interact with them, but to build a bond of trust takes some time. However this is an experience that I've found extremely rewarding. I discovered this when I took him to the vets for an intimate checkup, and was surprised to discover that he would only allow me to carry out the checks. It absolutely melted my heart to know that he had complete faith in me. He really is a sweet, colourful personality with so much love and energy to give, he just needs some time, love and repetition with a regular owner to steer all that energy in a positive direction

Since his last update Margate has been enjoying learning tricks and enjoying fun little games. We have learnt to "sit" "paw" and to" speak". Margate enjoys his treats so was very keen to learn and being as smart as he is, picks up training in a flash. He is a very vocal boy and will bark until I give a treat. I do not give in and he will stop and wait patiently. Margate does need to learn manners so will need adopters to remind him of this and to show Margate right and wrong.

Margate has come on so well since being here and I must admit that I do have a soft spot for him. He enjoys his walks but does pull a little when he has found a sniff, but can easily be managed with lead training. I'm currently teaching Margate to sit when I stop walking, to get his pulling under control, and as Margate is food motivated, it’s easy to see that this can be managed with little trouble. He is already  getting the hang of it!

Margate does jump up on you and can jump quite high, so adopters will need to encourage Margate not to jump up, which I am currently showing him, but again he is a very smart boy who is eager to learn. He is a funny, friendly, and vocal boy who will needs adopters to be mindful of his needs and issues and redirect him properly for him to be the best boy he can. He is still kenneled with his friend who both still continue to share their bed and space together with no problems. He is still only young and I can't wait to see him flourish more than what kennel life can offer him. Please read Margate's profile and updates and see what an amazing boy he is. He is perfect for someone that loves to share an active lifestyle with their dog, and we can’t wait to see pictures of him on all his future adventures!

Margate is a gorgeous boy who I have spent a lot of time getting to know recently and has come along way since his last update. He greets you with a waggy tail and excitement when he knows you and does enjoy a gentle fuss. He is also getting used to his name and when you call him he will turn to you. Margate has very high energy and needs an active household who enjoys long walks and adventures and are able to stimulate him mentally as well as physically. He also walks on a lead but can pull, this can easily be managed with lead training and I believe will settle once he gets to go on regular adventures with his new family as he enjoys the sniffs of the world . Margate loves to learn and play and due to his breed, he will need adopters to understand the Bocker breed and the energy they have. Margate currently is kennelled with a female cocker and they spend their time together playing in the yard and rolling around in their beds together. They both get on very well!

Due to Margate's resource guarding, him and his kennel friend have bedding and blankets rather than plastic normal beds and they both share this with no problems. Margate does have food guarding issues  and is fed separately from his kennel friend. This can also be managed in a home with another dog if he is separated at feeding times. Margate does take treats from you when his kennel friend is present and has not shown any issues towards them but this still needs to be remembered. I have been visiting him when I'm not on the same section to issue treats around other dogs in proximity to get him used to being in the same space around other dogs with food and treats. With these small issues, Margate will need an adult home with no children.

Margate has mastered a few tricks with treats, learning to sit and give paw. He gets very excited when you ask him but need to tell him firmly. Margate can be jumpy and will jump on you so he will need a firm voice to encourage him manners. Margate learns quickly so I think teaching him house training, lead training as well as other tricks and manners will come quickly for him, especially with treats!

He is a stunning boy and overlooked here at the rescue. If you have a Bocker sized hole in your heart and are active, love long walks, adventures and fun. Please consider applying for Margate.

Hi! My name is Margate I am 2 years old male Bocker (Cocker X Beagle) and have found myself at this amazing place called Many Tears. When I first arrived, I was extremely worried with my new surroundings, and adapting back to kennel life was very tough. I have spent a little time in a home but because of my high energy levels and need for adventures my family wasn’t able to provide this so unfortunately through no fault of my own I have set out on an adventure to find my new forever home. In my new home I would need a child free home with no visiting children. In my new home I will need someone who understands my breed and have had experience with similar breeds. I am currently in a bedroom (Kennel) with a female Bocker called Nova. We don’t interact too much but we do enjoy one another’s company so I could be happy having another friend in my new home upon having a meet and greet at the rescue. I can also be an only dog as long as I have a loving owner who plays lots of games with me. I have mastered walking on a lead and I absolutely love to explore and go on walks so having a busy home who is as on the go as I am would really benefit my energy levels. I still struggle a little to find the toilet but if I’m shown I will definitely find a way to go in the right place. when I meet new people I can become a little shy and anxious so go slow with me, have a kind voice and I will wag my tail and ask you for some fuss. After spending a day with you i trust very easily and I will become your best friend. If you’d like to meet me I am currently at the many tears rescue.Margate is still waiting for his forever home at Many Tears and despite his struggles around food and resource guarding from humans and dogs when food is involved, all he needs is an experienced home with the ability to adapt and help Margate with this. He will bring so much joy into your home and he can't wait to be a cherished member of the family where he can go on lots of adventures and finally be loved.

Margate is a stunning but very confused boy who is overwhelmed to be here and has been through so much change recently. He has come to us from a breeder to find his forever home. Initially when you first meet him he is mistrustful of strangers and unsure about being handled - he will make his worries known by growling. However once you have spent a few minutes in his company showing that you are safe he quickly warms up to you and jumps all over you for love and attention. We suspect he may have lived in a home before and then returned to the breeder hence his being unsure of people initially which is fair enough, poor boy! Margate already walks on a lead and would probably prefer to be an only dog.  Margate will need a cat free home as he is far too interested in them and a home without children or visiting children as he would find them too much. Margate may need help learning to live in a home and being taught housetraining skills. Margate is such a poor soul who just wants to be loved and to feel safe. Margate would need a home that is experienced in resource guarding and can help Margate work through his struggles. Margate can resource guard when he has food or high value treats/chews/bones around both dogs and people so this would need to be managed with an environment set up to ensure lovely Margate is set up for success.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Criss Cross
description:
I need an adult-only experienced home that understands me.

I'd prefer to be the only dog in the home and no cats please!

I’m still shy, so I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I can now walk on a lead and harness and enjoy my walks.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home!

Meet Criss Cross, a big-hearted, sensitive lad who has come to us from a breeder and is slowly learning to find his confidence in the world. When Criss Cross arrived as a worried soul, needing extra patience, understanding, and gentle care. He had very little formal handling in his past, and early on could be grumbly or anxious when unsure. He required slow, careful introductions to people, places, and experiences while he started to understand his new surroundings.

While Criss Cross can still be a little shy when meeting new people, once he feels comfortable his affectionate side really shines through. He actively seeks out fuss and cuddles and has become quite the cuddle bug, often trying to return kisses to the people he trusts. He is learning his manners well and responds nicely to calm, consistent guidance.

Criss Cross is growing in confidence with his harness training and now enjoys daily walks beyond the rescue. He has even started greeting some people on his walks for a hello and perhaps a treat or a fuss. He can still feel a little unsure if people are behind him, but gentle encouragement and reassurance help him relax and continue exploring the world around him.

He has also started learning about kennel cleanliness, with staff helping him practice good toilet habits to make the transition into a home life easier for him in the future. When enjoying some quiet time on his own, Criss Cross loves exploring his toy collection, especially anything involving treats or food puzzles.

Criss Cross would prefer to be the only dog in the home so he can fully relax and enjoy all the attention for himself.

He will need a calm, adult-only home with experienced adopters who can continue helping him build confidence at his own pace. He also appreciates having a quiet space at mealtimes where he feels safe and secure. Criss Cross will need a home without cats as he is overly interested in them.

Criss Cross is a truly gorgeous, sweet-natured gentle giant with so much love to give. With patience, understanding, endless bum scratches and plenty of tasty treats, he has every chance to flourish into the loyal and devoted companion he is meant to be.

Criss Crosswill need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Criss Cross is such a sweetheart! He is loyal to his friend Crumble, his kennel mums and dads. He’s still greeting everyone on walks with a bit of caution but once he’s recognised you, you’re considered family.

He’s going on occasional independent walks to gain his confidence but he is always looking for Crumble to keep him company. He’s very cautious of new things however day by day his confidence is growing and with his trust in his kennel mums he always runs back to check they’re okay, showing you just how brave he is and continues to be, so when the right home finds him….they will have to earn his trust and give him the patience and love he truly deserves.

This distinguished gent is progressing so well, every day!

He has started to get in the practice of being kennel clean, one of his kennel mums likes to come in a little early to help him practice this so when he finds his forever home it might be a little less stressful learning toilet training skills properly.

Criss Cross is even getting more adventurous greeting people on his daily walks now just to  say hello and maybe scoring a treat or fuss, and even recognising some of the staff when they visit him.

He is protective of Crumble and always looks to see her catching up on their daily walks, and enjoys playing with her or being near watching over her. But when he has his solo kennel time, he has been exploring his personal toy collection, anything with a treat in it and he’s happy. This cuddle bug deserves a home with endless bum scratches and scrumptious treats

This handsome guy is progressing every day! And showing his kennel mums just how much of a sweetheart he is. He is an affectionate goofball once he’s comfortable with you. He enjoys playing with his chow friend Crumble and he loves getting love from her and his kennel mums. He loves to jump up to have his harness put on, refusing to stand still because he gets so excited to go out and explore with Crumble.

Criss Cross will now even allow some of his part time kennel mums and dads to give him attention too. He is still very cautious of new people but is slowly meeting new people and remembering them!

With some time his new family will find out just how special Criss Cross is!

Criss Cross has made incredible progress over the last few days. He used to hide and grumble when people approached his kennel, but this has changed massively!

Now, Criss Cross comes up to the kennel door and wags his tail, getting very excited to see people. Spending time with this handsome boy brings me so much joy; I often find myself straying from my section just to sneak in five minutes of cuddles.

Criss Cross is still looking for a quiet, gentle home and someone who will take things slow while he gets to know them.

Criss-Cross has grown into an incredibly sociable and interactive boy, although he can still be a little shy when first meeting new people. Once he feels comfortable, he actively seeks out attention and loves a cuddle, often trying to give kisses in return. Criss-Cross is a big lad and is still learning his manners; when he’s excited he can occasionally use his size to try and barge past, but he responds well to clear, firm verbal guidance.

He will need a quiet area to enjoy his meals, as this helps him feel more secure. Criss-Cross is doing very well with his harness training and has already ventured out of the rescue for walks, managing a good distance down the road. He can still feel a little unsure if people are walking behind him, and one of his endearing little quirks is that he finds reassurance if you turn and face him while walking backwards. Criss-Cross is a truly gorgeous, sweet and gentle giant who will thrive in an active, patient and understanding home.

Criss Cross is a very worried soul who has come to us from a breeder, and is needing extra patience, understanding, and tender care. He appears to have had very little formal handling in his past and can be grumbly or anxious when he feels uncomfortable. He requires slow, careful introductions and time to begin to understand the world around him.

Criss Cross would thrive in an adult-only home with experienced owners who can provide the reassurance and consistency he needs to start building trust. He is still learning the basics of house training and walking on a lead, so his adopters will need to be patient and gentle as he gradually discovers confidence. He will need an understanding and kind resident dog in the home he can look to as he continues to learn and trust.

This sweet, sensitive boy is not yet ready for the full bustle of the world, but with time, quiet encouragement, and safe garden sniffing adventures, he will slowly open up and reveal the loyal companion he can be. Criss Cross will need a calm, loving home where his cautious nature is respected and nurtured and where he can finally feel safe and truly belong.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Buck
description:
I would like an active home filled with love and kindness!

I can be an only dog or live with a calm female dog.

I would love a garden to run and play in!

I can walk well on a harness and would love an active home.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have no tail, but this doesn't bother me!

Please meet the very handsome Buck, who has travelled all the way from Romania in the hope of finding his forever home. Since arriving here a few months ago, Buck has truly blossomed! Initially a little shy with new people, he now settles quickly and happily shows off his affectionate, gentle nature. He loves attention, enjoys fuss, and is more than happy to make new friends once he feels comfortable.

Buck is such a comical boy, always finding little ways to make you smile, but he is also incredibly affectionate. He has that spark of energy that brightens even the hardest of days.

Buck absolutely loves his food. You’ll hear his excited bark echo around the rescue at mealtimes without fail. He also really enjoys his walks and having a good run around the big yard, where he can let out all that happy energy. Buck also has the most cuddly side to him; he loves nothing more than resting his head on your lap while he slowly dozes off to sleep.

Buck has boundless, puppy-like energy and is so much fun to spend time with. He is happy, bouncy, and full of life, with an infectious enthusiasm that staff and volunteers can’t help but fall in love with. He walks beautifully on a harness and has made fantastic progress with lead confidence, now happily trotting along on a slip lead without a care in the world.

Buck was briefly adopted but was sadly returned as he didn't get along with the resident dog. Buck enjoys the company of well mannered female does and does enjoy sharing his kennel with another dog, so we would consider homing him with a laid back female dog if they were the right match. He can get a little jealous of sharing attention with another dog, but in the right home with experienced adopters this could be managed. That said, Buck would be equally happy to be homed as the only dog and get all the love and attention for himself.

Buck will be looking for an adult only home and would be best suited to adopters who have experience of owning dogs from a similar background before and who are around most of the time, so they can spend lots of time with him. He would love to find an active home where he can be kept both physically and mentally stimulated and enjoy plenty of walks and adventures with his new family.

Although Buck had never lived in a home before being adopted, he settled in wonderfully and was clean in the house. He wasn’t phased by the TV or radio and quickly found the sofas and beds! He can still be a little shy when first meeting men, but it doesn’t take him long to warm up. Buck also isn’t keen on sudden loud noises or sticks and brooms, so this is something his adopters will need to work on desensitising him to. He doesn’t like having his front paws touched, but he will politely let you know by gently pulling them away.

Buck is a truly special boy with so much love to give. He is absolutely wasted in kennels and will make a loyal, devoted companion for a very lucky family. He arrived with no tail, but this doesn’t bother him in the slightest and only adds to his charm. Please don’t overlook this wonderful lad, as Buck is more than ready to find his forever home.

Buck has received negative test results for Brucellosis, Leishmania, Giardia, Heartworm, Ehrlichia canis, Borrelia burgdorferi and Anaplasma.

Buck will need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

For the past few weeks, Buck has been front and centre in our big yard play group! This is a great chance for our people-shy new dogs to meet and play with more confident long-stayers like Buck, and he's met a lot of new dogs this way, which suggests that once he's settled into his new home, he might benefit from a dog park or opportunities to meet up with friends.

Buck's best friend is another Romanian dog called Olive. You rarely see one without the other, in their yard, the big yard, or on walks! Sometimes in big yard play group, Bucky and Olive will separate from the other dogs and chase and wrestle each other for a while, and it's lovely to see. Unfortunately, Buck and Olive don't like to be kenneled together as they prefer their own space to unwind after all that time spent playing.

However, Buck has really come out of his shell with people around the rescue. He has a few people he's very close to, and is always up for cwtches with them. How can you say no that handsome face? He's also happier now to meet new people than he used to be, and now goes on walks with volunteers (accompanied, of course, by Olive) most days.

Buck can still get possessive over his favourite people, which is something we have to be aware of in big yard play group and that his adopters will need to bear in mind. He may grumble at other dogs in warning if they get too close when he's having his cuddles, though this does not usually escalate beyond a warning snap. It's likely that our sweet Bucky may not have had stable affection in his past, so he feels the need to guard that love from other dogs.

Luckily for Buck, we have plenty of love to go around at Many Tears! Hopefully, as he settles, he'll realise he is safe and loved and doesn't have to guard anyone anymore.

I’ve had the absolute pleasure of working with Buck, or “Buckaroo” as I like to call him, since the very day he arrived with us. I know you’re not supposed to have favourites, but if I’m honest, Buck has completely stolen a place in my heart.

Buck is such a comical boy, always finding little ways to make you smile, but he is also incredibly affectionate. He has that spark of energy that can turn even the hardest days into better ones.

Buck’s kennel friend, who he came over from Romania with, has recently been adopted. He adored her so much, and since she left, there’s been a little piece of him missing. Now he is the one still waiting, and it is heartbreaking knowing he doesn’t understand why it is not his turn yet.

Although Buck has shared his kennel and enjoys playdates with other dogs, always greeting them nicely out on walks, he would also thrive as the only dog in a home. He loves human attention so deeply and would really benefit from having people around most of the time to give him the love, comfort, and reassurance he deserves.

I recently took Buck out for a day to a local beach. From the moment we arrived, he was in his absolute element and enjoyed every second of it. Buck spent the majority of the time on a long line; although he ran around and explored, he also came back to me when called and always turned to make sure I was still close. This just shows how much of a loyal companion he can be.

I honestly love Buck with my whole heart, and it breaks me a little every single day knowing he is still waiting for his people to come for him. He has so much love, loyalty, and joy to give; he just needs someone to give him that chance.

It’s been a few weeks since Bucks arrival, and oh my has he made his presence known. Every single staff member who has had the pleasure of meeting this boy is utterly obsessed, and it’s very clear as to why!

Buck’s energy is infectious, he is the happiest, bounciest, most adoring little man and whoever adopts him will be truly lucky!!! When you meet him you’d never assume he is 6 years old - he exudes puppy energy.

Following on from his initial write up, Buck has very quickly learned that having a lead around his neck isn’t so scary after all and now happily trots around on the slip lead without a care in the world.

Buck loves to play with his fellow Romanian buddy Jenny and they get along great. He also goes for play dates with the lovely Orietta, and he thoroughly enjoys it. He would like adopters who have experience with owning dogs before and who are around most of the time.

This sweet sweet boy is absolutely wasted in kennels, he is bursting with love to give and he’ll make a very lucky someone the happiest person in the world. Our foreign dogs are often overlooked and tend to hang around in kennels much longer than needed - please don’t let this be the case for our lovely Buck.

Please meet the very handsome Buck, who has travelled all the way from Romania in the hope of finding his forever home. Buck is a truly sweet boy who can be a little shy when he first meets new people, but it doesn’t take long for him to relax and show his affectionate nature. Once he knows you, he is more than happy to make friends and enjoys gentle fuss and attention.Buck is a gentle soul who is easy to handle. He is looking for a home where he can be the only dog and would benefit from adopters who have experience owning dogs before. Although Buck gets along well with other dogs and enjoys playing with and meeting them on walks, we think he would thrive as an only dog. He is around the size of a Labrador, but with a slightly smaller, lighter build.Buck already walks nicely on a harness as the tension on a slip lead can spook him but would love an active home where he can enjoy lots of walks, adventures and quality time with his new family. Buck has never lived in a home before, so his adopters will need to be kind, patient and understanding while he learns all about house training and home life.Buck is a very special boy who will make a wonderful, loyal companion for the right family willing to give him the love, guidance and security he so deserves. He has arrived here with no tail, but this doesn't bother him and just adds to his charm.Buck has recieved negative test results for Brucellosis, Leishmania, Giardia, Heartworm, Ehrlichia canis, Borrelia burgdorferi and Anaplasma.Buck has a passport.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.
