name: Topps
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I would like a daily walk and lots of cuddle time.

I'm learning house training and building confidence on the lead.

I have a slight click in my left stifle joint, read below for more info!

Topps arrived with us from a breeder and, at first, was an extremely worried and shut-down little girl. Everything was overwhelming for her, and she found human contact very frightening, as she didn’t yet understand how gentle and loving hands could be. With time, patience, and kindness, Topps has started to blossom and has already made such wonderful progress. From barking at her kennel mummy and wanting little to do with people, she now feels comfortable sitting close, taking treats, giving sweet nose nudges, and even sharing little kisses. She has recently begun to accept gentle ear tickles when she is feeling brave, a huge step for her!

Topps has now moved to a foster home, where she is starting to learn all about home life and new skills like house training for the first time. In the home, she is still a little nervous as she adjusts to her new environment. Despite this, she will spend time in the same room as her carers, happily follow for food or to venture outside, and is comfortable moving throughout the home. Although she approaches her carers with encouragement from her canine friend or the help of a treat, she will avoid close contact at times. However, she is still very waggy-tailed and really gains confidence from her furry friends.

Topps still has a way to go and will need an experienced, adult-only home where she can continue to build her confidence at her own pace. She must have at least one kind and confident resident dog, who will play an important role in showing her the ropes and helping her feel safe. A secure garden is essential for her as she continues to learn about lead walking and house training. Topps will tolerate being handled for check overs or to have her harness put on, but she is wary and needs slow handling and reassurance.

This lovely girl may be worried at first in her new home, but with patience, reassurance, and love, she has every chance of blossoming further into the happy, content little dog she was always meant to be. Topps has already shown us what she is capable of; now she just needs someone to take a chance on her and give her the forever home she so truly deserves.

Our vet has noted a slight click in her left stifle joint on palpation. This is not currently causing her any discomfort and does not require treatment at this time, but it may need monitoring in the future. We recommend being mindful of excessive jumping on and off furniture or other jarring movements. Gentle exercises to help build and maintain muscle, such as controlled walking and stepping up and down kerbs, will be beneficial.

Topps is coming along really well, although she is still quite cautious at times. Despite this, she is such a happy girl and will happily come over when she would like some attention. She absolutely loves going out for walks and gets very excited as soon as the harness comes out. She also really enjoys seeing other dogs while out and about, often going over to greet them, and she has even started to approach people on walks too. Topps is very food-motivated and takes treats from people very gently.

Topps is a happy, tail-wagging girl who has settled in wonderfully with the other dogs in the house. She loves cuddling up and sleeping alongside her canine friends, and she gets along well with other dogs she meets too.

While Topps is confident around dogs, she is still a little cautious and hesitant around people. That said, when she feels ready, she will happily come over for a gentle chin scratch or an ear rub, all on her own terms.

Topps would thrive in a home that is patient and understanding, giving her the time she needs to build trust and feel completely comfortable. With her cheerful nature and love for canine company, she is sure to bring joy and companionship to the right family.

My cheeky little lady Topps has been with me for just over a month now and she’s come out of her shell SO much in that time. When she arrived on my section she really didn’t want anything to do with me and would more or less bark at me constantly. Progress has been slow and steady over the past few weeks, but now she’s very comfortable being around me, she’ll sit near me, she’ll take treats from me, she’ll give me little kisses and nudges with her nose, AND she’s just started to accept a little tickle under her ears when she’s feeling brave … which is huge!!! I’m super happy with how well she’s doing and it gives me great hope for her future. If she can progress this much in a kennel environment, just imagine what she could do in a home with her own humans!?Topps will still need a resident dog in her new home and still no children as I do think that would be too overwhelming for her. She may be quite worried when she first gets home, but with lots of reassurance and patience, I do think she will blossom in time. She really just needs somebody to take a chance on her now, and show her how beautiful life can actually be. She deserves the world and more, so please don’t keep her waiting much longer.

Topps has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. She is an extremely worried and shut down girl at the moment who is finding everything very overwhelming. She is very frightened of being handled, as she doesn’t yet understand that human hands can be gentle and loving.

Topps is looking for a calm and quiet, adult only home with experienced adopters who understand the needs of scared ex-breeding dogs. She will need lots of patience, time and understanding to help her slowly come out of her shell.

She must have at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home who can be her friend and take her under their wing. This will play a vital role in helping Topps build confidence and settle in to life in a loving home.

Topps has never lived in a home before, and she will likely find this new environment very overwhelming at first. She will need gentle support and encouragement as she begins to learn about house training, and her adopters will need to take things at her pace.

She does not yet walk on a lead and will need to start slowly, practicing in a secure garden once she is ready. Because of how fearful she currently is, a secure garden is essential for her safety and confidence building.

Topps has a long journey ahead of her, but with the right family, she has the potential to transform into a happy and content dog. If you have the time, love, and experience to help her on this journey, please consider offering Topps the second chance she so desperately deserves.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Zinc
description:
Zinc is not ready for adoption at this time. Keep checking back for updates.

I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a resident dog to help me feel safe but I haven’t met cats yet so I'm not sure

I’m still shy, so I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I am learning about house training in my foster home!

Please read below for more information about my health!

Zinc is a truly sweet and gentle older lady who came to us from a breeder. Sadly, Zinc arrived to us with very limited vision as she had cataracts in her left eye and no sight in her right eye. After visiting a specialist, Zinc underwent cataract surgery, and having some sight again has made a wonderful difference to her life. While she was always a happy, waggy-tailed little dog even without vision, she is now much more confident and curious about the world around her, happily exploring her surroundings, meeting new people and dogs, and even enjoying short walks along the beach with her foster family and their dog.

Zinc has previously had an abdominal hernia successfully repaired and has had two teeth extracted. She has very few teeth remaining and therefore may benefit from a softer diet depending on her comfort when eating. She also previously received treatment for itchy skin, which has now improved greatly,  her skin is in very good condition and her coat is now soft and shiny.

Zinc is in a loving foster home where she has settled in beautifully. Despite having never lived in a home before arriving with us, Zinc took to home life like a duck to water and has remained clean both day and night with regular trips into the garden.

According to her foster mummy, Zinc absolutely loves exploring and being involved in daily life. She is very food-motivated and will happily supervise any activity in the kitchen in the hope that something tasty might appear. In fact, she loves food and treats, really anything edible! She also enjoys pottering around the garden, keeping an eye on anyone doing the gardening, and will occasionally burst into playful zoomies around the house or garden, acting more like a puppy than a senior dog.

Zinc gets on very well with other dogs and would be happiest living with dog-friendly companions of any size or breed. She enjoys meeting other dogs out and about and is comfortable sharing her space, as long as they are kind and tolerant.

Other than food, Zinc’s absolute favourite thing is human contact. She actively seeks out cuddles and will let you know she wants to be picked up by gently pawing at your legs. She loves joining her people on the sofa in the evenings and happily accepts cuddles from visitors too. Zinc is incredibly easy to handle and truly doesn’t have a bad bone in her body, everyone who meets her falls a little bit in love with her.

Zinc is a gentle, loving soul who is looking for a calm and quiet home where she can enjoy her golden years surrounded by love, comfort, and cuddles. Whilst she is an older dog, she now has a wonderful new lease of life and her tail rarely stops wagging.

Anyone considering adopting Zinc must be prepared to continue her eye drop routine and attend occasional follow-up appointments with an eye specialist to monitor and protect her remaining eye. Please note that this eye would not be covered by insurance.

Zinc will make a devoted and affectionate companion for someone with a big heart and a loving home.

Having sight has made a big difference to Zinc, whilst she was a very happy Waggy tailed little dog without sight, now, she explores everywhere, loves meeting other dogs, and people, even having short walks along the beach, with our own dog (a big GSD).

Zinc would be happiest living with dog friendly dogs, any size or breed.  She loves her food and treats in fact anything edible, her skin is now in very good condition plus she's no longer skinny and her coat is soft and shiny.

She loves to join us on the sofa at night and really enjoys a cuddle, even from visitors, letting us know by pawing at our legs to be lifted up. Zinc is very easy to handle with not a bad bone in her body plus rarely has a toilet accident indoors.Whilst Zinc is an older dog, she acts at times like a puppy, doing zoomies around the garden or indoors, she's also keen to supervise us gardening, or anyone in the kitchen cooking as she loves food.

Anyone considering adopting Zinc must be prepared to carry on her eye drop schedule plus visit an eye specialist for future tests to make sure her one remaining eye is maintained, her eye would not be covered by insurance.Zinc is really enjoying her new lease of life as her tail never stops Wagging..

Little Zinc has settled into living in a home like duck to water. She has been clean both day and night with regular garden trips.

Zinc's eyes will be assessed again in January, she is aware of what's around her, and nothing stops her from getting around, especially if  she can smell food, as she has a very good appetite.

Zinc is still stiff in her back legs, but it doesn't stop her getting around and exploring.

Zinc is great with other dogs, she's needs mates that are tolerant for her bumping into them now and again.

Other than food, Zinc's favourite pastime is seeking out a human to be picked up and cuddled. They don't come much sweeter than this little girl. Everyone who meets her are smitten.

Zinc is a truly sweet and gentle older lady who has come to us from a breeder. She may be a little unsure of the world at first, but once she knows you, she’s all love and wags. If you call her name and she’ll happily waddle over with her tail going a mile a minute, ready for a cuddle or to curl up in your lap. She adores a good snuggle and would love nothing more than to share your bed or snooze beside you.

Zinc has never lived in a home before, so she will need patient adopters who can gently guide her through house training and all the new experiences of family life. She relies heavily on the company of other dogs, so she must have a kind, confident resident dog in her new home to help show her the ropes and give her reassurance.

Zinc is an older girl with a few age-related challenges. She has full cataracts in her left eye and no sight in her right, and has been seen by a specialist who has determined that she may need cataract surgery in one of her eyes if she is deemed as a candidate, and may need the other removed. She is anaemic so nothing can be done for her at this time, but if things do improve, we will consider moving forward with the surgeries. Her adopters will need to be aware of this and will need to be in the position to support her financially as it will not be covered by insurance. Her back legs are slightly twisted and her stifle joints a little weak due to age. She has also had an abdominal hernia successfully repaired, as well as 2 teeth extracted, and is currently receiving treatment for itchy skin. She does not have very many teeth left and may benefit from a soft diet depending on her comfortability levels while eating.

Despite everything, Zinc’s spirit shines through, she’s affectionate, gentle, and loves nothing more than being close to her people. She will make a wonderful, devoted lap dog and a loyal companion for someone with a kind heart and the patience to help her enjoy her golden years in comfort and love.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

Many Tears Animal Rescue, Registered Charity No. 1192227Privacy PolicyCookie PolicyTerms & Conditions

name: Chocolate
description:
Chocolate is a gentle, sweet-natured girl who is discovering that the world isn’t quite as scary as she once thought. She can be shy at first and needs some time to build trust, but once she feels safe, she’ll start to come over for fuss and affection. Those moments make you feel like the luckiest person in the world.

Chocolate is looking for a calm, patient home with someone who will give her the time and reassurance she needs to continue growing in confidence. With a trusted companion by her side, this lovely little lady is sure to blossom into a devoted and affectionate friend.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Chocolate has previously been left home alone in her last home, so she may be able to do this again once she has settled in. However, due to how nervous she is around new people and in new environments, she will need refresher training to gradually build up the amount of time she is comfortable being left alone.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Chocolatehas reoccurring  medical conditions, namely a skin allergy and ear infection that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Chocolate would need to be the only pet in her new home

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Chocolate is a very timid and sensitive girl who needs plenty of patience, understanding, and respect for her boundaries. Once you become one of her trusted people, she forms a very strong bond and is described as incredibly affectionate. She loves following her favourite people around the house and enjoys nothing more than a cuddle and a snooze on the sofa. To help her feel safe and secure, we believe Chocolate would be happiest in an adult-only home or with older, dog-savvy children (aged 13 years and over) who can give her the time and space she needs to build her confidence at her own pace.
What type of environment would suit me? - Being such a nervous lady she will need her own secure garden with solid fencing in her new home. This safe space is essential, as she needs the space for decompression and to build confidence with her new family without pressure.

name: Ci Ci
description:
Ci Ci is a sweet, loving girl with a classic spaniel personality. She thrives on being out and about, exploring the world with her nose, and is happiest when she has the chance to sniff, investigate and stay busy.

Once Ci Ci has built a bond with you, her affectionate nature truly shines. She is playful and loves spending time with her people. Whether she’s curling up for a cuddle on the sofa or soaking up every bit of attention, Ci Ci enjoys being close to those she loves.

Ci Ci greets her favourite people with great enthusiasm and excitement. She can be bouncy when she’s happy to see you, and her joyful welcome is all part of her charming, loving personality.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support - Ci Ci struggles to be on her own so will need ongoing training around this to make her feel more comfortable.
Can I be left alone? No - Ci Ci can find being left alone worrying and quickly forms strong bonds with her people, struggling to be apart from them. She is looking for a home who can gradually build up her home alone time once she has settled in. This will need to be at her own pace.
Do I have any medical conditions? Existing conditions - Ci Cihas a current medical condition, she is going through a phantom pregnancy and is on some medication to help her through this, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Ci Ci could potentially live with small pets who are housed securely away from her.
Additional information - It’s Ci Ci’s time to shine, and she would love to be the only dog in the home where she can enjoy all the attention she deserves. She can be a worried girl around other dogs, choosing to not interact, especially around ones that are too in her face. She much prefers keeping herself to herself and being busy with spaniel sniffing when out and about!

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Due to Ci Ci’s sensitive nature and getting startled by some sudden noises, she is looking for a home with children aged 13 or over. Any children in the home or who visit should be calm, understanding and able to respect her space and boundaries, helping her feel safe and secure.
What type of environment would suit me? - Ci Ci will need a secure, private garden with 6ft fencing all around, as she can jump over lower fences.

name: Coco
description:
Coco is a cheeky little character with a quirky personality that’s sure to keep you entertained.

Once he gets to know you, he’s incredibly affectionate and loves to be on your lap getting fuss. Although he’s an older gentleman, he still enjoys getting out and about, and his face lights up with excitement every time he hears the magic word: “walkies!”

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - As he was a stray, we do not know if Coco has been left home alone before. Any alone time should be built up gradually, at a pace Coco is comfortable with, but he has the potential to do a few hours.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Coco has historic medical condition namely a Gait abnormality and a current medical conditions namely Brachycephalic airway obstruction syndrome (BOAS) Grade 1, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Coco can be unsure when he first sees other dogs which shows as barking. He has started some training around other dogs to help him feel more comfortable, which he’s doing well with and should be continued in his new home. Coco has been able to walk alongside calm dogs however, he would be happiest simply spending time with his known people.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Coco is looking for a home with older teenagers, 16+, with no visiting children. He would prefer a quieter home with minimal visitors, as he is very wary of new people and will need plenty of time, patience, and space from his new owner while he settles in. Once he has built a bond with his people, he becomes very affectionate and is happy to enjoy a fuss.
What type of environment would suit me? - Coco will need his new home to have its own private, secure garden so he’s got a safe space to explore and relax. It will also help give him regular toileting opportunities.

name: Daphne
description:
Daphne is a young lurcher with a big heart and an even bigger love for her people. She forms strong bonds with those she trusts and thrives on companionship, so she can find it difficult when left on her own.

One of Daphne’s favourite things in the world is zooming around at top speed, making a secure garden the perfect place for her to burn off energy and have fun. She loves playing with her toys, especially tossing them around and entertaining herself with a game.

Daphne can be a little shy when meeting new people and exploring unfamiliar places. The big wide world can sometimes feel overwhelming for her, so she is looking for a patient and understanding home that can continue building her confidence through positive training and encouragement.

With the right support, Daphne is sure to blossom into a happy, confident companion. She has so much love to give and will make a wonderful addition to a home that can offer her the time, reassurance, and affection she craves.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? No - Daphne struggles being left home alone, in her previous home her owners never left her and made sure there was always someone around. They worked hard with her so that she felt comfortable being in other rooms without them, but she will need someone at home all of the time while she has her training slowly worked on.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Daphnehas historic medical conditions, namely ear infections, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome Daphne and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Daphne can be quite worried of other dogs, if they’re in the distance she will stand and watch them before moving on. She is more relaxed around other long legged Sighthounds and with the right dog, can get playful. Daphne can walk with other calm dogs who will respect her space, but she will need time to build a relationship and she may not always want to walk with other dogs.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Daphne can live with teenagers, 13+. She is a nervous girl who is worried of people she doesn’t know but may choose to have a little sniff if she’s feeling brave. Once she’s been given time to build a relationship she gets very excited to see you and loves to have cuddles, but will take herself away when she’s had all the attention she wants.
What type of environment would suit me? - Daphne will need a secure garden with 6ft fencing so she’s got a safe space to burn off her energy, zoom around and play with her toys, it will also help for those days where she may not want to go on a walk. Daphne LOVES her toys! Soft toys are her favourite, she is quite independent with her play and usually takes her toys off to herself. She is very vocal when she plays and gets very growly- a typical Lurcher! Sometimes she will bring a toy over to you to play tug or fetch with.

name: Figgy
description:
Figgy is a fun-loving, lively girl with a wonderful personality and plenty of charm.

She’s incredibly affectionate and loves spending time with her people. Whether she’s out exploring new places or enjoying playtime with her favourite toys, Figgy approaches life with enthusiasm and joy.

She’s a clever girl who would thrive with a family committed to continuing her training and helping her reach her full potential.

Figgy is eager to learn and will reward her family with endless affection, laughter, and companionship.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Yes - Figgy has been able to be left for a couple of hours in her previous home. She could do so again after an initial settling period.
Do I have any medical conditions? None - Figgy has a clean bill of health and can be fully insured after rehoming.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Should be securely housed and be able to kept away from Figgy if necessary
Additional information - Figgy wishes to play with other dogs, however she can be over the top about it, which they don’t always appreciate. She is undergoing training around them which will need to be continued in her new home. She does have the potential to make some dog friends on walks as she has made a few here at Woodgreen. Any dogs she goes on walks with will need to be very well socialised and confident, so they can deal with her energetic enthusiasm.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Figgy can live with children aged 8+. She is a very bouncy girl but once she gets to know you she will show her sweet side, nuzzling into you and cuddling up on the sofa.
What type of environment would suit me? - Figgy will need a secure garden with 6ft fencing so she’s got a safe space to let out her energy with her favourite toys (frisbees, balls and cuddly toys), as well as continuing her training. She really loves digging, so having a sandpit or dig box available for her to use would be beneficial as an outlet.

name: Jerry
description:
Jerry has hung up his racing jacket and is now looking for a cosy bed where he can enjoy his well-earned retirement.

This sweet boy will politely say hello people and, once he gets to know you, will happily lean in for a fuss. Jerry enjoys gentle, leisurely walks, taking his time to explore the world around him at his own pace.

He’s ready to find a loving home where he can relax, snooze, and soak up all the affection he deserves.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs some support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - As Jerry previously lived in kennels, he has not been in a home before so he will need a settling in period, gradually increasing the time he is left home alone.
Do I have any medical conditions? None - Jerry has a clean bill of health and can be fully insured after rehoming.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? Yes
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - In his racing days, Jerry would regularly walk with other Greyhounds and previously got on well with them. As he hasn’t had much exposure to dogs outside of Greyhounds while in our care, he has found being around other breeds more challenging. He could potentially live with anothersimilar-sized sighthoundthat is calm and not overly boisterous, pending a successful on-site dog mix.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Jerry is incredibly affectionate with his people. He loves leaning against you for a fuss and resting his head on your knees for attention. Jerry has previously met teenagers and was calm and relaxed around them. Due to his size and bouncy nature, we feel that if Jerry were to live with children in his new home, he would be best suited to a home with children aged 11 years and over.
What type of environment would suit me? - Jerry will need his new home to have a secure, private garden with 6ft fencing, as he can jump very high. He loves playing with toys, especially soft toys, squeaky balls and footballs, and is happy to entertain himself by throwing them around, chasing them and batting them about.

name: Luigi
description:
Meet Luigi — our big brindle boy!

Luigi is a lovable young lad who’s still learning the ropes and taking in the world one adventure at a time. He thrives on being close to his people and absolutely loves heading out for exciting walks or having a good run off-lead in secure fields.

This handsome boy has a soft spot for toys, especially giant teddies he can proudly carry around and play with on his own. Enrichment and playtime make his tail wag like nothing else!

Luigi is hoping to find a home with someone who has experience or is willing to research his breed, be patient, committed and ready to continue the training he’s already started. With the right guidance, he’ll grow into the incredible companion he’s meant to be.

If you’re looking for a sweet, energetic, teddy-carrying buddy, Luigi might just be your perfect match!

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Yes - Luigi has been living in a foster home, where he has settled in well, and can comfortably be left home alone for up to 4 hours. Luigi has also shown great potential to continue developing by building more home alone with the right support and guidance in his new home.
Do I have any medical conditions? None - Luigi, on occasion, has been seen walking into things and is possibly long-sighted or short-sighted. This is something we’re not concerned with. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, whether historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Luigi can be conflicted when he sees dogs, some he will bark at, others he wants to meet (sometimes too eagerly). He’s responded brilliantly to his training since coming into us and we’ll show you the training we’ve started so it can be continued in his new home. He would prefer to be walked in quieter areas, although there is potential for him to socialise with appropriate dogs in the future.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - As Luigi came to us as a stray, we are unsure whether he has lived with children in a home environment before. With this in mind, along with his size and excitable nature, we would recommend that any children in his new home are aged 13 years or older. Luigi isn’t always sure how to feel when meeting new people. Sometimes he can be a little worried, while at other times he is a bouncy and enthusiastic boy. We are currently working on some training to help him stay calm when greeting people, and he is responding very well. With his favourite people, Luigi is just a big, heavy baby! He is very affectionate and loves attention, often leaning in for fuss and cuddles, especially if there’s a bum rub involved!
What type of environment would suit me? - Luigi is looking for a home with its own private, secure garden and fencing of at least 6ft. He loves nothing more than zooming around outdoors, burning off energy with a favourite soft toy in tow…… the bigger the toy, the better! Having a secure garden will not only give Luigi a safe space to play and relax, but will also support his ongoing training and provide him with some valuable off lead time.

name: Marnie
description:
Marnie is a sweet and friendly girl with people she is used to.

However she does find the world a bit of a worrying place to navigate so is looking for committed owners to help her build her confidence and be consistent with her training.

Marnie is a very strong girl and still very young so will get a lot bigger.

With caring and patient owners Marnie has the potential to thrive in a home.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs extensive support - Marnie will need a home visit as part of her rehoming requirements so any adopters will need to live within one hour of our Godmanchester centre.
Can I be left alone? Yes - Marnie is currently enjoying her time in a foster home where she has been left for a few hours. She should be able to do this again in her new home, once she’s settled in and had her training refreshed.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Marniehas previously had episodes of vomiting and alopecia which have resolved. This will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? Yes
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Marnie is a social girl with other dogs and gets excited when she see’s them, if she isn’t able to say hello she can become frustrated which shows as her barking. She is currently undergoing training around other dogs which she is making great progress with and will need to continue in her new home. Marnie could live with another dog in the home pending an dog mix on-site. If she lives with another dog they would need to be a similar size, confident, male dog to help her build confidence in her new home.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Marnie is looking for a calm, adult-only home, or one with older teenagers aged 16+ and no visiting children. She is a worried girl around new people and isn’t the sort of dog that would like to stop and greet people when she’s out and about. Once she gets to know you, true to her breed, she adores you and thinks she’s a lap dog. Her new family will need to be committed to continuing her training and supporting her ongoing development. She would be best suited to a quieter household and is unlikely to enjoy a busy environment.
What type of environment would suit me? - Marnie will need a secure, private garden in her new home so she has a safe space to build her confidence and let off any steam. She will need to be walked in quieter area’s so she can enjoy her walks.

name: Max
description:
Handsome Max is on the lookout for his forever family! This charming boy is searching for a patient and understanding home where he can settle in at his own pace and build confidence in his new surroundings.

Max hasn’t had the easiest start in life, but he’s ready to turn the page and begin an exciting new chapter filled with love, comfort, and plenty of adventures. While he may be a little reserved when meeting new people, give him some time and you’ll discover a loyal, affectionate companion who will be by your side through thick and thin.

Max is dreaming of a family who will help him explore the world, enjoy life’s simple pleasures, and most importantly, show him the love he’s always deserved.

In return, he’ll reward you with friendship, devotion, and lots of happy memories.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? No - Max currently finds being left home alone quite stressful, so he is looking for a family where someone is home for most of the day to help him settle in and build his confidence. With time, a consistent routine and gradual training, he may become more comfortable being left for short periods, but this is something that will need to be worked on once he has fully settled into his new home.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Maxhas historic medical conditions, namely an ear infection that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? Yes, conditionally - Max has lived with cats previously so he has the potential to again, but will need gradual and managed interactions
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Max can be worried around other dogs and would prefer to keep to himself on walks, much preferring to spend the time with his favourite people.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Max can live with children aged 11+. He can be worried of new people but with time and space, he will warm up. Once he’s got to know you he adores being with his favourite people and is a super affectionate boy who loves to play and be involved in whatever you’re doing.
What type of environment would suit me? - Max needs a fully enclosed garden with 6ft fencing where he is able to run off his zoomies and chase his tennis ball. It also gives him a comfortable, safe space for when he’s feeling a little worried of the outside world.

name: Molly
description:
Meet Molly, a sweet and sensitive girl searching for a gentle, understanding home.

She has all the classic Collie charm in her playful nature, but she also enjoys enrichment activities that keep her clever mind engaged.

Despite being an older lady, Molly certainly doesn’t act like it—she’s full of life and love, ready to share both with the right family.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support - Molly would thrive in a peaceful countryside setting with minimal noise and quiet places to walk, as she finds busy environments and the sounds of children overwhelming.
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Molly has been left home alone for shorter periods of time, but this was always with another dog. She should be able to do this again once she’s settled in and had her home alone training worked on.
Do I have any medical conditions? None - Molly has a clean bill of health and can be fully insured after rehoming.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? Yes
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Securely housed small pets
Additional information - Molly previously lived with another dog and really enjoyed the companionship, although she isn’t particularly interested in interacting with other dogs generally. She has walked alongside other dogs and is comfortable in their company, but tends to keep to herself rather than engage in play or interaction. Molly could live with a calm, confident dog who is similarly independent and not overly focused on interaction. While any relationship would likely develop gradually, there is potential for a lovely bond to grow over time.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Molly is looking for an adult only home with no visiting children. She is very worried of children in general and if on a walk, she will try to take herself away from them.
What type of environment would suit me? - Molly will need a private, secure garden that she can run around and play in. She cannot be in a home with neighbouring children or nearby a school/playing park due to her fear around children.

name: Mr Chips
description:
Meet Mr Chips!

“Say what you see!” If what you see is a handsome lad with a toy in his mouth, a sparkle in his eye, and a love of cheese, then you’ve got the answer… Mr Chips!

Mr Chips is a sweet-natured boy with a charming independent streak. He’s perfectly happy entertaining himself, and you could spend hours watching him launch his toys into the air, chase after them, and invent his own games. It’s impossible not to smile at his antics!

When it comes to food, “It’s good, but it’s not right!”… unless there’s cheese involved! Mr Chips is a true foodie, and tasty treats make him a brilliant training partner. He’s eager to learn, loves earning rewards, and is always ready to show off his clever side when snacks are on offer.

Like many sensitive souls, Mr Chips can sometimes find the world a little overwhelming. He’ll thrive with kind, patient people who can offer him reassurance when he needs it and help build his confidence at his own pace. Once he trusts you, you’ll have a loyal companion who’ll make every day brighter.

So, “What do points make?” A forever home! And Mr Chips is hoping his next answer is finding the family that’s just right for him.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - As he was a stray, we do not know if Mr Chips has been left home alone before. Any home alone time should be built up gradually, at a pace Mr Chips is comfortable with.
Do I have any medical conditions? None - This pet has a clean bill of health and can be fully insured after rehoming.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Mr Chips doesn’t appear to gain much from interacting with other dogs and prefers simply doing his own thing. For this reason, we feel Mr Chips would be happiest not greeting other dogs while out on walks. Instead, he will benefit from continuing his training to calmly disengage, walk past other dogs, and focus on enjoying his adventures with his people.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Mr Chips is friendly when meeting new people and will often approach with a waggy body and tail. However, he also has an independent side and isn’t constantly seeking attention. He loves spending time with his people and will happily ask for affection when he wants it, but he prefers fuss on his own terms and is happiest with people who respect when he’s had enough. He also enjoys learning and gets very excited when he’s rewarded for making good choices. If Mr Chips were to live with children in his new home, we would recommend that they are aged 13 or over.
What type of environment would suit me? - He will need his new home to have a secure, private garden where he can enjoy some off-lead zoomies and playtime. He loves toys, particularly flinging them around and zooming about with them. His favourites are definitely soft, squeaky teddies!

name: Otto
description:
Otto is a handsome Rottweiler cross with a heart as big as his smile. This charming boy is full of character and brings joy wherever he goes, thanks to his permanently waggy tail and loving nature.

Otto’s favourite thing in the world is spending time with his people. Whether he’s following you around the house, or simply relaxing by your side, he’s happiest when he’s close to those he loves. Affection is his love language.

When it comes to exercise, Otto enjoys gentle strolls where he can take in the sights and smells at his own pace. After a leisurely walk, you’ll usually find him curled up in a cosy spot, snoozing the day away and dreaming of his next adventure.

With his handsome looks, sweet temperament, and endless affection, Otto is sure to make a wonderful companion for a family looking for a loyal best friend. If you’re searching for a dog who will fill your home with love, companionship, and a constantly wagging tail, Otto could be the perfect match.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Due to being a stray we are unsure if Otto has been left in a home on his own before. He will need any home alone time built up gradually once he has settled in but he should be able to do a few hours.
Do I have any medical conditions? Existing conditions - Otto is currently experiencing an episode of diarrhoea. He has previously had an episode of dermatitis, which has now fully resolved. These will require consideration before rehoming. Our rehoming team will be able to provide further information about his medical history if you apply to rehome him and are identified as a suitable match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home. Due to the nature of the medical condition, a vet call with one of our onsite vets will be required before the pet goes home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Securely housed small pets
Additional information - Otto enjoys seeing other dogs, but if he can’t greet them he may show his frustration by barking. He is undergoing training for this which he’s doing really well with, and should continue in his new home. Otto would enjoy having some walking buddies in his new home. However, his enthusiasm can be a little overwhelming for some dogs, so any canine companions should be chosen with this in mind.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Otto can live with children aged 11+ who are used to a big, excitable boy. As he came to us a stray we are unsure if he has been around children previously. On foster he has met an 11 year old child in the home and was the same as he is with adults, friendly and happy to have fuss. He’s a very social boy who greets people with a waggy tail, when he’s out and about he’s happy to walk past people, enjoying his walk.
What type of environment would suit me? - Otto would like a secure, private garden in his new home.

name: Patch
description:
Patch is a young boy with a huge zest for life and enough enthusiasm to power a small city! Everything he does is turned up to maximum volume, if there’s fun to be had, Patch is all in.

One of Patch’s favourite things in the world is meeting people. He absolutely loves saying hello and will greet everyone as though they’re his long-lost best friend. His excitement can sometimes get the better of him, and he can be a bit of a bull in a China shop, often forgetting just how strong he is. Patch doesn’t have an off switch when it comes to enthusiasm, he’s wonderfully over-the-top in every sense!

Patch is a big fan of toys, although they rarely survive for very long. He takes great pride in thoroughly “testing” every toy he gets, and shredding them is one of his favourite hobbies. Tough toys are definitely recommended for this boy!

When he’s not busy destroying toys or making new friends, Patch loves being out and about. Walks are one of his favourite activities, especially when there’s plenty of time to stop and investigate all the exciting smells the world has to offer.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs some support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Patch has been left for a few hours in his previous home, any time home alone should be built up slowly once he’s settled in but he should be able to do a few hours again.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Pacthhas historic medical conditions, namely ear infections, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome Patch and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Securely housed small pets that he doesn't have access to
Additional information - When out on a walk Patch tends to ignore other dogs and walk past them. When Patch meets other dogs he can be a little over the top, which not all dogs will appreciate, so he would benefit from learning some manners around other dogs. We have found a play friend for him and they have enjoyed some time burning off energy together, so having dog friends in the future, who can tolerate his excitability, could be possible.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Patch can live with children aged 11+,  who are used to an excitable dog. He’s a friendly boy who loves meeting new people, but he has got an independent side to him.
What type of environment would suit me? - Patch would need a private garden with 6ft fencing so he’s got a safe space to burn off all his extra energy and play with his toys, especially tennis balls.

name: Phoebe
description:
Phoebe is a sweet girl who needs someone that can help build her confidence when out and about.

She is very training minded and showing real progress in a short amount of time. Anyone would be lucky to call Phoebe a friend she is affectionate and happy to see you at every moment, she just wants to spend time with you, share space with you.

She loves food, this makes training that little bit easier.

Phoebe has the sweetest of faces and is beautiful inside and out.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Yes - Phoebe has been left home alone for around half a day in her previous home, she should be able to do this again after a settling in period and having her training refreshed.
Do I have any medical conditions? None - Phoebe has a current medical condition, namely she is having physio to strengthen her foot, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Phoebe is unsure around other dogs which can show as her barking at them. She is undergoing training for this which she is doing well with and should be continued in her new home. Phoebe would benefit from owners who are committed to continuing her training and socialisation to help build her confidence around dogs in public and in time she can have some walking buddies who won’t be worried by her over the top play style.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Phoebe can live with children aged 11+. She can be worried of new people but letting her approach when she’s ready, she will soon warm up. Once she’s got to know you she gets very excited to see her people. She’s currently in a foster home where she is enjoying her home comforts and if she can’t finish a treat, she’ll hide it for later. Her fosterers have heard her having a cheeky midnight snack.
What type of environment would suit me? - Phoebe will need a secure, private garden in her new home. She does enjoy digging so she would benefit from a sandpit. She can be worried by things she hasn’t come across before but with some reassurance she will continue her walk. We have been doing some scent work with her on walks to help build her confidence and she has been enjoying watching the world go by.

name: Rocco
description:
Rocco is a super handsome gentleman with a big silly attitude. He lights up every room with his wiggly and enthusiastic greetings.

Rocco loves to soak up endless amounts of love from everyone he meets but is especially gentle and sweet with the people close to him.

He is also partial to a belly rub and sticks his little tongue out when he is really relaxed!

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs some support
Can I be left alone? Yes - Rocco has been left home alone for a full working day and was settled during this time. This longest he’s been left and it was after a good walk so he slept for the duration of the time alone. He does best when he’s left with mental stimulation toys or after having physical exercise. He will need time to settle into his new home and his training refreshed before being left for so long.
Do I have any medical conditions? Existing conditions - Roccohas current medical conditions, namely a luxating patellar, cherry eye and an issue with his skin, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home. Due to the nature of the medical condition, a vet call with one of our onsite vets will be required before the pet goes home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Securely housed small pets that are kept separately
Additional information - Rocco is a friendly boy when meeting other dogs, he can be a typical Bulldog and be a bit intense with his greetings but he’s generally very respectful of other dogs. Once he’s said hello he’s happy to continue his walk and do his own thing. Rocco would love some walking buddies in his new home but doesn’t want them to visit him in the home.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Rocco can live with children aged 11+. He can be a little cautious of new people, but with a bit of time, he soon warms up for some fuss. Rocco has shown good manners around children he has met, but as he was a stray, we do not know if he has lived with them before. Rocco takes his rest very seriously and needs respect when he’s sleeping and to be left alone.
What type of environment would suit me? - Rocco really enjoys snuffling for treats scattered in grass and messing around with a football so he will need a secure, private garden in his new home. Rocco also loves toys, either a ball to chew or a game of tuggy, if you initiate it.

name: Ruby
description:
Ruby is a beautiful girl with a heart full of love and plenty of slobbery kisses to share once you’re part of her club.

She can be a little nervous when meeting new people, but with patience and kindness she forms incredibly rewarding bonds and becomes the most loyal companion.

Watching Ruby grow in confidence and seeing her affectionate personality shine through is the best way to light up any day. She is looking for a fun and loving home where she can show off her goofy personality.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Ruby came into us as a stray so we’re unsure if she’s been left home alone before. Any home alone time will need to be built up, at Ruby’s pace, once she’s settled into her new home.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Rubyhas historic medical conditions, namely an allergic skin disorder, an ear infection (resolved) and dermatitis around her feet (resolved), that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home. Due to the nature of the medical condition, a vet call with one of our onsite vets will berequiredbefore the pet goes home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? Yes, conditionally - A bold, dog savvy cat
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Securely housed small pets
Additional information - Ruby enjoys walking with other dogs and is generally happy to do her own thing once she’s said hello. Her greetings can be a little intense at first, so she would suit calm, confident canine companions who won’t be worried by her enthusiasm. Ruby can have some polite walking buddies in her new home, particularly dogs who are happy to coexist alongside her as she isn’t a fan of overly excitable dogs.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Ruby can live with teenagers, 13+. She is a worried girl and is looking for a family that can let her come out of her shell in her own time. Despite her size, she’s not a rough and tumble type of girl, much preferring calmer interactions and fuss. Once you’ve built a relationship with her she is the sweetest girl!
What type of environment would suit me? - Ruby will need a secure, private garden with 6ft fencing in her new home so she’s got a space to be off lead and relax, and to help with her toilet training.

name: Sally
description:
Meet Sally – a gentle, mature lady who enjoys her tasty snacks and leisurely walks. She loves relaxing in a comfy spot and taking in the world around her.

Although she’s an older lady, she recently surprised everyone by making an impressive jump over a baby gate!

Sally may enjoy a slower pace these days, but she still has plenty of spirit and determination.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs some support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Sally came to us as a stray so we do not know what she is like home alone. Any home alone time will need to be built up gradually at her pace once she’s settled in, but she has the potential to do a few hours.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Sally has a historic medical condition, namely blood in urine which was resolved, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Sally is very polite when meeting other dogs but she doesn’t seek out interactions with them. She can walk with other calm dogs who like her, are happy to just enjoy the walk rather than a playmate. Sally is a lone ranger and doesn’t need canine companionship to be her best self.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Sally can live with children aged 8+. She can be a little nervous around new people and takes time to build a relationship. Once she’s settled she takes things in her stride and has an independent side to her.
What type of environment would suit me? - Sally will need a secure, private garden with 6ft fencing. This will give her a safe space to explore and sniff at her own pace, and also help with her housetraining. She’s often found sunbathing in her kennel.

name: Sean Paul
description:
“Gimme the Light”… and a little time, and you’ll see what a lovely boy Sean Paul really is.

When Sean Paul first arrived, he wasn’t quite ready to “Get Busy”. New things can still feel a little overwhelming, but every day his confidence grows. He’s recently discovered that walks can actually be pretty fun, and he’s starting to enjoy getting out and about with his trusted humans by his side.

The best part of Sean Paul’s day? Seeing his people. The moment they walk into the room, his excitement goes up like the “Temperature”. His tail starts wagging, his eyes light up, and he can’t wait to say hello.

This little guy loves his toys and enjoys having a garden where he can run, play and just be a dog. Give him a toy and some space to explore, and you’ll see his playful personality shine through.

Sean Paul is looking for a patient home that understands that confidence isn’t built overnight. He needs people who will move at his pace, celebrate the small victories, and remember that sometimes “We Be Burnin'” through those fears one tiny step at a time.

In return, you’ll get a loyal little companion who loves his people with his whole heart. He may not arrive ready to be the life of the party, but with time, kindness and understanding, Sean Paul will show you exactly why he’s worth the wait.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Sean Paul came to us as a stray so we do not know if he has been left alone before. He will need his home alone training worked on once he’s settled into his new home and has the potential to do a few hours.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Sean Paulhas historic medical conditions, namely ear infections which have been resolved, that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - When Sean Paul came into us he was quite worried around other dogs, so we have been working on helping him gain his confidence around them. He has made good progress with this and is happy to walk near other dogs, but he prefers not to interact with them. Sean Paul’s new owners will need to continue his training around other dogs and be understanding of his needs. With ongoing support, he may become more confident around them, although he may always prefer to keep to himself.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Sean Paul can live with children aged 11+. He can be a worried boy at times and prefers to approach people in his own time. Once he’s got to know you he gets very excited to see his favourite people and enjoys some gentle fuss.
What type of environment would suit me? - Sean Paul will need a private, secure garden to allow him to explore and play. He has recently learnt how to play tuggy and loves soft, squeaky toys!

name: Slim Shady
description:
Meet Slim Shady, a lively young lurcher who’s full of beans. Slim is a fun and affectionate boy who is in training to become the best boy. He is energetic, cheeky and learning to navigate the world with the help of his friends. Slim enjoys making friends with people and medium to large dogs. Mr shady also loves soft toys to throw around and play with.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - As Slim came to us a stray, we are not sure how he will act when home alone. Any home alone time will need to be built up gradually once settled in a new environment.
Do I have any medical conditions? Existing conditions - Slim shady has an abnormal gait , that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? No
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Slim can be quite over excitable with other dogs so will need some on going training in how to be polite around other dogs.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - We believe that Slim could live with children aged 13 years + who are confident and used to large bouncy dogs.  Older teenage children will be able to get involved with his training.
What type of environment would suit me? - Slim will need his own garden that is fully enclosed with 6 ft fencing around. This will help with toilet training and would be very nice to have an off lead place to run around.

name: Terry & Blu
description:
Meet Terry and Blu — a wonderful duo with so much love to give.

Blu is not only Terry’s best friend but also his loyal guide dog. Together, they make an inseparable team. Terry doesn’t need his eyes to see the world — his incredible senses tell him everything he needs to know. Although Terry is a vocal boy, with Blu by his side, he navigates life with confidence, curiosity, and plenty of personality. He loves nothing more than sniffing around, exploring his surroundings, and being close to his people.

Blu also loves being out exploring, enjoying some play and then soaking in all the love.

Together, Terry and Blu are a truly special pair, full of love, trust, and companionship.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs some support
Can I be left alone? Yes - Terry and Blue have been able to be left comfortably for 3 hours in their foster home. Any home alone time will need to be refreshed in their new home but they should be able to do a few hours again.
Do I have any medical conditions? Existing conditions - Terry has historic medical conditions, namely blindness as his eyes have been removed. He has also had episodes of coughing and a cyst that was removed. Terry will require a dental once he has been rehomed. This will be covered by Woodgreen and performed at our surgery. This will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. Blu has historic medical conditions, namely having Brachycephalic Airway Obstruction Syndrome (BOAS) Grade 1. She has also had an ear infection, and a ulcer on the eye which have been resolved. She did have a benign mass that was removed. This will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? Yes, conditionally - A dog savvy, bold cat following correct introductions
Can I live with small pets? Yes, conditionally - Securely housed small pets
Additional information - Terry gets excited when hearing other dogs on his walks, but since his eye operation, he hasn’t met any other dogs. Blu isn’t very interested in other dogs but is happy to say a quick hello before moving on. Terry and Blu are happy to have some calm walking buddies that like them, are happy to enjoy the sights and smells of their walk rather than a playmate.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Terry and Blu can live with children aged 8+ that can respect and understand that Terry is blind. They are an affectionate pair who love being around their favourite people. When Terry hears you he will run over for some fuss and relies on his nose to get around.
What type of environment would suit me? - Terry and Blu will need to have a secure private garden of their own. Due to Terry being completely blind this will be a nice safe space for him to explore.

name: Winnie
description:
Winnie is a sweet, affectionate girl who blossoms once she knows and trusts you. Once you’re part of her inner circle, she’ll shower you with kisses, greet you with her ever-wagging tail, and happily soak up all the cuddles she can get.

She loves spending quality time with her people, whether that’s snuggling up on the sofa, trotting happily alongside you on walks, or enjoying the occasional play session with her favourite toys. Winnie has a gentle, loving nature and thrives on companionship.

If you’re looking for a loyal best friend who will fill your days with love, cuddles, and happy tail wags, Winnie could be the perfect addition to your family.

Support and Health:
How much training will I need? Needs ongoing support
Can I be left alone? Potentially, once settled - Winnie used to be able to do a few hours home alone, but this wasn’t too regularly. We feel she will likely be able to do a few hours home alone once settled into her new home, but it will need to be built up gradually at a pace she’s happy with.
Do I have any medical conditions? History of - Winniehas historical medical conditions, namely Otitis Externa and Flea Bite Hypersensitivity that will require consideration before this pet is rehomed. Our rehoming team will be able to give you further information if you apply to rehome them and you are a successful match. We would also advise that any medical conditions, be that historic or ongoing, are discussed with your own vet prior to taking them home.

Who I can live with:
Can I live with dogs? No
Can I live with cats? Yes, conditionally - Winnie previously lived with 2 cats that were introduced to her slowly. She has the potential to live with cats again pending correct and gradual introductions.
Can I live with small pets? No
Additional information - Winnie much prefers the company of her people, so will need to be the only dog in the home. She can be unsure around other dogs out and about and is happiest when given space to keep her distance.

Family and environment:
What kind of family am I looking for? - Winnie is a worried girl around handling and will need her space respecting. For this reason, any children in her next home will need to be 11 years plus, who will be understanding of these sensitives.
What type of environment would suit me? - Winnie is looking to have a secure garden to call her own to play in and relax.

name: Mora
description:
Mora is a medium English Setter, 7 years old.

Mora is one of four setters who was given up by their hunter when he became unwell and was no longer able to look after them.

She is a very energetic, confident and happy girl.

She lives with four other setters and they all get on really well together, sharing their kennel and all eating together.

Mora is very playful and is always happy play with the other dogs.

She is very affectionate and always eager for a fuss and a cuddle.

She comes running when she’s called around the shelter, although this will no doubt be different on an exciting walk!

She can live with older children and can be cat tested for the right home.

name: Simba
description:
Simba is a medium mixed breed, 8 years old.

Simba turned up in a village on his own, nobody knew where he was come from but the rescue that took him in say he was in such a state he ‘must have lived through hell’.

He arrived very skinny but is putting on weight but sadly had to have half his tail amputated due to a bad infection.

Despite whatever his past may hold, Simba is learning to enjoy life again, rolling in the grass and living with other dogs.

He gets on well with all the male and female dogs he lives with and he’s also passed his cat test, meaning he could live in a home with a resident cat as well.

name: Sky
description:
Sky is a medium English Setter, 5 years old.

Sky’s story, as with so many of our dogs, didn’t begin in the easiest way. Rescued from a hunter in Spain, she arrived at the shelter frightened, overwhelmed and unsure whether people could be trusted.

After spending some time there, one of our wonderful foster homes in Essex offered to foster her in November 2025 and that’s when her new life truly began.

While she is still naturally shy and a little sensitive, every month has brought new milestones. Watching her confidence blossom has been incredibly rewarding, and she’s now ready to find the forever home that will help her continue her journey.

Sky shares her foster home with several resident dogs, and they have been her greatest teachers. She absolutely thrives in their company and has learned that life can be fun.

One of her favourite games is playing tag with the resident English Setter – where she has gone from always being the one chased to proudly becoming the chaser herself! It’s a lovely reminder of just how far she has come.

Although Sky remains nervous around new people and unfamiliar situations, once she feels safe her affectionate nature shines through.

She loves gentle fuss and will often quietly inch closer for a cuddle. When she’s completely relaxed, she’ll happily flop onto her back, inviting you to rub her tummy, a privilege reserved for those who have earned her trust.

Like many sensitive dogs, Sky appreciates having the choice to retreat to her own safe space when she needs a little quiet time.

Giving her the freedom to move at her own pace has been key to her progress, and her future family will need to continue offering her that patience and understanding.

Sky is looking for a calm, quiet and kind home with people who won’t expect too much too soon. A confident, friendly resident dog is essential, as they will give her reassurance and confidence.

Sky will celebrate her sixth birthday this September, and nothing would make us happier than seeing her spend it in the forever home she has waited so patiently to find.

name: Benji
description:
Benji is a small to medium sized crossbreed aged around nine years old. Benji fostered Fakenham Norfolk.

Benji was originally adopted from us a few years ago and has been very well loved and cared for. Sadly he has recently had to be very reluctantly returned due to insurmountable personal reasons.

Benji is a very sweet natured and a quiet dog. he’s no trouble whatsoever. His owners will need to be with him most of the time, as he can be anxious when being left on his own for more than a couple of hours.

He can live with respectful children aged eight plus.

Benji gets on with most dogs out and about but there are a few he takes exception to. He has lived happily as the only dog. Benji loves people, he walks well on the lead enjoy his walks.

When you adopt a Safe Rescue dog, you MUST use a slip lead. This will keep your dog safe: your new dog will be nervous and will not trust you, and you will not know which situations might upset your dog.

If your dog panics, then a slip lead is the only way to prevent your dog from escaping (many dogs can escape from a collar and/or harness). It will take AT LEAST 3-6 months for your dog to settle-in and for you to know your dog fully (longer for nervous dogs).

The slip lead must ALWAYS be used during this settling-in period. Even after your dog is settled, it is safest to use the slip lead in situations where your dog may become scared (e.g. visiting new places, around unfamiliar people, at the vet), and it situations where unexpected triggers might happen (e.g. around bonfire night).

Nervous dogs may always need to wear a slip-lead as a back-up safety measure.

The slip lead is a safety device and must NEVER be used as a training tool. Using the lead to apply pressure to the dog’s neck is damaging. If your dog pulls on the lead, then we can advise you on training methods that avoid harm.

Once your dog is settled, you may want to consider using a harness (together with the slip lead) if your dog is comfortable with being handled when it is fitted.

Most harnesses are not escape-proof, but harnesses with a strap behind the ribcage (e.g. Ruffwear Webmaster or Perfect Fit Harnesses) are safer.

Retractable / extendable leads must never be used on our dogs.

Adopted dogs must be collected from the rescue and transported straight home in a crate.

Fences and gates must be 5foot minimum in height and secure.

Gertie is a large crossbreed aged around five years old. Gertie fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Gertie came to the UK from a squalid shelter in Romania. She is a big girl but calm and placid around the home. Gertie gets on well with other dogs and ignores the safe rescue cat. She walks nicely on a lead.

She would prefer an adult family – but visiting children is fine. Large breed experience preferred.

name: Lyric
description:
Lyric is a medium sized crossbreed aged around four years old. Lyric fostered Norwich Norfolk.

We discovered this absolute beauty queen in the Botosani public shelter when we visited in March. It’s taken a few weeks for her turn to come to arrive in the UK, she’s here now and ready for a new life.

Lyric is a sweet and gentle girl with a cheeky side that’s just beginning to emerge.

Once settled she does tend to love her human a little too much and can become possessive/protective over them if allowed to and will therefore need an adopter who is experienced with shepherd types or similar natured dogs.

Lyric is living very happily with multiple other dogs in her foster home but could become bossy if allowed to “rule the roost”.

She can live with dog savvy, confident cats but would be likely to chase more timid or skittish cats. She needs an adult home environment and would be best suited to a single person or couple.
