name: Tilly
description:
Tilly – 2-year-old Cockapoo girl (approx. 10kg)

Currently being fostered in Gloucestershire in an adult family home with multiple dogs.

Tilly was originally homed by Starfish as a puppy and has recently come back into our care due to a change in her owner’s circumstances.

This is absolutely no reflection on lovely little Tilly, who has done nothing wrong. We are heartbroken for her, as returns are something that very rarely happen within our rescue.

When Tilly first arrived, our hearts broke for her. For several days she sat by the door waiting, barely eating and seeming very sad and withdrawn.

It was clear she missed everything she had known and was struggling to understand why her world had suddenly changed.

Her coat was also in poor condition and our wonderful groomer spent nearly three hours carefully clipping it all off. Throughout the entire process, Tilly was incredibly patient and good.

Fast forward a couple of weeks and we now have a very different Tilly – a happy, confident little girl who has settled beautifully into a home with six resident dogs of different ages, sizes and personalities without a single issue.

She has even attended agility classes and proved to be a natural!

There is honestly very little negative to say about this lovely dog.

“Tilly is the easiest dog I’ve fostered to date. She met my six dogs all at once, which can be intimidating, but took it all in her stride. She is affectionate and enjoys human company, but has also learnt to settle happily when a person is not in the room.

She loves routine, enjoys her two walks a day and is a bright little dog who would thrive in a home where she can continue to use her brain. She is inquisitive, confident and wants to say hello to every person and dog she meets.

She settles well at night and sleeps through without any problems.

She walks nicely on the lead and although interested in wildlife, she has not been overly fixated on it. She has not yet been let off lead, so we cannot comment on her recall.

One thing she does go deaf for, however, is a particularly interesting smell she wants to investigate or roll in!

She is pretty much the perfect dog. I feel she would be happiest in a home with another dog and ideally more than one person around, ensuring she receives plenty of companionship and attention.”

We know Tilly will be extremely popular. However, as you can imagine, we are very protective of this special little girl.

We watched her arrive feeling lost and confused, and we have seen her blossom back into the happy, joyful puppy we remember from two years ago.

Because of this, we will not compromise when it comes to finding her the right forever home.

Tilly has been spayed, vaccinated, microchipped, wormed and flea treated.

Below are various videos showing Tilly’s journey with us so far. ??

name: Sombra
description:
Sombra is a 5-year-old female Podenco Maneto. She is a small-sized dog with a friendly and sociable nature. While she enjoys spending time with people, she also has an independent side that allows her to be content in her own company.

She loves going for walks and enjoys exploring her surroundings. Sombra is inquisitive when out and about and likes meeting new people. Her curious personality means she takes an interest in the world around her without being overwhelming.

Sombra is not reactive and is well behaved on the lead. She walks nicely and remains calm when encountering different situations.

Her good manners make her a pleasant companion for daily outings and adventures.

She is good with other dogs and could happily share her home with canine companions. Sombra could also live with dog-savvy children aged 10 years and over.

Her friendly temperament and balanced nature help make her a wonderful family dog.

Sombra is kennel clean and has all the qualities of a loving family companion. She is neutered, inoculated and microchipped. She is available for meet and greets by appointment from the rescue premises in Wistaston, Cheshire.

Sombra is ready to find a caring home where she can enjoy companionship, walks and family life.

name: Dante
description:
Dante is a 6-year-old male Cross-Breed. He is a medium-sized dog who is described as handsome, loving and affectionate. Dante is also obedient and quick to learn, making him a wonderful companion for a family looking for a friendly and sensible dog.

He is very good on the lead and has no known fears. Dante is sociable with all dogs and enjoys being around other canine companions.

His happy nature and confident personality help him settle well into different situations.

Dante is kennel clean and has no health issues reported. He is a cheerful and well-balanced dog who appears to take life in his stride.

He could live with other dogs in his new home and would be suitable for families with dog-savvy children aged 12 years and over. Consideration may also be given to families with younger children.

His loving and sensible nature suggests he would enjoy being part of an active and caring household.

Dante has been neutered, inoculated and microchipped. He is available for meet and greets by appointment from the rescue premises in Wistaston, Cheshire. He is a happy, affectionate and sociable dog who is ready to find a loving forever home.

name: Dora
description:
Dora is a medium English Setter, 11 years old.

Dora is a beautiful older girl who has found herself looking for a new home after being surrendered simply because she is now considered too old to hunt.

But don’t let her age fool you – Dora still has plenty of love, energy, and joy to share.

With people, Dora can be a little shy at first as she takes a moment to feel safe with new faces.

However, once she gets to know you, she quickly blossoms into a cheerful, affectionate, and loving companion.

Dora is still quite active but would love to find a home where she can enjoy gentle exercise, companionship, and a comfortable sofa.

Currently in Spain, transport available to the UK.

name: Boss
description:
Boss is a medium English Setter, 10 years old

Boss is a charming older gentleman who is looking for a loving home after being given up due to his age.

For his age, Boss is still active and enjoys stretching his legs and running in the enclosure, especially alongside his friend Dora. The two share a lovely bond and make a delightful pair.

Boss loves his food and has a great appetite. He has a calm, balanced nature and is incredibly affectionate with people, always seeking out attention.

He absolutely adores cuddles making him a perfect companion for anyone looking for a loving, loyal friend.

Boss would thrive in a home where he can enjoy both relaxed companionship snuggled on a sofa, and some gentle walks.

Currently in Spain, transport available to the UK.

name: Zeus
description:
Zeus is a medium English Setter, 6 years old.

Zeus is a gentle, loving boy who is now thriving in one of our amazing foster homes in Kent.

Since arriving in the UK at the end of May, he has settled in beautifully. He has done really well with toilet training with no accidents in the home.

He is learning to walk well on the lead without pulling, and loves a bit more freedom on a long line in the park. However he is likely to take a step or two backwards with training when moving home.

Zeus is sociable with other dogs and currently lives with two resident dogs. He particularly enjoys the company of the female setter and loves to play in the garden with her followed by a snuggle on the sofa.

We think he would be fine as an only dog or happy to have a playmate in his new home.

His foster family describe him as an absolute gem. A sweet natured and incredibly affectionate boy, who is eating well and adapting steadily to family life.

He instantly fell in love with the foster family’s daughter when she returned home from university and has shown what a wonderful family dog he can be. He is especially gentle and affectionate with her.

Zeus may be six, but he’s got loads of energy for running and playing and then, in true setter style, settles beautifully at home.

name: Reyna
description:
Reyna is a medium Border Collie, 9 years old.

Reyna is a beautiful Border Collie in foster in Cambridgeshire, her passport says she is 9 years old, although her vet believes she may actually be younger.

Reyna came into the shelter with her three puppies, Ares, Sena and Zeus, after being surrendered by a family who had kept her outdoors for her entire life.

We believe she may have spent much of that time chained up, with very little opportunity to interact with people, other dogs or the world around her.

Her puppies are all in wonderful homes now, and although Reyna is going from strength to strength, we would love to find her a home of her own.

When Reyna first arrived at the shelter, she was extremely frightened and overwhelmed by everything around her.

Reyna was at the shelter for a few months before one of our wonderful foster homes offered to foster her in June 2025 and Reyna has been there since.

She shares her home with several resident dogs and, despite likely having very limited socialisation in her previous life, she has learned so much from them. She had many fears and insecurities and found it difficult to trust people.

Since then, she has made wonderful progress and now enjoys gentle attention and affection from those she knows.

However, she is still a sensitive girl who will need a patient, understanding home where she can continue to build her confidence at her own pace.

Once she feels safe and familiar with someone, she is incredibly sweet, affectionate and eager to be close to them.

Although she was very nervous when she first arrived in foster care, Reyna has slowly but surely started to come out of her shell.

With the support of her foster family and input from a behaviourist, she has even enjoyed some small adventures, including trips to a local café.

While she can still be shy around new people, she is gentle and affectionate once she feels comfortable. Her foster mum is undoubtedly her favourite person, especially as she’s the one who takes her on all her walks!

As her confidence grows, Reyna’s personality is beginning to shine through. She has a playful, cheeky side and can be quite the little character.

Watching her discover new experiences and learn that the world can be a safe and enjoyable place has been incredibly rewarding.

Reyna is an extremely special girl looking for a kind, patient and understanding family who can continue the fantastic work started by her foster home.

With time, reassurance and gentle encouragement, we believe she will continue to flourish and be an incredible loving member of the family to someone.

Reyna currently lives with a cat, so can do again providing they both have their own space and adopters taken introductions slowly. She would do best in a home without young children. She can live with other dogs or as an only dog.

name: Kane
description:
Welcome the handsome and friendly gentleman, Kane. Kane is a 5 year old male Black Labrador Retriever with a medium build, weighing around 20–25 kilograms.

Kane has been in the shelter for over a year now. His best friend, Kahu, was finally adopted last month and now Kane is left behind. They had been together for the past year so now, more than ever, Kane needs to find his happy ever after.

Kane is a good, well-rounded boy and can already respond to basic commands. He is good with dogs of all shapes and sizes and relishes the opportunity for attention and a way to burn off energy.

He is super cuddly and will be a wonderful sofa dog, but will also require decent walks and new adventures.

Kane will need ongoing basic training and some patience to help him settle into home life. He is your typical Labrador Retriever – super friendly with everyone he meets, excitable, and food-oriented.

This lovely boy will suit most home settings – all he asks for is affection, nice walks, a roof over his head, and regular meals. And he will shower you with love.

We have no doubt that he will be the most wonderful companion.

name: Captain
description:
Captain is a 2 year old male Cross-Breed who was abandoned but still hasn’t lost his faith in love.

We may never know his full story — maybe his family moved away, or maybe they just decided they no longer wanted him.

Whatever the reason, he was left behind: alone, confused, and heartbroken.

And yet… Captain still shines. He remains hopeful, gentle, and ready to love with his whole heart.

He hasn’t let his past break him, but he’s longing for something real — a forever home, a family who will never walk away again. Could that be you?

Rescue backup: Saving Souls Animal Rescue – UK Registered Charity (1209645)

Please share Captain’s story — you might be the reason his forever family finds him.

name: Demis
description:
Demis is a 2-year-old male Pointer Cross currently waiting in Cyprus for his forever home in the UK. This gorgeous boy is bursting with love, energy, and the potential to be someone’s perfect companion.

Friendly and affectionate, Demis thrives on human connection and would do well either as the only dog or living alongside a resident dog. He’s all about that family life—he just hasn’t found his yet.

He still needs some basic training and socialisation, especially when it comes to walking nicely on the lead (yep, he’s a bit of a puller). But don’t let that fool you—he’s not destructive and can be left alone for short periods without turning your house into a war zone.

A spacious garden where he can zoom around would be his absolute dream.

Demis is suitable for homes with children aged 12 and up, and with the right guidance, he’s going to be an incredible addition to the family.

He’ll come vaccinated, travel-ready, and with full rescue back-up, because this boy isn’t just being shipped off—he’s being set up for success. If you’re up for a bit of training and a whole lot of love, Demis could be your guy.

name: Russett
description:
Russett – 4 year old male Cross-Breed is medium in size weighing twenty-two kilograms.

What a cool guy Russett is! From the moment he entered the shelter as a stray, he was a perfect shelter dog. Such striking and handsome markings, many comment on just how handsome a dog he is too, but there’s more to him than just a pretty face.

He has a calm personality, other dogs are drawn to him for this reason.

A gentle character, full of strength though and maturity and confidence in himself.

The shelter team say Russett thinks he’s a lapdog! Climbing onto staff and visitors’ laps to get as much love as possible. Hugely cuddly and loving, Russett struggles sometimes to recognise personal space! He adores the attention when he gets it.

The shelter team describe him as a very good all-round dog.

He’s a great boy with other dogs, he prefers those who are more calm in nature as he is.

He’s loving and hugely sociable with everyone he meets.

Russett is quite food orientated which is great for training.

He enjoys any interactions where there’s a treat involved!

Pulls a little on walks but soon calms down.

He doesn’t have high energy levels, in fact he would be happy with a nice walk then a snooze, followed by some cuddles on the sofa. His needs aren’t great or demanding.

Russett would enjoy a household that had a chilled feel about it like him. He’d be happy to share his space with another dog of a similar energy level, maybe even living with older dogs would suit him.

Russett would be the happiest boy if he had humans around him who realised his greatest need in life is to be loved by his people. Tactile, loving and affectionate adopters are a must for this boy.

Only what he deserves now after patiently waiting for the right people to find him.

Rescue back up by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply.Homecheck applies.Fully vaccinated and tested/neutered.He has his passport/ready to fly!

name: Dave
description:
Meet delicious Dave — the cuddly charmer from Cyprus! Dave is a 1 year old male Labrador Retriever Cross, medium-sized at around 20 kilograms. A firm favourite of the shelter team, this little fella hasn’t put a paw wrong since he arrived as a stray.

Hugely loving, immensely cuddly, and incredibly affectionate — Dave’s capacity to love knows no bounds! This doesn’t just extend to his human companions but to his furry friends too.

Dave seeks out kisses and cuddles from anyone willing to share them, nuzzling into people’s shoulders for a proper full-body hug.

With his doggy companions, he’s equally sociable and kind — welcoming new dogs to the shelter with open paws, initiating play with those who want to, or sitting calmly with the ones who are a bit unsure.

Dave is such a great all-round kid — balanced and happy in equal measures.

His ideal home would be an active one that understands he’s young, playful, and full of life. He’ll thrive with physical and mental stimulation, and a large secure garden is a must — he’s known to be a bit of an escape artist! In a loving home, that urge may fade, but high fencing will still be important just in case Dave decides to test his skills.

Dave would be happy living with another dog or as an only dog. A companion dog would be lovely for him, but he’d also enjoy meeting other dogs for playtime.

He could live with children over 10 years old and hasn’t been cat tested.

Dave is young, with medium energy levels, and like most shelter rescues, will benefit from further training. The rewards, though, will be plentiful — Dave will give back a lifetime of love. Seems like a fair exchange to us!

Rescue back-up by Saving Soula Animal Rescue, a UK-registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply. Home check required. Fully vaccinated, neutered, passport ready, and all set to fly!

name: Seiko
description:
How has a 6 year old pure bred akita ended up in shelter for the past 6 months 🙁 He’s a lovely, friendly boy who does not deserve this so late in life.

Given his nature we can only assume he was well-loved before, but not quite loved enough to be cared for his whole life. This is particularly heartbreaking given how fiercely loyal akitas are.

His name is Seiko and he is ready to be adopted, fostered, or we will consider foster with a view to adopt.

He is your typical akita – affectionate and wants attention for himself. For that reason he is best homed as an only dog.

Seiko is a well-rounded boy from the small experiences he’s had since arriving – calm at the vets, travels well, walks well on the leash, takes treats gently.

All Seiko asks for is a comfy bed, nice walks, and someones love and attention. It’s best for any home to be experienced with a larger breed or even with the akita breed.

Please let’s not let Seiko waste any more of his short life caged up. He deserves to know love again after being heartlessly abandoned.

name: Billy and Blackjack
description:
Billy is a one year old male Labrador Retriever Cross and Blackjack is his two year old brother, also a Labrador Retriever Cross. These bonded boys are inseparable and are looking for a forever home together in the UK.

Are you searching for loyal companions to fill your home with love and adventure? Meet Billy and Blackjack, two deeply bonded Labrador Crosses, both rescued from the streets of Cyprus and now waiting for that special person or couple who can take them in together.

These remarkable dogs are more than just pets—they’re best friends who have journeyed through hardship side by side. Most likely brothers, their connection is unbreakable; they refuse to be parted, always looking out for one another.

After being rescued from neglect, their gentle natures have emerged, and they now greet each day with growing trust and hope.

• A large, secure garden for play and exploration• An active lifestyle, as the boys are medium to high energy• A single person or a couple with experience in caring for anxious dogs• No children, cats, or resident dogs — these boys will thrive in a calm and focused environment• Patience and understanding to continue their basic training and help them adjust to a loving home

Billy and Blackjack have been waiting far too long in a noisy shelter that doesn’t suit their sensitive personalities. Outside, their confidence and happiness shine, and what they need most now is the chance to flourish in a peaceful home.

Unfortunately, in Cyprus, black dogs like Billy and Blackjack are often overlooked and face an uncertain future. With your help, these wonderful companions can finally leave the shelter behind and start a new life in the UK.

Supported by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK registered charity (1209645), Billy and Blackjack are ready for adoption. An adoption fee, travel costs, and a home check will apply.

They will be vaccinated, rabies vaccinated, blood tested (including Brucella Canis), issued a Pet Passport, microchipped, neutered, given flea and worm treatment, fit-to-fly report, and full rescue back-up (RBU).

If you’re interested, reach out to Christine Malin — your kindness could change their world. Share their story with friends and family! Together, we can give Billy and Blackjack the loving family they truly deserve.

name: Buzz
description:
Buzz is a one and a half year old male Cross-Breed, medium in size, who has grown into a happy and friendly boy despite spending much of his early life in a shelter.

Buzz entered the shelter at just eight months old and has been there ever since.

Initially scared and withdrawn, he gradually became affectionate and curious once he realised he was safe, had regular meals, doggy friends, and kind human company.

He adores spending time with people and loves getting out of his kennel.

Buzz gets along well with everyone—dogs, children, and adults.

He would likely suit a first-time dog owner who can understand his background and teach him to enjoy life as a loved dog.

He can be cat tested for serious enquiries.

name: Batu
description:
Batu is a 2.5-year-old male Cross-Breed. Weighing 30kg and classed as a large dog, Batu currently lives in a shelter in Cyprus after being heartbreakingly surrendered by his previous owner.

Once living in an apartment, he’s now learning how to trust again in the noisy, unpredictable environment of a municipal shelter.

Though understandably wary at first, Batu has a heart of gold and a gentle nature that shines through once he starts to feel safe. He’s calm during vet visits, enjoys car journeys, and shows polite behaviour even when surrounded by barking, stressed-out dogs.

His composed reaction to chaotic situations is impressive and says a lot about his potential in a stable, loving home.

Batu can live with other dogs, particularly those of a similar size, or thrive as an only pet. In the shelter, he’s always curious and keen to say hello to the others, which shows his social side.

He’s already house trained from his previous home, and possibly has some basic training under his belt. Still, like all rescue dogs, he’ll benefit from ongoing support, routine, and positive reinforcement.

He would be happiest in a peaceful home that offers regular walks, space to rest, and most importantly, consistent affection. Batu deserves a second chance—not just at life, but at experiencing the kind of unconditional love and loyalty he’s more than ready to return.

name: Teddy
description:
Teddy is a 1-year-old male Shar Pei Cross. He’s medium-sized, weighing around 15kg, and currently based in a kennel in Cyprus. Once living in a home, Teddy now finds himself back in a shelter environment.

Although not much is known about his past, what’s clear is his calm, friendly temperament and his soft spot for people—especially once he knows you come with treats.

He’s got a relaxed vibe, rarely reacting to other dogs, but prefers to live as the only dog in the household. This isn’t because he’s aggressive—he’s not—but because he enjoys his own space and craves undivided attention.

He would thrive best in a quieter home, ideally with a single person or someone who can give him plenty of one-on-one time.

True to his Shar Pei roots, Teddy is loyal, reserved at first, and then suddenly your best mate once he warms up. His food motivation makes him easier to train, and his gentle nature makes him a cuddly companion, especially with that ridiculously soft coat.

He’s also calm in kennels, which hints at a low-stress personality and possibly a housetrained background—supported by how clean he keeps his living space.

Teddy enjoys the simple pleasures in life—walks, quality time with his humans, and the occasional play session. He’s good on the lead, loves a ride in the car, and takes food gently. In short, he’s low maintenance but high reward.

Teddy isn’t just adoptable—he’s desirable. His future family, whether in Cyprus or the UK, is in for a very loyal, very loving addition to their home.

name: Gabriel
description:
Gabriel is a 2 year old male Cross-Breed who is affectionate, friendly, and ready to start his new life with a loving family.

Gabriel arrived at the shelter with his sister Gabriella a year ago. They were found together as strays. From the minute they arrived, they were super friendly and loving towards the shelter staff.

For a while, they shared a kennel just the two of them and were very attached. To give them a better chance at adoption, they are now mixing with other dogs and can be adopted separately or together. They will be OK either way.

We think they are approximately 2 years old. They both get on well with other dogs, usually quite aloof and in their own little world. They are well behaved and adore people and lots of cuddles.

Both of them require a home that will take them on exciting walks and preferably have a large garden for them to run around in.

Both Gabriel and Gabriella have been superstars in the shelter. We just hope their situation changes soon. Living their young lives in a small kennel is not what they are here for.

They are here to be adored, and you will get plenty of love and cuddles in return.

Gabriel and Gabriella can be adopted in Cyprus or in the UK. They are both ready to fly. They will be neutered, vaccinated, and have a full health check and blood tests for infectious diseases.

name: Pebble
description:
Pebble is a 9 month old female Cross-Breed who is a friendly, happy girl eagerly waiting for her forever home. She does not know what being a puppy truly means.

She should be living her best life in a loving home, cared for and cherished, warm at night instead of shivering through the cold Cyprus evenings.

Confiscated from her home, she now sleeps with her three siblings on a cold concrete floor, huddled together for warmth. She will need time spent on training and time to get used to walking on a lead, but she is already great with other dogs and children.

She is far too young to grow up in a shelter and has missed such an important stage of learning and exploring.

Her ideal home will be with either a single person, a couple, or a family.

Full rescue back-up is provided by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK-based registered charity.

Pebble can be adopted in Cyprus or the UK. She will be tested for infectious diseases, travel with a passport, be vaccinated, including rabies, and receive regular flea and worm treatments.

name: Orion
description:
Orion is a 9 month old male Cross-Breed who is a sweet, friendly, lovely boy waiting for his forever home. He has never truly known what it means to be a carefree puppy.

He should be enjoying life in a loving home, cared for and cherished, warm at night instead of shivering through the cold Cyprus evenings.

Confiscated from his owners, he now sleeps with his three siblings on a cold concrete floor, huddled together for warmth.

When he grows up, he will be a medium to large dog who will need training and time to get used to walking on a lead, but he is already wonderful with other dogs and children.

He is far too young to spend his days in a shelter and has missed an important stage of learning and exploring.

He could live with a single person, a couple, or a family. A secure garden and nice walks would be ideal.

Orion can be adopted in Cyprus or the UK. He will be tested for infectious diseases, travel with a passport, be vaccinated, including rabies, and receive regular flea and worm treatments.

Please help give this lovely boy the life he deserves.

Full rescue back-up is provided by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK-based registered charity.

name: Ntina
description:
Ntina is a 3 year old female Kangal Shepherd Dog. She would prefer to be the only dog in the home and can live with children over the age of 12. She is currently located in Cyprus.

Kangal Shepherd Dogs often find themselves in shelters, abandoned due to their size. We made a promise to help find them all loving homes. Here are two Kangal Shepherd Dogs who ended up in the same shelter.

Next up is Ntina. She adores people, cuddles, and attention. She is slightly bigger than Keith, weighing approximately 50 kilograms. Ntina is easy to get along with and is much loved by the shelter staff and volunteers.

She was found as a stray, and it is hard to understand how anyone could abandon a dog and leave her to fend for herself, especially a dog of her size and with her thick coat.

Temperatures of 40 degrees on the streets of Cyprus in summer could not have been easy for her.

Ntina is friendly and placid with people and children, but due to her size she will need an experienced handler, and any children in the home must be over 12 years old. She needs a home where she can enjoy plenty of cuddles as well as long walks.

Ntina needs to be the only dog, as she can be selective around other dogs. Given her temperament, she would likely be happiest as the only dog in the home.

name: Bianca
description:
Bianca is a 2-year-old female Husky. This medium-sized girl, weighing 30kg, has already been through more than most dogs should ever have to.

Found starving and alone on the streets of Cyprus, she was so desperate for food that she was eating scraps alongside a colony of cats. Emaciated, weak, and suffering from leishmania, she had clearly been abandoned once her illness made her “inconvenient.”

But despite the pain and neglect she endured, Bianca never gave up. She quietly accepted her rescue, knowing on some deep level that things were about to change.

She just needs a simple tablet twice a day to keep the condition in check, plus a routine blood test once a year. It’s manageable and absolutely worth it for a dog who gives love as fiercely as Bianca does.

For the past six months, Bianca has been living in a foster home and learning how to be a dog again. She’s fully house trained, understands basic commands, and while she still pulls a bit on the lead, she’s improving.

She’s food motivated, making her very trainable — and she’s totally obsessed with squeaky balls. Her ideal day? A long sniffy walk, a fun play session, and cuddles with her favourite humans.

Bianca is incredibly affectionate and loyal to her circle, though she can take a little time to warm up to strangers. Once you’re in her heart though, you’re there for life.

She’s not keen on other animals and must be the only pet in the home. That said, she’s all the dog you’ll ever need — her love runs deep.

Her perfect match would be someone who loves big, bold personalities with even bigger hearts. An active home is ideal, with people who appreciate her breed’s intelligence and energy.

While experience with dogs — especially Huskies — is helpful, it’s not a strict requirement. What matters most is a loving, understanding home that’s ready to give this beautiful girl the second chance she’s waited so long for.

name: Richy
description:
Richy is a 7 month old female Cross-Breed.

This poor girl was dumped outside a shelter when she was only 12 weeks old. Richy was quite poorly and almost didn’t make it.

Now fully recovered and in good health, she is ready for her forever home. Richy is now 7 months old and a lovely and gentle natured girl.

In her short life so far she has known nothing but a cage but at least she has known the kindness of humans in the shelter.

Richy will need to be shown how to live in a home, just like any puppy! She is great with all other dogs, people, and children. She will suit most home scenarios and will grow to be medium sized.

Richy will travel with a passport and has been tested for infectious diseases. She will be fully vaccinated.

Needs resident dog: Can live with a resident dog or alone

name: Simon
description:
Simon is a 2 year old male Cross-Breed, medium in size and weighing between 20 to 25 kilograms. He’s got that cheeky, happy energy that lights up a room—or, well, a shelter kennel for now.

Even though he’s been at the shelter since July, he’s still his lovable, upbeat self, which honestly says a lot about this guy’s spirit.

He has an affinity for people. He adores spending quality time with volunteers that visit the shelter. For now, he is cheeky and happy. Simon has been in the shelter since July, so the fact that he is still his happy self is a testament to him.

It shows how strong and brave he is, waiting for his family to notice and adopt him. Maybe he doesn’t quite realise that being in a cage day in day out in a shelter is not what life should be like for a dog.

We do know that Simon will beside himself with gratitude once it’s his turn to be adopted.

Simon will enjoy being part of a family where he has human company most of the time. He does equally enjoy being around other dogs, happily living in a pack with male and female dogs of a similar size.

He can be homed with a similar energetic resident dog, or as an only dog where he can lap up all the attention for himself.

He would suit an active home and one that also has the time and patience to help him settle into a home environment.

Simon gets excited around children so any children would need to be 10+ and dog savvy. He is very friendly with people and dogs and generally a lovable happy-go-lucky boy!

If you’re looking for a playful and loving companion, Simon might be the perfect match for you.

name: Stratos
description:
Meet Stratos, a 2 year old male Pointer Cross currently in Cyprus.

Upon meeting Stratos on a recent trip to Cyprus, I can honestly say this boy broke me. Huddled in a compound with many other dogs, Stratos struggled to be seen.

The dogs rushed the fencing for attention. Poor Stratos tried his best to get to the people, making some fuss. But he missed out, overcome by the crowd. His face was full of sorrow and sadness. The shelter team told me his passion is human interaction but rarely did he get it.

I managed to get him out of the compound for a little one to one time. His little face literally lit up when he saw I’d chosen him to come out.He totally adored the attention, loved being fussed and stroked. He gave the best kisses ever.He pulled at my heartstrings like few dogs do.

Stratos can still live a happy, healthy and long life with the condition. He’ll just need an inexpensive tablet a day to keep him healthy; that’s it.

Sadly, there’s some prejudice around Leishmania; many won’t want to give Stratos the chance he deserves to be adopted. The odds are heavily stacked against him. I realised then I had to help him. Having had a Leishmania dog myself, I knew he was just as worthy as the next.

Stratos is very good with people and most dogs. He isn’t great with more dominant boys, so he’d be better placed with non-dominant boys or any girl dogs.

He’s brilliant with children, too, enjoying their company, showing how gentle he can be.

Dogs like Stratos can end up languishing in shelters for an eternity. All it takes, though, is that one special person to see a very special dog who will give mountains of affection, love and loyalty to whoever rescues him.

I fell in love instantly with this sweet little boy. I’m hoping someone else will see past his condition and see what I saw, an awesome dog who has been let down badly, thrown on the streets because his owner saw he had Leishmania symptoms, an all too common practise in Cyprus.

Dumped like rubbish and now losing precious months trapped in a compound of dogs in a municipality shelter.

Please think about adopting dogs like Stratos. Dogs like him, with a pre-existing condition, are almost the last to be adopted, along with older dogs or bigger dogs; they all suffer months, sometimes years, of imprisonment through no fault of their own.

Be that special, incredible person to help a less fortunate dog like Stratos. The love and devotion he’ll show you will not compare.

name: Gabriella
description:
Gabriella is a 2 year old female Cross-Breed who is friendly, affectionate, and ready to find her forever home.

Gabriella arrived at the shelter with her brother Gabriel a year ago. They were found together as strays. From the minute they arrived, they were super friendly and loving towards the shelter staff.

For a while, they shared a kennel just the two of them and were very attached. To give them a better chance at adoption, they are now mixing with other dogs and can be adopted separately or together. They will be OK either way.

We think they are approximately 2 years old. They both get on well with other dogs, usually quite aloof and in their own little world. They are well behaved and adore people and lots of cuddles.

Both of them require a home that will take them on exciting walks and preferably have a large garden for them to run around in.

Both Gabriel and Gabriella have been superstars in the shelter. We just hope their situation changes soon. Living their young lives in a small kennel is not what they are here for.

They are here to be adored, and you will get plenty of love and cuddles in return.

Gabriel and Gabriella can be adopted in Cyprus or in the UK. They are both ready to fly. They will be neutered, vaccinated, and have a full health check and blood tests for infectious diseases.

name: Vasilis
description:
Vasilis is a 3 year old male Cross-Breed, medium in size and all heart. This friendly and gentle soul is looking for a forever family to vibe with—and yes, he’s the full package minus the flight ticket.

Are you looking for a loyal and loving companion?

Meet Vasilis, an adorable 3.5-year-old crossbreed with a heart of gold! Vasilis is friendly and gentle, getting along wonderfully with other dogs, children, and people of all ages. Families with children over 12 yrs.

He’s a calm traveller—whether it’s a trip to the vet or a car ride, Vasilis takes everything in stride. With his playful nature and moderate energy, he’s just as happy to join you for a walk as he is to snuggle by your side.

Vasilis enjoys affection and physical activity, making him a perfect addition to an active or relaxed household. His only quirk? He’s a bit of an escape artist, so a fully secure garden with a minimum of a 6ft fence (which he can clear) – he must not be left unattended in the garden at any time.

If you’re ready to welcome a sweet, fun-loving pup into your life, Vasilis is waiting to meet you.

Vasilis will be fully vaccinated, neutered, blood tested, a pet passport, microchipped, flea and wormed.

Full Rescue Back up (RBU) by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK registered charity 1209645.

Note: Adoption fee, Travel fees and home check apply.

Every adoption opens a space to rescue more—it’s the beautiful cycle of saving lives.For info, message Christine MalinSHARE this post far and wide—you never know whose heart he will tug on.

name: Stella
description:
Sweet soul Stella is a 3.5 year old female Greek Hound and is small in size, weighing around 15 kilograms. This shy girl was picked up as a stray, quite likely dumped by a hunter.

When Stella first arrived at the shelter, she was frightened of her own shadow—fearful of humans. It’s sadly no surprise why she trembled when people approached her; those who should have cared for her likely neglected or mistreated her.

Unfortunately, it’s an all-too-common story among hunting dogs in Cyprus, whose gentle spirits are often broken by cruelty.

But the most beautiful thing about dogs is their ability to put the past behind them, and Stella has done just that.

She’s been in the shelter for around six months now, and she knows what a full belly feels like, what a gentle touch is, and that not all people are unkind. Stella now smiles—broadly!

During a recent trip to Cyprus, one of our team met her in the kennels, watching as another dog got the attention. Stella quietly tried to be seen, longing for a little stroke.

It was impossible not to fall in love with her, and we vowed to find this beautiful girl the best home she deserves after everything she’s been through.

Stella remains shy and timid by nature, and while that may never fully change, she’s got huge potential to grow within her gentle personality.

She adores cuddles and kisses from those she trusts. Her new family will need to understand that she might not be immediately forthcoming, but once she feels safe, she’ll shower you with affection.

Stella’s ideal home would be a quiet one. She’s easily spooked by loud noises, likely due to her past, so she’ll do best in a calm environment.

She gets along wonderfully with other dogs and would thrive in a home with one or two canine companions, though a home that ensures plenty of doggy interaction could also work well. A companion dog would help her build confidence and adjust to home life.

Stella would be best suited to an adult-only household or one with children aged 12 and over, as smaller children may make her nervous. She cannot live with cats.

An active hound girl of medium energy, Stella enjoys walks and using her hound nose for a good sniff. She’s struggling to get noticed in a big shelter, but she’s ready to be seen by the world and find her forever family.

Rescue back-up by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply.Home check applies.

Stella is neutered, fully vaccinated, tested, and has her passport ready—she’s just waiting for someone to buy her ticket to a new and amazing life.

name: Jacob
description:
Majestic Jacob is a 2 year old male German Shepherd Dog. This handsome boy currently resides at Simba Shelter in Cyprus.

How did such a majestic guy end up in a municipality shelter could be our first question—but nonetheless, that’s where this stunning boy has found himself.

Found very thin, wandering the surrounding area, he was clearly dumped like so many before him. His breed is sadly much maligned in Cyprus—overbred and then tossed out like rubbish.

He’s actually quite a shy and timid boy with people he doesn’t know but soon warms up when he realises there’s affection involved! Jacob is very human-orientated.

He’s a people dog for sure. He’d adore a home that appreciates that he’d like to be a “velcro dog.” True to his breed, Jacob is an incredibly loyal and loving companion who will give his all to the human who rescues him.

As with all young, larger dogs, Jacob would be best placed in a home that enjoys walking. He loves a good sniff while out. Being a working breed, he’ll enjoy being challenged mentally as well as physically.

Families with older children (10 years and up) are welcome to apply. Not cat-tested. He has minimal leash experience but isn’t a puller. Someone with large breed or German Shepherd experience would be helpful, although not essential.

Ultimately, what Jacob really wants is to be loved and cuddled. Just because he’s a big guy doesn’t mean he’s not a soft one! Don’t be put off by his size—Jacob is a little dog in a big dog’s body.

His aspirations are to be a lapdog! If you appreciate big, loving, loyal dogs with massive potential to love endlessly, Jacob is your boy.

Rescue back-up by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK-registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply.Home check applies.Fully vaccinated, tested, and neutered.He has his passport and is ready to fly.

name: Aries
description:
Meet Aries, a 2 year old male Cross-Breed with a gorgeous brindle coat and a heart full of love. Aries is currently at Simba Shelter in Cyprus and is dreaming of a home where he can finally relax, learn, and be the best version of himself.

At Saving Souls Animal Rescue, we believe every dog deserves a chance to escape the shelter life — and Aries is no exception. Sadly, brindle dogs like him often get overlooked, and because he’s not good with other dogs, many people scroll past his profile.

But dogs are just like people — some need a bit of extra time, patience, and love to truly shine.

Aries is reactive toward most other dogs, but he’s profoundly affectionate and loving with every person he meets. He’s a total people dog who prefers humans over canines any day.

He needs to be the only dog or pet in the household and will thrive in a family that can give him consistent training, structure, and patience as he adjusts to home life.

Although described as medium energy, Aries will likely enjoy both outdoor adventures and cozy sofa snuggles once he’s out of the shelter. He’s an active boy who’ll be the perfect match for someone who enjoys walks, playtime, and plenty of affection.

Please don’t let Aries be another dog who waits months or even years to be noticed. He’s loving, loyal, and ready to give his heart to the person who gives him a chance.

Rescue backed bySaving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK-registered charity.Adoption and travel fees apply.Home check required.Fully vaccinated, tested, neutered, and ready to travel with his passport!

name: Rex
description:
Rex is a 2 year old male Cross-Breed who was surrendered by his owner after previously living in a home.

We know he lived in a home before, which makes it even more heartbreaking that he has known comfort and a human companion and has now found himself in a cage day in and day out.

It is not unusual for dogs like Rex to find themselves waiting in kennels for months or even years due to their intimidating size. But Rex is the calmest and biggest softy.

Rex is remarkably sweet and affectionate with people. He adores fuss and craves a good cuddle. Having said that, he does not like other dogs, so he needs to be homed as the only dog.

We do not want Rex to lose the best years of his life in a cage. His size should not matter. He is a dog and deserves a home just like the small, cute, fluffy dogs that get adopted so easily. Rex will be a wonderful companion.

Rex can be adopted in Cyprus or the UK. He has been tested for infectious diseases, will travel with a passport, be vaccinated, including rabies, and receive regular flea and worm treatments.

His adoption fees are sponsored, so we are only asking for travel costs.

name: Toulis
description:
Meet Toulis, the charming and easy-going lad. Toulis is a 2 year old male Labrador Retriever Cross of medium size.

Toulis entered the shelter when he was about a year old, likely after his owner decided they no longer wanted him once he was past his puppy stage.

It must have been stressful and heartbreaking for him, wondering what he did wrong, but we believe it was actually the best thing that could’ve happened.

His perfect home is out there—we just need to make sure they see him.

Toulis is super friendly with everyone—those he knows, strangers, children, and other dogs.

He’s easy-going and very trainable; one of those dogs who will be a total pleasure to train and might even master a few tricks!

Although he’s a lovely-natured boy, he has shown signs of trying to escape in the shelter due to stress, so a secure garden is an absolute must.

name: Dylan
description:
Dylan is a 4 year old male Cross-Breed. He could live with a female resident dog but would be best in an adult-only home. He is currently looking for a forever home after spending years in a shelter.

Since January 2023, this poor boy has been in a cage through no fault of his own, other than not being a dog typically sought after—he is not small, fluffy, or “cute” enough. Admittedly, he was advertised late, so his time cooped up has been longer than expected.

Dylan is approximately four years old, which means he has been in the shelter since he was just a young dog. He does not tolerate shelter life well; he is stressed, paces his kennel, and has tried to escape the misery of a cage with minimal human interaction.

This has made Dylan quite nervous. He has not experienced life as a dog should and currently does not like being handled at the vets, often fearful when someone reaches over his head.

Despite this, he is friendly with strangers and loving with people he knows. He gets very excited when approached at his kennel, clearly delighted by attention.

Dylan will need an experienced and patient home with ongoing basic training. Given his long stay in the shelter, he may need a very special home to help him understand the world and know that he can be loved—and that he deserves it.

A home environment may feel very new to him, and he will need time to decompress. He could live with a female resident dog, but not males, as in his current environment he does not get along with them.

We recommend an adult-only household due to his nervousness and lengthy shelter stay. Dylan would love an active home where he can enjoy exciting walks, and ideally a garden to run around in. Essentially, he just needs a home to call his own.

Shelter dogs like Dylan convey a deep sense of gratefulness when they find a companion. They know they have been given a second chance and are thankful forever.

Due to Dylan’s difficult start in life and to give him the best chance, his vet fees have been sponsored. Only his travel costs would need to be covered.

name: Bella
description:
Meet Bella, a 4 year old female Belgian Malinois Cross weighing 30 kilograms, currently in Cyprus and dreaming of her forever home.

This stunning girl has spent three long years in a shelter — far too much time for such a loyal, intelligent dog with a heart of gold. Despite everything, Bella still has so much love to give.

She may take a little time to open up, but once she trusts you, she’s all affection and devotion. Bella is fantastic on the lead — no pulling, just lots of sniffing and exploring.

She’s independent by nature and would love a calm environment where she can truly relax and be the queen of her castle.

She’s selective about her doggy friends, so she’d be happiest as the only pet, but she absolutely adores people and thrives on human companionship.

Bella has endured years on cold concrete floors and scorching summer heat, and now she deserves a warm, loving home where she can finally feel safe.

She’s the perfect companion for someone who enjoys outdoor adventures, gentle training, and cozy evenings in.

An owner who enjoys training and bonding time

A patient, understanding person ready to show her what love truly feels like

Bella’s adoption fee has been sponsored due to the long time she’s spent waiting for her chance. She’s fully vaccinated, tested, neutered, microchipped, and ready to travel to the UK with Saving Souls Animal Rescue (UK Registered Charity 1209645).

name: Casper
description:
Meet Casper, a 4 year old male Cross-Breed who’s been waiting far too long for his shot at happiness. He’s currently based at Simba Shelter in Cyprus and has sadly become one of their longest residents.

Despite being such a handsome and clever boy, Casper keeps getting overlooked — maybe because he’s over 4 years old, larger in size, or simply because he’s black and tan. None of that takes away from what a truly special dog he is.

Adoption fee sponsored / travel fees apply. Foster placement considered.

Casper is a profoundly wonderful dog on so many levels. Not only is he incredibly handsome, but he’s also clever and eager to please. He would suit an adopter willing to take the time to get to know him, train him, and invest in his potential.

With his intelligence and willingness to learn, he’ll be a hugely rewarding dog for anyone patient enough to guide him.

He’s best suited to a pet-free home with experienced owners who understand that rescue dogs sometimes need time and structure to settle into a new life. In return, Casper will give back tenfold — loyalty, love, and gratitude in abundance.

Rescue backed by Saving Souls, a UK-registered charity.

Fully vaccinated, tested, neutered, and ready to travel with his passport!

name: Keith
description:
Keith is a 2.5 year old male Kangal Cross currently in Cyprus. With his striking looks and intelligent nature, he’s the full package — a big, handsome boy with both brains and heart.

Since arriving at the shelter, Keith has quickly become a favourite among the team.

No one knows how anyone could have abandoned him on the streets of Nicosia, but their loss is certainly his future family’s gain.

Keith loves people — he soaks up every bit of attention and affection from volunteers.

He walks well on the lead once his initial excitement calms down, and he may have lived in a home before. He’s great with other dogs and could happily live with a calm female dog or be the only dog in the household.

Keith hasn’t met any cats yet, though it’s suspected he might chase them.

He’d love a family home with older children (10+), mainly because of his size and enthusiasm — at around 35 kilograms, he could easily knock over a smaller child without meaning to.

The shelter team has also discovered that Keith can be a bit of an escape artist, so he’ll need a secure garden and plenty of stimulation to keep his active mind and body engaged.

Keith is bright, loving, and ready for a new adventure. With the right family who understands large, intelligent breeds, he’ll make an incredible and loyal companion.

Rescue backed by Saving Souls Animal Rescue, a UK-registered charity.

Adoption and travel fees apply. Home check required.

name: Cassie
description:
Cassie is a one year old Shepherd mix and was found running through farmland when a local rescuer was able to coax her into her car and take her to safety.

Surprisingly, during her check up at the vets they discovered that she actually had a registered microchip, so they were able to track down her owners and asked them to come and collect her.

But of course, had the story ended positively, then Cassie wouldn’t be on our website. Sadly, her family said they didn’t want her anymore as she was too energetic, and that the rescuer could rehome her.

There was no sense of remorse or accountability, they just gave her away and moved on with their lives.

Cassie is a shepherd mix and she is indeed an energetic, active and highly intelligent young dog. She’s estimated to be just under a year old, and she is always raring to get outside and go on an adventure.

Right now, she’s in private kennels where she’s safe and sound until she finds her forever home, but she needs more exercise and enrichment than they can offer her, and as a result whenever she goes out for a run in the yard she is like a coiled spring.

Cassie is looking for a family who have had high energy dogs before and can give her the physical and mental outlets that she needs to thrive. She is so, so loyal and bonds with people very quickly.

She’s extremely keen to please and eager to learn, and as she’s so food motivated this makes the perfect reward!

Cassie would be best suited to a home outside of the city with an active family who enjoy running, exploring and just generally being outdoors.

When her needs are met, like any dog, Cassie is a bundle of love and loves hanging out with people. She’s also great with other dogs, especially those of a similar energy level to her, and she loves racing around in the grass with them.

She would also be best suited to a home with a reasonable size garden, as she really likes being outdoors.

Cassie may be young and bouncy, but she also has lots of love to give. She is the perfect dog for someone seeking a best friend, adventure partner and a companion that they can train.

How big? MediumHow old? Adolescent (just under one year)Male or female? FemaleLiving with kids? I can live with children (8+)Living with dogs? I can live with other dogsResident dog required? NoLiving with cats? I can’t live with catsWhere can I live? I’d like an active home outside of the city with a gardenWhere am I from? Cyprus

name: Cece
description:
Cece is an 18 month old Shepherd mix who arrived at the shelter as an unclaimed stray, a story we’ve heard many a time. No one came looking for her, and she had no microchip or collar, so who knows where she came from.

She was however in good health, and happy to meet new people, so we’re hopeful that she’s not had a difficult start in life.

Cece is around a year and a half old and she is a beautiful girl with a very happy, friendly and sociable personality. She loves being around people and is very eager to please, quietly confident, and reads other dogs well.

It took a little while for her to initially calm down when she first arrived in the shelter as she had some nervous energy to burn off, but once we’d found her the ideal kennel mates and she’d had some time to decompress, her true nature started to shine through.

As mentioned, Cece now shares her kennel with multiple other dogs of various sizes, ages and energy levels. She’s loving having the opportunity to make new friends and enjoys playing with them and just being in their company.

While we don’t feel she’s dependant on other dogs, she definitely likes hanging out with them, and she’s more than happy to share her bed, which is so cute.

18 months old is still young, so Cece would benefit from some basic training to help refine her manners a little. She learnt to sit in no time, despite all the distractions around her in the shelter.

As she is food motivated, this works really well as a reward. When she’s excited to see you Cece is prone to jumping up a bit, but it doesn’t take long for this to pass and with some routine and a proper home setting, we’re confident that is very trainable.

The ideal home for Cece would be with a family who enjoy time outdoors, have direct access to a secure garden, and who are keen to invest some time into a young dog learning about a whole new world.

We think the outskirts of the city would be preferable for Cece, or of course a more rural setting would suit her just fine too.

She’d enjoy a fairly active lifestyle, and with some time and patience we think she will make a brilliant addition to a family home.

NoLiving with cats? I’ve not been tested with catsWhere can I live? I’d like a fairly active home outside of the cityWhere am I from? Cyprus

name: Toffee
description:
Meet Toffee, a 2-year-old small-medium male mix breed.

Toffee’s journey hasn’t been easy. He was found on the streets after being severely abused and abandoned by his previous owner.

When he first arrived at the shelter last November, he was terrified of human touch and didn’t understand what was happening. It took weeks of gentle care and patience before he began to trust people again.

Today, Toffee is a loving, playful little bundle of joy. He adores cuddles, loves playtime, and is friendly with everyone he meets, including children.

He walks nicely on the lead and quickly makes friends wherever he goes. He also gets along nicely with other dogs, especially females.

After all he’s been through, Toffee deserves a forever home where he is cherished and included in family life, rather than left alone for long periods.

He thrives on companionship and love, and like all rescue dogs, he will need time and patience to settle into his new home.

Toffee would happily live with a friendly female dog or as the only dog, enjoying the full attention of his new family. He’s ready to bring love, loyalty, and joy to a home that will treasure him forever.

Toffee is in Hungary and will travel to the UK once a home has been settled for him.

He comes neutered, fully vaccinated, microchipped, with an EU pet passport.

Home check and adoption fee apply. Full RBU provided.

name: Bambi
description:
Bambi is a 16 month old girl who will grow into a smaller medium-sized dog (current weight: 7 kg). She currently lives in a foster family in Hungary.

She and her sibling were strays when tragedy struck — Bambi was hit by a car and suffered a hip injury. Thanks to surgery and lots of care from her foster family, she’s healing beautifully.

Today, she walks, runs, hops onto the sofa, and will continue to benefit from gentle physiotherapy.

This sweet girl has a gentle, kind nature. She can be timid in new situations, but once she trusts you, she’s a cuddly, affectionate shadow who loves to snuggle in bed.

She’s also a little mischief-maker — stealing slippers, playing with toys, and happily chasing her ball!

Bambi adores other dogs and would be happiest in a home with a canine friend. She doesn’t like being left alone, so a companion will give her the comfort she needs.

This sweet pup is still getting the hang of walking on a lead and can be a little shy around traffic and crowds, but with patience and encouragement, she’s making great progress!

She is toilet trained and clean in the home. And one last fun fact… Bambi loves cheese!

Bambi will need an experienced home that can give her the time, patience, and reassurance she needs to settle and blossom. She is fully vaccinated, microchipped, and will need to be spayed by her forever family once fully grown.

Adoption is subject to Home Check and Adoption fee. Full RBU.

name: Kabo
description:
Kabo is a four year old German Pointer. He arrived at the shelter along with two other pointers, all of whom were found roaming as strays at the end of the hunting season.

The three of them are happy in each other’s company, and now they share a kennel together. As their individual personalities have emerged we’ve been getting to know them, and now we would love to find each of them their perfect forever home.

Kabo is around four years old and has no idea how beautiful he is. He’s sweet, soft and sensitive, and has a gentle, affectionate and curious nature.

He can be a little apprehensive with new people, but he’s a sucker for treats so that really helps to earn his trust.

Unfortunately, the hunting culture in Cyprus dictates the treatment of a lot of hounds, so we’re not sure that Kabo has ever truly experienced the love, security and compassion that he has deserved throughout the past few years.

Around other dogs, Kabo is great. He is friendly, very silly, playful but also provides gentle guidance to the younger dogs.

He is good with both males and females, and we definitely think that he would really enjoy a home where he had another resident dog to learn from and play with.

Without another dog, it may take longer for Kabo to settle in properly, but it wouldn’t be impossible as long as his family are prepared to give him the time and patience he needs.

Kabo would be best suited to a semi-rural home where he’s surrounded by lots of places to explore. Being a pointer, he loves to follow his nose and he’s always curious about his surroundings.

He would also be best suited to a home with a garden, as it’s likely that he has spent the vast majority of his life living outdoors in some capacity. A garden will provide Kabo with a safe outdoor space where he can acclimatise to living indoors little by little.

Once settled, Kabo will be the perfect companion for an outdoorsy family who enjoy an active lifestyle, but also want a best friend who’ll be happy snuggling on the sofa.

Pointers are fantastic cuddlers, and you won’t be able to get enough of Kabo’s velvet ears and soft muzzle.

name: Remi
description:
Remi is a two year old English Pointer who arrived in the shelter recently after being found as a stray at the end of the hunting season.

Although Cyprus has a reputation for neglecting their hounds and spaniels during the hunting season, Remi actually came into the shelter in really good condition, so we’re hopeful that he’s not had a miserable start to life.

Remi is estimated to be around two years old and he is such a sweet soul. Being an English Pointer, he has that lovely endearing squishy face, a heart of gold, and such velvety soft fur.

He is quite a quiet boy with a gentle, calm nature, and he is very trusting and good tempered around new people.

Remi is currently sharing a kennel run with five other dogs, males and females, and he gets on with them all really well.

He mixes well in a group, reads other canine body language well, and recognises when other dogs want to play, or want some space. He has quite a goofy character, and you can often see the cogs turning, but he’s truly nothing but love.

Treats and cuddles are both welcomed by Remi, and he loves making friends with new people. He would be suitable for a home with children, and he could definitely live with other dogs too, but he’s not been tested cats.

We think he has the potential to be a great addition to a family looking for a very sociable, friendly and adaptable young dog.

Remi’s ideal home would be outside of the city, with access to a garden and plenty of nice walks each week.

He would love the opportunity to explore lots of green spaces, or perhaps even the beach, and he’ll jump at any chance to use his nose and sniff out interesting scents.

He’s an active breed naturally, but is by no means bouncing off the walls and a moderately energetic lifestyle would suit him well.

He would also relish any opportunity to snuggle on the sofa with his new family – pointers are amazing cuddlers! Once he realises the wonder of home comforts, he’ll never look back!

Remi is such a good natured dog and any family would be lucky to welcome him into their home. We can’t wait to see him settle in as a companion and best friend, and we know that he’s going to make such an impact wherever he goes.

name: Indie
description:
Indie is a three year old Shepherd mix who was brought into the shelter after locals notified the dog warden that she had been roaming freely around the village for a few weeks.

No one seemed to know where she’d come from, or was able to advise who she belonged to. With no microchip to help us connect the dots she ended up in the shelter, and is now waiting for a new home.

Indie is a beautiful girl who just blends in at the shelter.

She doesn’t ask for much, she doesn’t demand attention, and she shares her kennel with two very silly male dogs who she is extremely tolerant and gentle with, despite their complete lack of spatial awareness or manners!

She is very patient with other dogs and gets on well with them, but equally would be fine to be homed as the only resident dog, as she’s quietly confident and independent.

Indie is a very clever girl and loves her treats. As she’s food motivated this will really help with any further training her new family might want to do with her, as she responds really well to positive reinforcement.

It’s likely that Indie is a shepherd mix, so she will enjoy training, enrichment and brain games, as dogs like her thrive with focus and direction.

The ideal home for Indie would be an active family who can offer her plenty of opportunities to explore and are looking for a loyal, devoted companion to accompany them on their adventures.

Indie bonds with new people quickly and is a very loving dog, so we think she will adapt to a companion lifestyle well.

Indie would be happiest in a semi-rural setting where she’s surrounded by nice places to walk, fields to roam through or woods to wander in.

She would welcome trips to the beach or long hikes at the weekend, and she will be able to keep up with a more active pace of life. We’re sure she’ll be very excited to experience home comforts as well though!

We don’t know if Indie has ever had the opportunity to have her own bed or even live indoors before, so her family need to be patient while she adjusts in her own time. For anyone looking for a loyal best friend, Indie would be perfect.

name: Bonnie
description:
Bonnie is a two year old Pointer mix who was found as a stray and had been hiding in abandoned buildings across open farmland for a while before she was able to be caught.

The vet confirmed she had not been microchipped, and as she’d been roaming for a while it was clear she’d not come from a home.

She was rescued from agricultural land where strays are often found, and unsurprisingly, no one came to the shelter to claim her as their own.

Bonnie arrived just after Christmas and has been in the shelter without any interest ever since. She was absolutely terrified when she first arrived, and was so avoidant of people.

She didn’t want anyone to touch her, and would hide in the back of her kennel when anyone approached. She was then paired up with a confident male dog who has zero fear of anyone or anything, and slowly she started to realise that she was safe.

Bonnie is now a different dog. She’s still a little bit reserved and needs time to trust new people, but she’s much more friendly, so much happier, and she’s really come out of her shell in the past few months.

She is more confident in the company of other dogs, and she’s really benefitted from having a kennel friend to learn from and follow their lead. This in mind, we think she would be better suited to a home with another calm, resident dog.

Bonnie would struggle in a loud home in a busy area, so we’re looking for a laid-back home atmosphere based in a calmer residential, or semi-rural location.

She would love to be surrounded by fields and nature walks, rather than roads and buildings, and she is definitely looking for a home with direct access to a secure garden.

We know that given the time she needs, and a gentle, patient approach, Bonnie will absolutely blossom and she’ll make an amazing companion with a little time and understanding.

Bonnie has had some introductory lead training, and has responded well, but still needs a bit more work. She is a relatively active dog and would welcome long walks, but is also quite happy basking in the sun on a warm day.

Given that she’s a shyer dog, we are looking for a home with children aged 8+, so as not to overwhelm her. There’s not a bad bone in her body, but she’d struggle with a very unpredictable household, and would prefer a calm home.

Bonnie absolutely deserves a second chance and we know that given the patience that she needs, she will do so well in a new home.

She spent a long time in survival mode, so she just needs a secure environment to understand that she doesn’t have to look out for herself all the time – she’ll be treated with kindness, love and affection.

She’s already grown in confidence and made great progress in the shelter, so in a proper home she’s going to do just fine.

name: Gaston
description:
Gaston is a two year old Labrador mix who arrived recently after being found running among traffic.

Once again, without a microchip, there was no way to know if anyone was missing him, and he was admitted to the shelter in the hope a family might come and claim him as their own.

Unfortunately, that never happened, and now we’re looking for Gaston’s forever home.

At the moment Gaston shares his kennel with a number of other dogs, both males and females. He has slotted in really well and gets on with everyone he meets.

He has an energetic, silly style of play and loves a bit of rough and tumble with other dogs, but he can also read the room and knows when it’s time to stop.

Gaston is still a young dog, and he’s therefore looking for a more active lifestyle with a family who can take him on long walks and engage in some basic training with him, to help him let off some steam and to give him some mental focus too.

Although he comes across as quite the goofball, there are some brain cells in that big head of his, and he does actually like to learn. He loves being made a fuss of and gets very excited when he’s praised for being a good boy.

Gaston is still working on lead training, and he can be quite strong when he pulls so this is an area that may need some continued work by his new family.

He is responsive to positive reinforcement, and unsurprisingly for a Labrador type, he loves food as a reward! He can also get over-excited quickly and will jump up without realising his size can be a bit overwhelming!

With this in mind, he would be better suited to a home with slightly older children who are used to being around dogs. We’re confident this behaviour can be improved with training, but there’s only so much we can achieve in a shelter setting.

Gaston is a very friendly, loving, goofy and sociable dog, and he would make a lovely companion in the right home.

We’re unsure if Gaston has lived in a conventional home setting before, so he will need some patience as he adapts to a new lifestyle, but as he’s so people oriented we’re sure with lots of reassurance he will settle in quickly.

The ideal home for Gaston would be based outside of the city with direct access to a secure garden.

He’d really benefit from some introductory training to improve his manners and build a strong bond with his new family, and he would also love a fairly active lifestyle.

He loves rolling around in the grass at the shelter, napping under the shade of the trees, and digging in the dust, so a family who spend a lot of time outside would be perfect for him.

He is such a silly love bug with a heart of gold, and we’d love to see him find the forever home he deserves.

name: Jasper
description:
Jasper is a 1 year old hound mix. He was brought into the shelter in Cyprus as a stray without a microchip, although he arrived in good condition and is clearly comfortable around people.

From the moment he arrived, it was obvious that he is a very people focused boy who craves interaction and affection, and finds the shelter environment both limiting and overwhelming at times.

Jasper is a lovely, silly and slightly goofy young dog with a big personality and even bigger feelings. He is incredibly handsome and full of enthusiasm, but sometimes struggles to contain his excitement when he gets the chance to be fussed over.

He means absolutely no harm, he simply gets carried away in the moment, which is very understandable given how little stimulation shelter life can offer. He loves treats, enjoys attention and is desperate to make friends.

Around people, Jasper is friendly, social and very bouncy. He is quick to approach and keen to engage, but would benefit from continued training to help him learn calmer ways to channel his excitement.

In the right environment, with consistency and positive reinforcement, we think he will make great progress and really enjoy learning. Given his hound genes, he’d be great at scent work!

Jasper is good with other dogs and currently shares a kennel with a lovely Labrador who copes well with his energetic play style. The two of them get on nicely, and Jasper is able to take social cues from her when she’s had enough.

He could live with another dog, but is also confident enough to live as the only dog as long as his social and exercise needs are met.

Jasper enjoys attention from children, but due to his bouncy nature we would recommend a home with children aged eight and over unless t

uld be happiest in a semi rural or rural setting, with access to a garden and an active lifestyle. A quieter environment away from the constant stimulation of city life would allow him to settle, focus and build confidence.

He is very people oriented, eager to please and desperate to be part of a family unit. With the right guidance, patience and structure, Jasper will grow into a loyal, affectionate and very fun companion for the right home.

name: Misty
description:
Misty is a two year old Shepherd/Hound mix who was found as a stray in Cyprus and spent almost a year in the shelter before she found a home in the UK.

Through absolutely no fault of her own, within 24 hours she was surrendered back into our care due to a change of personal circumstances, and unfortunately, it hasn’t worked out for Misty this time either.

We’re now looking for a new home for her. Third time’s a charm, right?

Misty’s family have put a lot of time into her training and understanding her behaviour, but feel they can’t give her the type of lifestyle and home she truly needs.

Over the past few months she has developed reactivity towards strangers, which seems to be a mixture of fear and protectiveness towards her family.

The sad thing is that when Misty first arrived, she could be around anyone, anywhere. We hope that once she’s in an environment more suited to her, she’ll feel more at ease again.

With those she knows, Misty is the most wonderful, loyal and loving companion. She’s so cuddly and absolutely adores her family. When she feels safe, she is an amazing girl and there’s never any shortage of affection from her!

Misty really loves other dogs, and we think she would be best suited to a home with another resident dog.

Although she currently lives on her own and is fine, we think having a resident dog around will help her to gain confidence in new situations, around new people, and realise that there’s nothing to fear.

She’s a very sociable, playful girl, and her favourite days are when she gets to go to daycare where she be with her friends all day.

Misty is also wonderful with children of various ages. She lives with two teenagers, and during family holidays she stays with a family who have a ten year old son, who becomes her favourite person.

With adults that she doesn’t know, particularly men, her response is normally to assume they might be a threat.

She also doesn’t like strangers entering the house, and this requires environmental management (keeping her separate) or slow introductions.

Misty is very trainable. She’s super smart, very food motivated, and always keen to please. She loves pretty much any food, including vegetables.

She has also done scent work sessions which she was brilliant at, and really enjoyed. She also walks very nicely on the lead, until she sees someone she feels unsure about and then becomes reactive.

However, if the stranger has another dog she is much more comfortable and trusting.

The ideal home for Misty would be based semi-rurally with a large garden for her to run around and sniff in. She would prefer a canine companion, and would feel more relaxed in a quieter home with few comings and goings.

She would enjoy a fairly active lifestyle, and a family who recognise that her reactivity needs some work and understanding.

However, we do feel that a change of environment will really contribute to her progress. Misty has so many positive traits, and in the right home she will blossom.
