name: Prince
description:
Prince is a 20 month old Lurcher and he is looking for a new home. Although he’s not originally an Underdog, when we heard that he needed some support we wanted to try and help him.

Unfortunately, Prince is living in a home that is not suitable for his needs. He has been struggling in the city and needs a less overwhelming environment to call home.

He is also a very energetic boy, and is looking for an active, patient and understanding family.

Prince is just under two years old and is a sighthound mix. He was part of an accidental litter, and was bought as a puppy, but has consistently struggled in London and requires a more experienced handler.

With the support of a local day-care and dog walking business, Prince has had a lot more exposure to other dogs and new people, and he has made a lot of progress regarding his confidence and social skills.

We think this will continue to improve in a semi-rural area.

He is however apprehensive of strangers, and needs time to get used to new people. Although Prince doesn’t like being in big groups of dogs in day-care setting, he is very happy on group walks and trots along happily with them.

Prince has been handled quite roughly in the past which has left him sensitive to touch. He has been muzzle trained to make vet visits more manageable, and he also wears this when on walks in case he meets a dog he feels uncomfortable around.

During the day, Prince is quite happy to be left alone for a few hours. He is housetrained and enjoys playing with toys. He has been conditioned to race after a ball when out on walks, so this is his primary source of exercise and he is very ball driven.

He has responded well to training and is generally a very good boy who is eager to please. He sits and waits for his food and takes treats nicely, but doesn’t like to be approached whilst he is eating.

Prince loves spending time in the countryside where he has more space to run around. He would love a home with a big garden and an active lifestyle, and he’s much calmer outside of the hustle and bustle of London.

We would love to see him get the second chance that he deserves in a more suitable home where he can get the attention, focus and training that he thrives on.

name: Aspen
description:
Aspen is a 10 month old Beagle mix who’s life got off to a very rocky start. He was abandoned as a young puppy in Cyprus and found seriously unwell with parvovirus.

After spending time in the vets and making a full recovery, he joined the shelter and eventually travelled to the UK as a foster.

Since arriving, it’s been a bit of a journey for him, and it’s taken time to really understand what he needs to feel settled and secure.

After struggling to find the right fit in a home environment, Aspen has spent the last couple of months in kennels, where he has surprised everyone with just how well he’s done.

With clear routines, patient handling and consistent care, he has really come into himself. He’s responded brilliantly to basic training, built very strong relationships with the staff and grown hugely in confidence.

Aspen is a clever, energetic young dog, but new and unfamiliar situations can still feel overwhelming for him, and are often made more challenging by the fact that he is deaf.

Aspen is great with people and has been incredibly sociable, affectionate and engaged with those caring for him. Everyone who has been involved with him at the kennels has been really charmed by his personality.

He’s a cheeky, busy puppy who loves cuddles, fuss and being the centre of attention. Once he feels safe, his personality really shines and he shows just how much he enjoys human company.

When it comes to other dogs, Aspen is more selective. While he has previously lived alongside other dogs, is kennelled parallel to other dogs, and can pass dogs calmly on walks, we think that overall he would be happiest as the only dog in the home.

We suspect that earlier experiences may have left him feeling automatically defensive, and giving him a space where he doesn’t have to share attention or resources will help him relax and feel secure.

We are looking for an adult only home without other pets. As Aspen is deaf, it’s important that his environment is predictable and calm, and we don’t feel it would be fair to place him in a home with children or cats.

He would thrive in a quieter, more rural setting with access to a garden and plenty of opportunity to burn off energy in a safe way.

Aspen is clever and has learned hand signals for certain commands, so he would love to continue with further training as he really likes to learn and engage.

Aspen needs a confident, committed adopter who understands that he’s a young dog who has had a lot of upheaval in a short space of time.

With the right people, he lights up and shows what a sweet, silly and loving boy he really is.

Experience with training or deaf dogs would be helpful but isn’t essential, as long as his new family are patient, open to learning and ready to give him the structure and stability he’s been missing.

name: Freya
description:
Freya is a beautiful Segugio Italiano cross, estimated to be around 4-5 years old. She’s on the smaller side of medium and has been patiently waiting for almost three years to find her forever home.

Found abandoned and severely underweight, Freya has been cared for by a local boarding kennel in Cyprus, where she’s blossomed into a gentle, affectionate, and sweet-natured dog.

Freya is slightly reserved and shy when meeting new people, but she has made significant progress in building her confidence, thanks to her kennel mate, Ira. She thrives in calm and predictable environments and would do best with a patient family who can help her continue to grow.

Freya gets along well with other dogs and has a submissive nature in group settings. She enjoys canine company but is also content living as the only dog, provided her adopters give her plenty of attention and reassurance. If there is a resident dog, it might help Freya settle in faster, but it’s not a requirement for her to thrive.

Freya would be best suited to a semi-rural home with a garden where she can engage in scentwork and sniffing games, which help her relax and exercise her natural instincts. Busy households or homes with younger children might overwhelm her, so a calm home with children aged 10+ would be ideal.

True to her scent hound heritage, Freya loves to explore her surroundings, sniffing and searching for treats. She is very food motivated, which is a fantastic way to build trust with her. Freya takes treats gently and patiently waits her turn, endearing herself to everyone she meets.

Freya is healthy and ready to travel from Cyprus to her forever home. Could you be the one to give this lovely girl the peace and happiness she deserves?

name: Lykos
description:
Lykos is a 7-8 year old mixed breed who sadly came into the shelter after his elderly owner passed away and none of his family members were able to take his dog in.

It’s always particularly sad to see dogs that are accustomed to living in a home try and adjust to the shelter, as it’s so overwhelming, confusing and scary for them.

Despite his slightly older age, Lykos has slowly but surely adapted well, and now he’s ready to find a forever home for his later years.

Lykos is around eight years old and is a sweet, calm boy with a gentle nature. Right now he’s sharing his kennel with a female dog who he enjoys hanging out with.

He was previously in a kennel on his own, so we think he’s quite liked having a friend come to join him.

Although he’s a little older than most of our other shelter residents, Lykos still really likes going out for walks and is well mannered on the lead.

He loves sniffing through the long grass, peeing on anything that’s already been peed on, and he also likes to just stand and observe, taking in his surroundings.

He enjoys basking in the sun on a warm day, and he’s also quite a nosey neighbour so he loves watching what everyone else is up to.

Lykos finds the close capacity of the kennels quite confronting, and he really doesn’t like being barked at by other dogs in surrounding kennels.

He would definitely prefer a calm household with a laidback lifestyle, and we don’t think he would be too fussed either way if he was the only dog, or if he shared with a resident dog.

He can be quite independent, but if he was homed with another dog we think he’d be happiest with a female simply based on his experience in the shelter.

Being an older dog, Lykos is looking for a patient family. Just because he’s been in a home before, it doesn’t mean he will adapt seamlessly, and he still requires understanding and time to settle in properly.

A UK companion home is still very different to a Cyprus companion home, and there will be a lot of change for him to navigate.

The perfect home for Lykos would be based in a calm, residential area, or semi-rurally, with direct access to a secure garden. He would suit an older family given his calmer nature, or a younger family just wanting to give a senior dog a happy life.

He’d enjoy leisurely walks in the woods or along the beach, but he won’t be too demanding in his exercise needs. As long as he has a comfortable bed and lots of attention, we think he’ll be very happy.

name: Lucy
description:
Lucy is a 7-year-old Beagle cross, likely mixed with a smaller terrier breed. She’s on the smaller side of medium, full of personality, and ready to find the forever home she so deserves.

Rescued from the streets of Cyprus while pregnant, Lucy’s puppies were quickly adopted, but she has been waiting in kennels for over two years. Despite this, her zest for life and love for people remains unshaken.

Lucy is a people-oriented dog who adores human interaction. She’s confident, friendly with new people, and her playful, cheeky personality makes her a joy to be around.

When let out of her kennel, she sprints like a rocket but quickly circles back to you for belly rubs. Her favourite pastime is splitting her energy between zoomies and lounging in the dirt for cuddles, always with her tail wagging furiously.

Lucy is energetic but not overwhelming, and with a more active lifestyle, she’s ready to thrive. She loves being outdoors, exploring green spaces, or playing in a secure garden. Her ideal home would be outside the city, with access to safe spaces where she can run and play.

While she does need some basic training (like curbing her tendency to jump up in excitement), Lucy is eager to please and already well-behaved in many ways—she returns to her kennel without protest after playtime.

Lucy is good with other dogs but doesn’t need a resident canine companion to be happy. She’d also be a lovely fit for families with children aged 6+ due to her friendly temperament, though her playful energy might require supervision at first. An adult-only home with plenty of love, cuddles, and treats would also suit her well.

Though currently living in a kennel, Lucy’s adaptable nature means she’ll likely settle quickly into a home environment. She has a clean bill of health and is ready to travel from Cyprus to meet her new family.

name: Joey
description:
Our extra special boy Joey is approximately two years old and a very small cross breed possibly with a hint of Kokoni. Joey weighs just five kilos and measures eight inches from shoulder to paw.

Having been found at the shelter gates overnight Joey has been in our Cyprus shelter for two weeks.

Joey is a shining star. Our shelter manager describes him as loving, bubbly and with a huge personality. Certainly, Joey oozes quality and style all of which are enhanced by a natural gentility and calmness.

Joey so enjoys his time with human companions and his near perpetual smile shines through. Such a handsome little chap with the most endearing appearance.

His tri colour coat is beautifully enhanced with an array of freckles on his front legs. A perfect adoption candidate for a very fortunate family who will be blessed with years of love and affection.

Joey walks nicely on his lead and seems to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children.

name: Maverick
description:
Meet Maverick — 2 year old dachshund cross, full of life, and always ready for his next adventure.

This boy doesn’t do “boring.” He’s playful, fun, and brings that kind of energy that makes every day feel a little brighter.

Whether it’s zoomies, games, or just being your shadow, Maverick is all about living life with you.

He’s at that perfect age — full of joy, curiosity, and personality. The kind of dog that will make you laugh daily and keep you on your toes (in the best way).

Maverick is looking for someone who wants more than just a dog —he’s looking for a partner in crime, a best friend, a life shared.

Someone out there is going to be very lucky to call him theirs.

name: Porky
description:
Porky is a small to medium-sized cross-breed puppy of approximately 4 months old.

Porky is currently living in a cage-free foster home in Bulgaria and will be ready to travel to the UK via DEFRA-approved transport.

Porky is one of Mums Duchess’s 7 beautiful puppies and has inherited both her lovely temperament and gentle nature. Raised in a home environment from a young age, he has grown into a happy, confident and affectionate young boy.

Porky is a friendly puppy who enjoys people, loves attention and is always ready to join in whatever is going on around him. He has a cheerful outlook on life and approaches each day with enthusiasm and a wagging tail.

He mixes beautifully with other dogs and enjoys spending time playing with his brothers, sisters and the older dogs in foster care. Porky is also comfortable around cats and has been socialised with a variety of animals and everyday household life.

As a young puppy, he is curious, intelligent and eager to learn. He enjoys exploring, playing and then finding a comfortable spot for a well-earned nap.

Porky would suit many different types of homes, whether that’s an active family, a couple, older adopters or someone looking for a loyal companion. He has the kind of adaptable and loving personality that makes him an excellent family pet.

Being raised in a cage-free home environment has given Porky an excellent start in life. He is familiar with household routines, people, dogs and daily activities, helping him prepare for life with his future family.

He will travel with vaccinations, microchip, blood tests and a pet passport, together with ongoing rescue back-up for life.

Porky is a handsome, affectionate and well-rounded puppy who is ready to begin the next chapter of his life with a family of his own.

name: Charlie
description:
Our extra special boy Charlie is approximately five years old and a really small but delightful cross breed. Charlie weighs six and a half kilos and measures seven inches from shoulder to paw.

Having been found abandoned on the streets Charlie has been in our Cyprus shelter for only two weeks.

Our shelter manager describes Charlie as the sweetest, calmest little boy with exceptional manners.

Charlie’s appearance is sublime. He has a coat encompassing snowy white with several shades of tan along with a lovely curly tail.

Charlie has the most endearing facial expressions and seems to be very knowing. His puppy dog eyes and happy smile just win hearts wherever he goes.

Having clearly been preloved for his whole life Charlie is the perfect readymade adoption candidate who will bring joy and happiness to his very fortunate adopters.

A fabulous little chap with so much to offer.

Charlie walks nicely on his lead and seems to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children.

name: Betty
description:
Betty is a small cross-breed puppy of approximately 7 months old.

Betty is currently living in a cage-free foster home in Bulgaria and will be ready to travel to the UK via DEFRA-approved transport.

Betty was rescued as a young puppy and has blossomed into a happy, affectionate little girl who is now ready to find a family of her own.

Expected to remain a small dog when fully grown, Betty is the perfect size for those looking for a companion who can easily fit into family life, whether that’s countryside walks, days out, holidays or simply curling up beside you on the sofa.

Betty has a sweet and loving personality. She enjoys human company, loves affection and is always happy to be involved in whatever is happening around her.

She is playful without being overly demanding and has a gentle nature that makes her a pleasure to be around.

She mixes beautifully with other dogs and enjoys playing with her canine friends. Betty is also comfortable around cats and has been raised in a home environment alongside a variety of animals.

As a young puppy, Betty is curious, intelligent and eager to learn. She is growing up surrounded by everyday household life, helping her develop into a confident and well-socialised young dog.

Betty would make a wonderful addition to many different types of homes. She would be equally happy with a family, couple, older adopter or someone looking for a loyal companion to share life’s adventures.

She will travel with vaccinations, microchip, blood tests and a pet passport, together with ongoing rescue back-up for life.

Betty has all the qualities people look for in a family dog — affectionate, friendly, intelligent and small enough to accompany you almost anywhere.

name: Margot
description:
Margot is an 18-month-old female Collie Cross. She is a medium-sized dog based in York and is looking for a patient and understanding home.

Margot came into rescue in Romania with her mother and siblings when she was just a tiny puppy after spending time in a shelter.

She has been in her foster home for one month and is gradually settling into her new surroundings. Margot is a shy and gentle girl who is still building her confidence.

She would benefit greatly from living with other dogs, as their presence helps her feel more secure and encourages her confidence to grow.

Margot enjoys the company of other dogs and is suitable to live in a home with canine companions. She can also live with dog-savvy cats that understand how to interact respectfully with dogs.

Her calm and gentle nature means she has the potential to fit well into the right household environment.

She can live with older children who will be patient and considerate of her shy personality. A quieter home that allows her to settle at her own pace would help her continue making progress. Her future family should be prepared to give her time to adjust and develop trust.

Margot is mostly clean in the home, although she may occasionally have the odd accident as she continues to settle and learn. She is currently undergoing lead training and is making progress with this important skill.

She can also be left alone when accompanied by another dog, which provides her with reassurance and companionship.

With continued support, understanding, and the company of other dogs, Margot has every opportunity to flourish.

She is a sweet-natured young dog who is looking for a loving home where she can continue to gain confidence and enjoy life as part of a family.

name: Peggy
description:
Peggy is a 9-year-old female Deerhound Cross Wolfhound. She is a large breed dog who is looking for a calm and settled home where she can enjoy a quieter pace of life.

Peggy previously lived in a rural home and was accustomed to country walks and regular outings to the local coffee shop and pub.

She has experience living in a multi-dog household and has shown that she can live alongside other dogs.

Despite this, her ideal home would be one where she can be the only dog, allowing her to enjoy a peaceful environment and receive all the attention she deserves.

A home with a retired person or someone who works from home would suit her particularly well.

Peggy is not a demanding dog and is happy with a balanced lifestyle that includes a pleasant walk followed by a comfortable snooze.

She appears to enjoy a relaxed routine and would thrive with owners who appreciate a gentle companion. Her laid-back nature makes her well suited to a quieter household.

She has good manners in the home and is clean indoors. Peggy is also lead trained, making walks straightforward and enjoyable. In addition, she can be left alone when needed, which provides flexibility for her future owners.

Peggy has been waiting for a home for five months and is ready to find a family that can offer her the calm and comfortable lifestyle she enjoys.

She would benefit from a home without children and cannot live with cats. With the right home environment, Peggy has all the qualities needed to be a loyal and easy-going companion.

name: Indie
description:
Indie is an 18 month old female small Cross-Breed.

This gorgeous girl is an absolute joy – calm, gentle and full of love. She is affectionate and has a very sweet nature. She can be a little shy in new situations and meeting new people but a good natured resident dog will make all the difference.

She travels well in the car especially when she realises there is a good walk in the offing. She loves to smell all the different things around her from wildflowers to rabbit poo!

She really loves going to the beach where she can roll in the sand and have a really good run around.

Indie walks beautifully on the lead and her recall has come on well. Indie loves playing with other dogs and having a friendly dog to snuggle up with.

She is an absolutely super little girl and all she wants now is a loving home of her own with a resident dog to be her best friend.

Originally from Romania, indie was rescued with her siblings from a hoarding situation there.

Please get in touch if you can give Indie the forever home she needs and deserves.

name: Fry
description:
Fry is a small crossbreed aged around five years old.

Fry initially came into our care several months ago from the Botosani public shelter in Romania. He was a submissive little chap who had quite a hard time in there.

He was quite quickly adopted and for a while things went well. Unfortunately as he found his feet he became possessive over beds etc and has recently been returned for this behaviour.

Since his return Fry has been learning that possessive behaviour isn’t accepted here and with firm rules and boundaries and confident handling the issue is manageable.

He will however need an experienced adopter who will not be phased by “stroppy terrier” behaviour.

Fry gets on well with other dogs but would be quite happy as the only dog in the home. He wouldn’t want to live with any dog that was too boisterous or in his face.

He ignores the safe rescue cat and will need an adult home environment. Fry enjoys his walks and is good on and off lead. He is an affectionate little boy who enjoys cuddles and fuss.

When you adopt a Safe Rescue dog, you MUST use a slip lead. This will keep your dog safe: your new dog will be nervous and will not trust you, and you will not know which situations might upset your dog.

If your dog panics, then a slip lead is the only way to prevent your dog from escaping (many dogs can escape from a collar and/or harness). It will take AT LEAST 3-6 months for your dog to settle-in and for you to know your dog fully (longer for nervous dogs).

The slip lead must ALWAYS be used during this settling-in period. Even after your dog is settled, it is safest to use the slip lead in situations where your dog may become scared (e.g. visiting new places, around unfamiliar people, at the vet), and it situations where unexpected triggers might happen (e.g. around bonfire night).

Nervous dogs may always need to wear a slip-lead as a back-up safety measure.

The slip lead is a safety device and must NEVER be used as a training tool. Using the lead to apply pressure to the dog’s neck is damaging. If your dog pulls on the lead, then we can advise you on training methods that avoid harm.

Once your dog is settled, you may want to consider using a harness (together with the slip lead) if your dog is comfortable with being handled when it is fitted. Most harnesses are not escape-proof, but harnesses with a strap behind the ribcage (e.g. Ruffwear Webmaster or Perfect Fit Harnesses) are safer.

Retractable / extendable leads must never be used on our dogs.

Adopted dogs must be collected from the rescue and transported straight home in a crate.

Fences and gates must be 5foot minimum in height and secure.

name: Conga
description:
Conga is a medium sized crossbreed aged around four years old. Conga fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Previously to arriving in the UK recently, Conga has spent the whole of his life in the Botosani public shelter in Romania.

Astonishingly he has retained a happy disposition and love of humans despite the awful conditions there.

Conga is a loyal and loving boy who will follow you to the ends of the earth once you show him kindness. He will need some basic training to stop him jumping up to gain attention which we have started here.

He gets on well with other dogs and would benefit from another dog in his forever home but we don’t feel it’s essential. He could live with respectful children aged twelve plus.

When you adopt a Safe Rescue dog, you MUST use a slip lead. This will keep your dog safe: your new dog will be nervous and will not trust you, and you will not know which situations might upset your dog.

name: Juno
description:
Juno is an adorable three-month-old Staffie Cross puppy with a beautiful lilac coat and a personality that is impossible not to fall in love with.

Since arriving into foster care, Juno has charmed everyone she meets with her affectionate, friendly, and social nature. She is a happy little puppy who enjoys human company and loves being involved in everything that’s going on around her.

Juno has shown excellent manners with other dogs and enjoys their company. She would happily live with another friendly resident dog who can help continue teaching her about the world, but she would also thrive as the centre of attention in a loving family home.

She has also been assessed around a dog-savvy cat and has shown a gentle and respectful attitude, meaning she could potentially live successfully in a cat-friendly home.

As you would expect from a puppy of her age, Juno is still learning all the important life skills she will need as she grows. She is intelligent, eager to engage with her people, and responds well to positive interaction and encouragement.

Her new family should be prepared to continue her training, socialisation, and development as she matures.

Juno has settled exceptionally well into foster life and is already crate trained. She sleeps happily in her crate and can currently be left for up to four hours without issue.

Like all puppies, she can occasionally be mischievous, but this is simply part of normal puppy development and is easily managed with supervision, routine, and appropriate enrichment.

As she is still awaiting completion of her vaccinations, Juno has not yet started lead walking. Once she is ready to explore the outside world, she will benefit from reward-based training to help build her confidence and develop good lead manners.

One thing potential adopters should be aware of is that Juno may have a hearing impairment. She appears able to hear certain tones, pitches, and volumes but does not respond consistently to all sounds.

This does not impact her loving personality, ability to learn, or quality of life. Many dogs with partial hearing loss live happy and fulfilling lives using hand signals, visual cues, and consistent routines.

Juno is currently fed Beta kibble and is thriving on her diet.

Juno is looking for a committed family who can provide the love, patience, guidance, and training every young puppy needs. In return, she promises a lifetime of affection, companionship, and plenty of puppy cuddles.

For anyone looking to welcome a sweet, affectionate, and incredibly special puppy into their lives, Juno could be the perfect addition to the family.

name: Sienna
description:
Sienna is a medium sized crossbreed aged around seven years old. Sienna fostered Norwich Norfolk.

This sweet little girl has had a torturous existence in the Botosani public shelter in Romania for around a year, we don’t know how long she suffered prior to this.

Poor Sienna has had chronic and painful ear issues that could not be treated at the shelter.

The constant discomfort must have been intolerable for her and the constant and vigorous shaking of her head has left her with permanent cauliflower ears were the blood vessels in them burst.

She is now in the UK and at last being treated for her condition.

Sienna is a kind a gentle girl who can’t quite believe how her luck has changed. She arrived very skinny and matted and it’s been quite a task to get her into a tidy state.

Despite her the sufferance of her past, Sienna is a happy and attentive girl who enjoys a fuss and a cuddle. She walks beautifully on a lead and is a pleasure to take out and about.

She can live with or without other dogs in her forever home and would be ok to live with respectful children aged ten plus.

When you adopt a Safe Rescue dog, you MUST use a slip lead. This will keep your dog safe: your new dog will be nervous and will not trust you, and you will not know which situations might upset your dog.

If your dog panics, then a slip lead is the only way to prevent your dog from escaping (many dogs can escape from a collar and/or harness). It will take AT LEAST 3-6 months for your dog to settle-in and for you to know your dog fully (longer for nervous dogs).

The slip lead must ALWAYS be used during this settling-in period. Even after your dog is settled, it is safest to use the slip lead in situations where your dog may become scared (e.g. visiting new places, around unfamiliar people, at the vet), and it situations where unexpected triggers might happen (e.g. around bonfire night).

Nervous dogs may always need to wear a slip-lead as a back-up safety measure.

The slip lead is a safety device and must NEVER be used as a training tool. Using the lead to apply pressure to the dog’s neck is damaging. If your dog pulls on the lead, then we can advise you on training methods that avoid harm.

Once your dog is settled, you may want to consider using a harness (together with the slip lead) if your dog is comfortable with being handled when it is fitted. Most harnesses are not escape-proof, but harnesses with a strap behind the ribcage (e.g. Ruffwear Webmaster or Perfect Fit Harnesses) are safer.

Retractable / extendable leads must never be used on our dogs.

Adopted dogs must be collected from the rescue and transported straight home in a crate.

Fences and gates must be 5foot minimum in height and secure.

name: Nico
description:
Nico is a handsome red Staffie Cross of around 10 months old who is looking for a loving forever home. Despite a difficult start in life, he has blossomed into a gentle, affectionate, and adaptable young dog who has so much love to give.

One of Nico’s greatest strengths is his wonderful social nature. He is dog friendly and has integrated extremely well with other dogs in foster care.

He is also cat friendly and has shown a calm and respectful attitude towards feline companions. Having lived alongside a seven-year-old child, Nico has demonstrated a gentle and affectionate nature that makes him suitable for a family home.

In the home, Nico is crate trained and approximately 95% toilet trained. He settles well indoors and is not destructive, displaying only the occasional mischievous puppy behaviour expected from a youngster his age.

When left alone, he may initially cry for a few minutes but quickly settles and has comfortably been left for up to four hours.

Nico is still building confidence when out and about. He can be nervous on walks and sometimes finds the outside world a little overwhelming, but he is making excellent progress with patient guidance and encouragement.

As his confidence grows, he is becoming increasingly comfortable exploring new environments.

He is also discovering the joys of toys and playtime and now becomes wonderfully excited when it is time for games and interaction with his people.

Nico is believed to have a hearing impairment and appears to be hard of hearing rather than completely deaf. This does not impact his loving personality or ability to enjoy life.

He takes confidence from the dogs around him and copes incredibly well with daily life. If adopted as an only dog, his new family should be prepared to continue building his confidence and communication skills using positive reinforcement and consistent routines.

Nico is currently fed a raw diet and is thriving on it.

Nico is a truly special young dog with a gentle soul and a bright future ahead of him. He would suit a variety of homes and will reward his new family with endless loyalty, affection, and companionship.

For someone willing to continue his journey and help him grow in confidence, Nico will make an exceptional family companion and best friend.

Adoption Fee: £400Location: Worcestershire

name: Nora
description:
Nora is a beautiful lilac and white Staffie Cross puppy who is looking for her forever home.

At just three months old, she is already showing all the qualities that make Staffies such wonderful family companions – affectionate, sociable, playful, and full of love.

Nora has settled exceptionally well into foster care and has adapted beautifully to family life. She is a happy, confident little puppy who enjoys spending time with people and making friends wherever she goes.

She is currently living alongside other dogs and has shown herself to be very dog friendly. Nora enjoys playing, learning from canine companions, and would happily live with another friendly resident dog.

Equally, she would thrive as the centre of attention in a loving home.

Nora is also living successfully with cats and has shown a calm and respectful attitude towards them, making her suitable for a home with cat-friendly felines.

She has spent time around children aged seven years and has been wonderful with them, showing a gentle and affectionate nature. As with all puppies, interactions should always be supervised while she continues to learn appropriate manners.

Nora is crate trained and settles well in her crate. She can currently be left for up to four hours, although as a young puppy, toilet training remains a work in progress and her new family should be prepared to continue this.

Lead walking is still very new to Nora. She currently finds the outside world a little overwhelming at times, but her confidence is growing every day.

She takes reassurance from other dogs and is gradually learning that new experiences can be fun and rewarding.

Nora has shown no signs of reactive or aggressive behaviour and has adapted wonderfully to living with dogs, cats, and children. Her friendly, easy-going temperament means she could fit into a variety of different homes.

She is currently fed a raw diet and is thriving on it.

Nora is looking for a family who can continue her training, socialisation, and development while providing the love, patience, and guidance every puppy needs. In return, she promises a lifetime of companionship, affection, and plenty of puppy cuddles.

Nora is a truly lovely little girl with a bright future ahead of her and will make a fantastic addition to a lucky family.

name: Jill
description:
Jill is a larger-breed cross-breed puppy of approximately 4 months old.

Jill is currently living in a small cage-free foster home in Bulgaria and will be ready to travel to the UK via DEFRA-approved transport.

Jill and her brother Jack were abandoned during the night outside the rescue as young puppies. Thankfully, they were found safe and have since grown into happy, healthy youngsters who are now ready to find loving homes of their own.

Jill is a gentle soul with a wonderfully kind nature. She is a quiet, thoughtful puppy who enjoys observing the world around her before joining in.

While she enjoys playtime with the other dogs, she is not boisterous or demanding and has a naturally calm temperament.

Jill mixes beautifully with other dogs and would be equally happy living with canine companions or as an only dog receiving plenty of love and attention.

She is also comfortable around cats and has been raised in a home environment alongside a variety of animals.

We expect Jill to mature into a larger-sized adult, but she has a gentle and sensible nature that will make her an excellent family dog. She would suit families with respectful children, couples, or individuals looking for a calm and affectionate companion.

Being raised in a home environment has given Jill an excellent start in life. She is familiar with everyday household routines, people, dogs and normal daily activities.

She will travel with vaccinations, microchip, blood tests and a DEFRA registered pet passport, together with ongoing rescue back-up for life.

Jill has all the qualities that make a wonderful family companion — kind, loving, gentle and loyal. She is simply waiting for a family to call her own.

name: Robbie
description:
Robbie is a small cross-breed puppy of approximately 8 months old as of June 26.

Robbie is currently living in a cage-free foster home in Bulgaria and is expected to remain a small dog when fully grown.

Like many of our rescue puppies, Robbie had an uncertain start in life, but he has blossomed into a happy, affectionate and well-socialised young boy who is now ready to find a family of his own.

Robbie has a wonderful temperament. He is friendly, loving and enjoys human company.

He likes to be involved in family life and is always happy to join in whatever is going on, whether that’s playtime in the garden, a walk, or simply relaxing with his people.

He mixes beautifully with other dogs and enjoys making new canine friends. He is also comfortable around cats and has been raised alongside a variety of animals in his foster environment.

As a young puppy, Robbie is curious, intelligent and eager to learn. He is being raised in a home environment where he is exposed to everyday household sounds, routines and experiences, giving him an excellent foundation for life in his future home.

Robbie would suit a wide variety of households, including families, couples, older adopters and homes with existing pets.

Most importantly, he is looking for a family who will continue his training, provide plenty of love and include him as part of the family.

He will travel on DEFRA registered transport to you with vaccinations, microchip, blood tests and a pet passport, together with ongoing rescue back-up for life.

Robbie is a sweet little boy with a bright future ahead of him and is sure to make a wonderful companion for a lucky family.

name: Sky
description:
Sky is a larger-breed cross-breed puppy of approximately 4 months old as of June 2026

Sky is currently living in a cage-free foster home in Bulgaria and is ready to travel to the UK via DEFRA-approved transport.

Sky was found abandoned in a cardboard box outside a Lidl supermarket as a tiny puppy. Thankfully, he was rescued and has never looked back. Despite his difficult start in life, he has grown into a wonderfully friendly, loving and confident young boy.

Sky is expected to be a larger dog when fully grown and already has the long legs and frame to suggest he will mature into a handsome big lad. However, his size is matched by an exceptionally gentle temperament.

He absolutely loves people and enjoys being involved in everything that is going on around him. He is affectionate, intelligent and eager to please, making him a wonderful companion for an active family.

Sky mixes beautifully with other dogs and enjoys playing with puppies and older dogs alike.

Due to his expected adult size, we feel Sky would be best suited to a home with older children who are confident around larger dogs. He would thrive with a family who enjoy walks, outdoor adventures and having a loyal companion by their side.

Sky is being raised in a home environment, giving him an excellent start in learning everyday routines, social skills and household life.

He will travel with vaccinations, microchip, blood tests and a pet passport, together with ongoing rescue back-up for life.

Sky is a special boy with a huge heart and an even bigger future ahead of him. Whoever adopts him will gain a loyal, gentle and loving companion for many years to come.

name: Chase
description:
Chase is a medium-sized cross-breed of approximately 9 months old.

Chase is currently living in a cage-free foster home in Bulgaria and is ready to travel to the UK via DEFRA-approved transport.

Chase was rescued as a young puppy and has grown into a gentle, sweet-natured boy. He has a naturally shy personality and can be cautious when meeting new people or experiencing new situations, but there is absolutely no malice in him.

He simply needs a little patience and understanding while he learns to trust.

He is a calm, non-reactive dog who prefers a quieter lifestyle and would thrive in a home where he can settle at his own pace. Chase is not demanding and is happy to relax nearby once he feels comfortable and secure.

One of Chase’s favourite things is the company of other dogs. He mixes beautifully with dogs of all sizes and gains confidence from having canine companions around him.

He is particularly gentle with smaller dogs and puppies and would happily live with another friendly resident dog.

Chase could also live as an only dog, provided his new family understands that he may need a little extra time and reassurance while adjusting to his new surroundings.

Due to his sensitive nature, we feel Chase would be happiest in a home with older teenage children or adults only. A busy household with young children would likely overwhelm him.

He is fully vaccinated, microchipped, castrated, has completed blood tests and holds a pet passport ready for travel.

Chase is looking for a kind, patient family who can see beyond his initial shyness and allow his lovely personality to shine through. For someone willing to give him that opportunity, he will make a loyal and devoted companion.

name: Lilly
description:
Lilly is a medium sized collie cross aged around six years old. She originally came from Botosani Public shelter in Romania – and has surprisingly been with us in foster for over a year now, patiently waiting her turn. Lilly fostered Milton Keynes.

She is a very calm, obedient and quiet girl who doesn’t ask for much. Doesn’t even jump on the furniture!

Loves to run and play with the resident dogs and would love to have a dog companion in her forever home.

She’s still a bit shy around new people but her confidence is growing each day.

What Lilly likes:country walks;rolling on the grass;treats in a kong toy;gentle strokes;calm environment.

What Lilly doesn’t like:traffic;loud or sudden noise;

Lilly pays no attention to the cats in her foster home. Whenever they approach she shows no interest.

name: Freya
description:
Freya is a large crossbreed female aged around ten years old. Freya fostered Kings Lynn Norfolk.

She was found in bushes in Crivatu Romania a few years ago and was taken to the private shelter nearby.

She is a calm and steady girl who still enjoys short walks. She mixes well with all dogs but is quite a dominant member of the pack. She keeps all the youngsters at the shelter in check with a firm but fair approach and they all love and respect her for that. She could also live happily as the only dog in the home.

Freya is an affectionate and loyal companion who would suit someone with large breed experience who can pamper her in her twilight years. She would be ok with respectful children aged twelve plus.

name: Chico
description:
Chico is a 10 year old Jack Russell Terrier cross. Chico fostered Watton, Norfolk.

He was adopted from Safe Rescue around 4 years ago but has been recently returned to the charity through unforeseen reasons.

We were told by his adopters that he was nervous around new people but he settled with his fosterers on the day they brought him home, climbing on to their laps for cuddles and strokes.

He is currently a little reactive to the other dogs in his foster home but we think this is down to a lack of socialisation and are working on improving this. However, we do think he would be better off as an only dog as that is what he is used to.

For a 10 year old, he is quite playful and enjoys running around and having fun with his toys and his playful moments are mixed in with plenty of naps.

He is both house trained and lead trained, making for an easy addition to new home.

He will need adopters with terrier experience.

name: Tanner
description:
Tanner is a medium to large sized German shepherd cross aged around six to seven years old. Tanner fostered Easton Norfolk.

He has come into our care recently from the Botosani public shelter in Romania.

We’ve known this boy for over two years now and walked him on several trips to the shelter. He’s always been so happy to see us and a really nice boy to take out. He’s very friendly and confident.

Tanner could live very happily with another dog or dogs but would also enjoy being the only dog in the home. He could live with respectful children aged ten plus.

name: Freya
description:
Our extra special girl Freya is approximately four years old and a medium sized cross breed with something of a hound appearance.

Freya weighs fourteen kilos and measures twelve inches from shoulder to paw. Having been found abandoned on the streets Freya has been in our Cyprus shelter for six weeks.

Our shelter manager describes Freya as gentle and loving. She has a calm natural kindness about her and a most emphatic nature. Her demeanour is so appealing and generates warm feelings which radiate to those around her.

Freya’s beautiful appearance reflects this wonderful girl. Her soft brown coat is perfectly enhanced with a white flash to her chest. Freya’s eyes are of film star quality, and her ears are absolute showstoppers.

Freya is the perfect adoption candidate for a quiet and loving family who will be paid back with huge premiums. She is so deserving.

Whilst enjoying her time in the car, Freya also walks nicely on her lead. She also seems to mix perfectly well with other dogs, cats and children.

name: Dolly
description:
Dolly is a beautiful large breed cross of just over four years old who is searching for a home where she can truly thrive.

Sadly, Dolly is returning to rescue due to a mismatch in energy levels within her previous home. This isn’t because she is a difficult dog or has done anything wrong.

Instead, she needs a calmer environment with clear boundaries, consistency, and owners who understand how to help her feel secure and relaxed.

While she can initially be a little nervous around strangers, a handful of treats and a little patience are usually all it takes before her soft, loving side appears.

Dolly adores human company and is a real cuddle lover. One of her favourite places to be is curled up next to her person, soaking up affection and enjoying life’s simple comforts.

She enjoys her walks and loves exploring new scents and environments. She is very food motivated, which makes her highly trainable and eager to learn. Around the home, Dolly is generally calm and relaxed, happy to settle down and enjoy family life.

Dolly has lived around children aged 5 and 9 years old and has shown herself to be a loving companion. Any children in the home should be dog-savvy and able to respect her space and boundaries.

While Dolly enjoys social interactions with dogs out and about, she would likely be happiest as the only dog in the home where she can fully relax and receive the attention she deserves.

For experienced owners, she could potentially live alongside a calm, balanced dog following careful introductions and ongoing management.

She walks well overall, although she can pull on the lead at times. A head collar has helped improve this significantly. She travels well in the car and enjoys getting out and about with her people.

Dolly is not currently crate trained, although crate training may help build her confidence and provide her with a safe space of her own.

She can show some anxiety when left alone, likely because she has become accustomed to having canine company around her. She has occasionally been lightly destructive, such as chewing slippers, but nothing excessive.

She has also shown some reactivity in situations involving high excitement levels and around food with other dogs.

These behaviours are manageable with structure, boundaries, and appropriate handling, and are another reason why a calmer home environment would suit her best.

Dolly is a bright, affectionate, and intelligent girl with so much love to give. Beneath everything is a dog who simply wants stability, understanding, and a family who will help her be the best version of herself.

For the right home, Dolly will be a fiercely loyal companion, an affectionate cuddle partner, and a devoted best friend.

name: Nelson
description:
Nelson is a handsome 10-month-old Staffie Cross with a huge heart and an even bigger capacity for love. Still very much a youngster, he combines the fun and enthusiasm of a puppy with the calm, affectionate nature of a dog far beyond his years.

Since arriving into foster care, Nelson has quickly become a firm favourite thanks to his loving personality, excellent manners, and easy-going nature. He is the type of dog who simply enjoys being around people and being included in everyday life.

Nelson is a friendly and affectionate boy who loves spending time with his humans, whether that’s joining you on an adventure, relaxing at home, or enjoying a cuddle on the sofa.

He is equally happy playing with other dogs, exploring on walks, or settling down for one of his favourite activities — an afternoon nap.

He is very dog-friendly and has shown lovely social skills with the dogs he has met in foster care. Nelson enjoys canine company and could happily live with another friendly dog.

However, he would also be perfectly content as the only dog in the home, where he can soak up all the attention for himself.

Nelson has met children aged between 6 and 10 years old and has been gentle, calm, and happy to interact with them. He would be well suited to a family home with sensible, dog-savvy children who understand how to respectfully interact with dogs.

Although generally a confident young dog, Nelson can sometimes feel a little unsure in unfamiliar places.

When faced with something new, he may seek reassurance from his handler, but with encouragement and support his confidence continues to grow every day.

He is not particularly fond of heavy rain or loud noises, but copes well when guided by someone he trusts.

Nelson already has some lovely training foundations in place. He knows basic commands including sit and down, walks beautifully on the lead, and has excellent manners around the home.

He is non-destructive, settles well indoors, and can currently be left alone for around three hours without issue.

He is currently thriving on a Paleo Ridge raw diet and enjoys his meals immensely.

While Nelson is dog-friendly and small animal friendly, he is looking for a cat-free home. Careful introductions have been attempted with a cat during foster care, but his body language suggests he would be likely to chase, making a home with cats unsuitable.

Nelson is searching for a loving forever family who can continue helping him build confidence while giving him all the love and companionship he deserves.

Whether joining a home with another friendly dog or becoming someone’s one-and-only best friend, Nelson has all the makings of a truly wonderful companion.

Young, affectionate, well-mannered, and eager to please, Nelson is a special boy who will reward his future family with endless loyalty, love, and companionship.

name: Rona
description:
Rona needs a patient home that will give her that time to trust and learn that not all people are bad. She warms up quite quickly if she feels safe and secure, she will be an excellent companion in the right home.

At just one year old, beautiful Rona is struggling with life in a busy Cyprus shelter. The noise, the constant movement, and the long days of waiting are overwhelming for a dog as gentle and sensitive as she is.

She tends to stay quiet in her kennel, watching everything around her, unsure how to cope with the chaos.

Rona doesn’t thrive behind kennel doors — she’s the kind of dog who needs the calm and reassurance of a real home, where she can finally feel safe, loved, and understood.

She can be reserved until she has had time to trust. When she has learnt to trust then she is a loving girl that will just curl up on your lap for a cuddle.

Please don’t overlook her. Many quiet, sensitive dogs like Rona are passed by, and extended time in a shelter can be hard on them. She deserves a home, a family, and the warmth that comes with being cared for.

She can be adopted anywhere in the UK or Cyprus — her forever home could be with a family (children 12+) a couple, or a single adopter.

She walks well on the lead (once she has learnt to trust) and travels calmly, making her a lovely companion for days out and new adventures.

She is good with other dogs and shares her space with both males and females, large and small. She has not been cat tested but this can be arranged.

Rona will be neutered, fully vaccinated (including Rabies), microchipped, blood tested and have her own passport. Full rescue back-up with Saving Souls Animal Rescue – a UK based charity.

name: Duncan
description:
Duncan is a medium to large crossbreed. His passport states him to be six years old but we think he is probably older, possibly eight or nine. Duncan fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Duncan was rescued from a kill shelter in Romania. He has been in a private shelter since then but we are now delighted to have welcomed him to the UK.

Duncan is a sleepy boy who just loves the fact that he now has comfortable beds to sleep on and will quite happily snooze away his hours in the blissful knowledge that he is now safe from harm.

He is mixing with other dogs in his foster home but in all honesty would prefer it if they weren’t there. He could live with another dog with a similar temperament that didn’t pester him.

He’s happy to have a brief hello and a tail wag and then both parties go about their business. He wasn’t at all phased by the safe rescue cat.

Duncan would like a calm and quiet retirement home to live out his senior years in peace. He can manage short walks for a change of scenery but doesn’t want to venture far because he has beds that need to be slept on at home now!

He would prefer an adult home but would be ok with visiting children as long as they respected his need for peace and quiet and snoozes. He is a friendly and affectionate boy with his humans in between naps

When you adopt a Safe Rescue dog, you MUST use a slip lead. This will keep your dog safe: your new dog will be nervous and will not trust you, and you will not know which situations might upset your dog.

If your dog panics, then a slip lead is the only way to prevent your dog from escaping (many dogs can escape from a collar and/or harness). It will take AT LEAST 3-6 months for your dog to settle-in and for you to know your dog fully (longer for nervous dogs).

The slip lead must ALWAYS be used during this settling-in period. Even after your dog is settled, it is safest to use the slip lead in situations where your dog may become scared (e.g. visiting new places, around unfamiliar people, at the vet), and it situations where unexpected triggers might happen (e.g. around bonfire night).

Nervous dogs may always need to wear a slip-lead as a back-up safety measure.

The slip lead is a safety device and must NEVER be used as a training tool. Using the lead to apply pressure to the dog’s neck is damaging. If your dog pulls on the lead, then we can advise you on training methods that avoid harm.

Once your dog is settled, you may want to consider using a harness (together with the slip lead) if your dog is comfortable with being handled when it is fitted.

Most harnesses are not escape-proof, but harnesses with a strap behind the ribcage (e.g. Ruffwear Webmaster or Perfect Fit Harnesses) are safer.

Retractable / extendable leads must never be used on our dogs.

Adopted dogs must be collected from the rescue and transported straight home in a crate.

Fences and gates must be 5foot minimum in height and secure.

name: Lyric
description:
Lyric is a medium sized crossbreed aged around four years old. Lyric fostered Norwich Norfolk.

We discovered this absolute beauty queen in the Botosani public shelter when we visited in March. It’s taken a few weeks for her turn to come to arrive in the UK, she’s here now and ready for a lucky adopter to come and snap her up!

Lyric is a sweet and gentle girl with a cheeky side that’s just beginning to emerge. After an initial short spell of hand shyness she soon learns to trust and is an affectionate and attentive companion.

She can live with or without another dog in her forever home and would be suitable to live with respectful children aged ten plus.

name: Gabriel
description:
Gabriel is a medium sized crossbreed aged around eight years old. Gabriel fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Wow, I can’t believe I’m typing this boy an album, I don’t think anyone ever thought that I would….

Since the very building of the shelter, Gabriel was the heart and soul of Crivatu, he was moved there before the shelter was even finished being built and we have known and loved him since meeting him after lock down on our first visit there.

He was an integral part of my family summer holiday there a couple of years ago and a favourite with my adult children. He is special, I can’t tell you why he is but he just is and he is family to us all.

Every time we left at the end of a trip and he had to go back to spending his nights in a pen it hurt us and he cried for three days. He is perhaps not the easiest dog and he needs someone with a good level of experience.

He can be the only dog in the home or he can share his home with another non too boisterous dog. He can object when told to get off furniture, but he is very manageable to anyone who has dealt with these issues before.

He is a pleasure to walk, never reacted when the street dogs followed us down the street yapping at him, but he doesn’t like large vans or Lorries passing him. He is not good with cats.

This boy is just something special and anyone adopting him will have the most loyal friend. He lives for his walks and is so grateful for all time put into him.

name: Megan
description:
Megan is a medium sized crossbreed aged around six to seven years old. Megan fostered Norwich Norfolk.

If ever a dog deserves a change of luck and oodles of love it’s this one. She was found limping and starving by the side of a road in Romania.

Her hip was broken and she had a bullet in her leg where she’d also been shot. She was rescued by a friend of ours and taken to a vet.

Her hip was broken and she had surgery to fix this, the shot gun pellet was left as it was decided it wasn’t causing harm where it is. As if that wasn’t enough, her blood tests showed that she also had heartworm. She was treated for this too.

You could argue her luck began to change when she was found by someone who would actually help her and save her….. but the change isn’t complete until we find her forever home and family.

Megan is now in the UK and is an absolute delight. Goodness only knows why she still loves people after all she’s been through but she really does.

She is friendly, affectionate, gentle and just a pure joy. She gets on really well with other dogs but could also be the only dog in the home. She could live with respectful children aged ten plus.

Following her rescue, she tested positive for heartworm. She has since completed successful treatment and is doing well.

As a precaution, she will continue on a routine monthly anti-parasitic tablet, which will be provided and sent with her to her adoptive home. There will be no cost to adopters for this ongoing care.

name: Lol
description:
Lol is a medium sized crossbreed aged around five years old. Lol fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Lol has joined us recently from the Botosani public shelter in Romania. The first thing you need to know about him as that he ‘grins’.

At first you have to look twice as it can be mistaken for a snarl but he just grins every time you speak to him. This, combined with his under shot jaw, makes him a uniquely cute chap that we just had to name Lol because he appears to be laughing.

Somewhere along the line he has also lost most or all of the sight in one eye.

Lol is the most cuddly and affectionate boy. He can be submissive at first and shows signs of an abusive past as he will cower at sudden moves and sounds. He gets on very nicely with the other dogs in his foster home but could also live as the only dog.

He would like a quietish and calm household with no children under the age of twelve.

name: Phillip
description:
Phillip is a medium sized crossbreed plonked on a small dogs legs! He is around eight years old and fostered Norwich Norfolk.

This is a very special little man who comes with his own fan club/following. He has lived at the Crivatu shelter in Romania for the last few years and been a big part of all of our trips there.

He has been very happy there living freely and doing as he pleases. A combination of a change of ownership at the shelter, and our boy being older now led us to decide that it was his turn to come to the UK now and find his own living home.

Phillip has adapted very well to domestic life and really enjoys being around humans a lot more. He has been fabulous with the other dogs in his foster home but is also connected enough to humans to live as the only dog.

He is initially a little hesitant with strangers but once his trust is earned you wouldn’t find a more loyal friend and companion.

Phillip enjoys his walks but is still a little cautious of having his lead put on as he’s run freely for so long. This is improving daily as he’s learning that leads mean being taken to interesting places.

He has become a little rotund at the shelter so his fitness level will need to be increased in the coming weeks.

Phillip would like an adult home environment with no cats.

name: Badger
description:
Badger is a medium sized crossbreed aged around two years old.Badger fostered Badersfield Norfolk.

Badger has come into our care recently from the Botosani public shelter in Romania. He arrived there last month when we were on a visit, brought in by the dog catchers.

Badger is a brilliant character who gets on with everyone and everything. He is friendly and affectionate and would fit easily into most homes.

He can live with or without other dogs and is fine with cats.

He could live with respectful children of any age.

name: Lizzie
description:
Lizzie is a medium sized crossbreed aged around two to three years old. Lizzie fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Lizzie was one of multiple dogs seized by the police in Romania from a hoarder who kept about ninety dogs in a caravan and a car. She has been waiting in a private shelter since she was a pup for an offer of adoption to no avail.

Lizzie arrived in our care a few months ago and was adopted shortly after. Sadly she was returned to our care a few weeks ago.

She’d been doing so well there and they loved her but unfortunately got protective of her female owner against visitors to the home.

We feel that she now needs a home with another dog in the hope that she doesn’t feel solely responsible to keep her family safe.

We have waited a few weeks to repost her for adoption to reassess her. She has been an absolute joy back in her foster pack. She is friendly and playful with everyone and loves to play with the other dogs and is an absolutely perfect house guest.

She will need an adult only environment with experiences adopters.

name: Laura
description:
Laura is a medium sized collie type crossbreed aged around five years old. Laura fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Laura has come into our care recently from the Botosani public shelter in Romania. Despite us visiting the shelter three or four times a year we hadn’t noticed her in the two years she’d been incarcerated there.

Getting noticed in amongst six to eight hundred dogs is certainly a challenge! In our trip in March this year she found the courage to push herself forward and we found her to be a real hidden gem.

Laura is coming on leaps and bounds since arriving in her UK foster home. Lead training was quite scary for her but as you will see from the pics she soon overcame her fear and is now loving being able to run freely for the first time in so long.

Laura can live with other dogs but could also be the only dog in her forever home.

We anticipate she will be shy when arriving at her new home but once given time her lucky new family will see the sweet and affectionate girl that we have been honoured with getting to know.

Laura will need an experienced owner due to her shyness with strangers. She will need a calm and adult only environment.

name: Grace
description:
Grace is a small crossbreed (possibly pug chihuahua in the mix) aged around four years old. Grace fostered Poringland Norfolk

Grace has come into our care recently from the Botosani public shelter in Romania. She was terrified in the shelter environment and, although still very timid here, she is gradually progressing in confidence.

She will need the company of another dog or dogs in her forever home. Despite being scared she has never so much as snapped at any of us whilst handling her.

She is a delicate little soul living in a world of giants that she is only now starting to understand.

Grace is not a quick fix and will not be a friendly little lap dog overnight. She needs an adopter that understands that trust takes time to earn when you have only ever been let down and failed by humans.

It’s a long road ahead but an incredibly rewarding and worthwhile one. She is tense to the touch and she flinches at sudden movements, there’s a lot of emotional wounds to heal that only time, patience and love can patch up.

Grace will need a quiet home environment with no children. She can live with cats and must have at least one dog in full time residence.

name: Nancy
description:
Nancy is a medium to large sized crossbreed aged around two years old. Nancy fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Nancy was found on the streets as a puppy and has grown up in a shelter until she travelled to us in the UK recently. She is an affectionate and friendly girl who loves to be around people.

She is still finding her feet on walks but is gaining confidence with every venture out. Nancy gets on well with other dogs but could also live as the only dog in the home. She could live with respectful children aged seven plus.

name: Rosie
description:
Rosie is a medium sized crossbreed. Her passport states her to be aged around eleven years old but we think she could be a bit younger. Rosie fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Rosie was one of a pack of four dogs who were rounded up from a factory premises in Botosani, where they’d been living for years, by the dog catchers.

They were taken into the public shelter and two of the four were dead in less than a week . There was no question that we had to step up for the two survivors.

Rosie acts like she’s lived the life of a princess her whole life, despite what we know to the contrary. It did not take her more than an hour to locate the sofa and decide that that’s where she belongs now.

Her stumpy tail wags furiously at all times. She’s a sweet natured girl who loves people, her submissive approach shows that people have not always been kind to her but she wants to be friends with everyone anyway.

Rosie would like to be the only pet in the home but is happy to mix with other dogs out on walks. She enjoys short walks and still has a cheeky little playful streak at times, mostly she likes cuddles and snoozing though.

She could live with respectful children aged eight plus.

name: Boris
description:
Boris is a large crossbreed aged around ten to eleven years old. Boris fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Boris has come into our care recently after spending many years in a private shelter in Romania. This big bear of a dog has spent much of his life confined in a pen. To watch him experience the new freedoms of a foster home is really very special.

Boris is a dear old chap who loves every body he meets. He is getting on well with the other dogs in his foster home but would be very happy to live as the only dog in the home. He will need a cat free home but loves children of all ages.

name: Barbie
description:
Barbie is a small female crossbreed aged around 2-3 years old.

Barbie came into our care from the Botosani public shelter in Romania at the end of 2023 and has grown up with us. Having started life in the shelter environment she was very shy and under socialised on arrival and it took us weeks to warm her trust.

She is now confident and affectionate with everyone in her foster home and loves cuddles and to play. She is still wary of strangers and not confident in unfamiliar surroundings but this will all improve in time.

She needs to move on from her multi dog foster home now and get used to life out of the shadows of a large pack.

Barbie gets on well with other dogs and would need the company of another confident dog in her forever home. She is a strong member of the pack here and often the instigator of the fun and games but she will initially come into any new home as submissive and shy.

Barbie could live with respectful children aged twelve years and will need a fairly experienced adopter.

name: Alba
description:
Alba is a small to medium sized crossbreed. Her passport states her to be five years old but we feel she is probably at least seven or eight. Alba fostered Hempnall, Norfolk.

Alba was found in a cemetery in Romania and was rescued a few weeks ago. She is now in a UK foster home.

Her fosterer says, “She’s a lovely girl who likes to rest outside, she’s great with other dogs but would also be happy to be on her own.

She’s no trouble, you literally don’t know you have her. She loves walks where she likes to plod beside you. She has the happiest tail waggle and sweet soul who just wants tummy tickles and love.

Shes great with children and all people. Shes house trained and cat tested. Alba would be the bestest friend to anyone.”

Alba can live with cats and would like a quietish household. She can live with respectful children aged eight plus.

name: Jellybean
description:
Jellybean is a medium sized crossbreed aged around four years old. Jellybean fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Jellybean has spent all of her life in the Botosani public shelter until she came to the UK to a foster home a few weeks ago.

Due to the limitations of shelter life poor Jellybean has had to get used to pretty much everything. She has done so well in the short time she’s been here so we are confident that over the coming months she will continue to shine.

She is cautious of new people and a bit bemused about human affection generally as she’s never experienced it, but it’s dawning on her more and more that it’s a nice thing and not so worrying after all.

She will need another dog in her forever home to learn from and for reassurance as she encounters more new life experiences.

Jellybean is not bothered by the safe rescue cat. She will need a calm home environment with no children under the age of twelve. She walks beautifully on lead but is still a little wary of the big wide world.

name: Ebony
description:
Ebony is a medium to large crossbreed who is coming up to a year old. Ebony fostered Norwich Norfolk.

Ebony came into our care recently from Romania. A rescuer had been keeping some dogs on her grandmothers land but when asked to move them she was facing putting them in a public shelter.

She successfully found homes for all except Ebony so we agreed to take her in.

Ebony is a calm and quiet girl who has adapted easily into her foster home. She is mixing well with the other dogs in her foster home and would enjoy company but could also be the only dog in the home.

She lives very nicely with cats. Ebony could live with respectful children aged ten plus. She walks beautifully on a lead and is a pleasure to take out.

name: Looby
description:
Looby is a small to medium whippet -ish crossbreed aged around three to four years old. Looby-Lou fostered Norwich Norfolk.

A few years ago a mum and two pups turned up at the gate of Crivatu. Just some of many dumped there over the years. They were tempted through the gates and into the safety of the shelter with trails of food.

They were wary little dogs that took a while to trust humans. The mother, Maria, and sister Bella, were more confidant and adopted in the UK some time ago.

Looby was a lot shyer until very recently. Now is her turn to at last come to the UK and find a new home.

Looby is loving being in a home environment. She’s still becoming accustomed to some things that other dogs take for granted but her tail is wagging with delight most of the time!

She is a sweet and affectionate girl once given time to get to know you and will continue to flourish in the weeks ahead.

She can live with or without another dog, she is very sweet with the safe rescue cat and will wag her tail and gently nose bump him. She’s an absolute delight and just needs to be given a little time to trust new people.

She will need a calm home environment with no children under the age of twelve.

name: Trinity
description:
Trinity is a medium sized female cross breed aged around 2/3 years old. Trinity is fostered in Norwich Norfolk

I met Trinity during a trip to a Romanian shelter back in October last year. She was living outside on the street along with a few other dogs and was pregnant, not for the first time sadly.

When the rescuer captured her inside the gates of the shelter she asked if we would take her and of course we agreed.

Unfortunately the neighbourhood were not kind to the dogs in that street and would throw rocks at them to chase them away as they didn’t want them around.

As a result of this Trinity is extremely wary of men and will bark at them. In time she can learn to trust individual men but we sadly cannot erase these memories altogether.

Ideally an all female home would be best but a very patient and understanding man could win her trust over time.

It is only barking, it’s like she’s trying to warn the others that there’s a man around and they should be scared, the others ignore her and carry on with their day lol.

Trinity is currently living with many other dogs and for the most part is ok. She can be a little jealous over food and fuss though so maybe a home as the only dog would be easier.

She would need a home with no children under the age of twelve. Trinity walks nicely on a lead and is loving her new domestic life.

name: Figgy
description:
Figgy is a petite little Italian greyhound/ whippety shaped crossbreed aged around three years old. Figgy fostered North Walsham Norfolk.

Figgy and his family went to Roxanas shelter in Romania from the Ukraine after the war broke out. They grew up there and only now have the chance of a better future.

Figgy is the sweetest little dog but he lacks confidence with new people and new situations. Once given time to get to know you he is a happy and affectionate little boy.

He mixes great with other dogs but would be equally as happy as the only dog in the home. He could live with respectful children aged twelve plus.

When you adopt a Safe Rescue dog, you MUST use a slip lead. This will keep your dog safe: your new dog will be nervous and will not trust you, and you will not know which situations might upset your dog. If your dog panics, then a slip lead is the only way to prevent your dog from escaping (many dogs can escape from a collar and/or harness).

It will take AT LEAST 3-6 months for your dog to settle-in and for you to know your dog fully (longer for nervous dogs). The slip lead must ALWAYS be used during this settling-in period.

Even after your dog is settled, it is safest to use the slip lead in situations where your dog may become scared (e.g. visiting new places, around unfamiliar people, at the vet), and in situations where unexpected triggers might happen (e.g. around bonfire night). Nervous dogs may always need to wear a slip-lead as a back-up safety measure.

The slip lead is a safety device and must NEVER be used as a training tool. Using the lead to apply pressure to the dog’s neck is damaging. If your dog pulls on the lead, then we can advise you on training methods that avoid harm.

Once your dog is settled, you may want to consider using a harness (together with the slip lead) if your dog is comfortable with being handled when it is fitted. Most harnesses are not escape-proof, but harnesses with a strap behind the ribcage (e.g. Ruffwear Webmaster or Perfect Fit Harnesses) are safer.

Retractable / extendable leads must never be used on our dogs.

Adopted dogs must be collected from the rescue and transported straight home in a crate.

Fences and gates must be 5foot minimum in height and secure.

name: Lindsay
description:
Lindsay is a 1 year old pup who we think will grow to be medium sized but parentage is unknown.

Lindsay has come into our care recently from the Botosani public shelter in Romania.

She is a gentle girl who’s had a difficult start in life, but she’s shown such courage and strength along the way.

After recovering from a broken leg, she’s now doing really well and is expected to make a full recovery.

Though a little shy when meeting new people, Lindsay’s affectionate and playful side soon shines through once she feels safe.

She would be happiest as the only dog in her new home, but this isn’t essential and could live with children aged 12+.

Lindsay has been patiently waiting for her chance, and now she’s ready to find her forever home.

name: Solomon
description:
Solomon is a smallish whippet/sheltie type crossbreed aged around five years old.

Solomon was brought to the UK from Romania as a puppy by a private adopter who then couldn’t cope with him so we were asked to step in. He was rehabilitated and adopted back in 2020 but has recently been returned to our care due to a change in domestic circumstances.

Solomon is quite whippety/sheltie like in behaviour as well as looks. He is a bit of a worrier and tense around new people but very loving to his family. In a pack he is very playful but a little bossy with smaller dogs so would like a home with a dog either his own size or larger. He will need an adult only family. Solomon loves his walks and getting out and about but will need a confident and experienced adopter.
